title: Tokyo “Grand van Gogh Exhibition: Terrace of a Café at Night” Admission E-Ticket
overview:
Van Gogh’s masterpiece Terrace of a Café at Night (Place du Forum) comes to Japan




Vincent van Gogh (1853–1890) — a painter beloved around the world. Although his career as an artist lasted only about 10 years, the many paintings and letters he left behind reveal a man who faced a life full of struggles and transformed them into art.
highlights: View Van Gogh’s masterpiece *Café Terrace at Night (Forum Square)*!
The exhibition focuses on the first half of Van Gogh’s life.
Purchase with your Rakuten account to earn and use Rakuten Points!
important_info:
• Tickets sold on this site are valid only from 2026-05-29 to 2026-06-30. Please note that they cannot be used for visits from 2026-07-01 onward.
• After purchasing a ticket on this site, we recommend making a free date-and-time reservation through the official online ticket system (etix). Without a reservation, you may be required to wait before entering.
Official website:
English: https://www.e-tix.jp/vangogh_tokyo/en
Japanese: https://www.e-tix.jp/vangogh_tokyo/
• Please present both your purchased e-ticket and your time-slot reservation (free) QR ticket upon arrival.
• Up to 10 tickets can be purchased per reservation.
• Visitors with a disability certificate and one accompanying caregiver receive a 50% discount. Please purchase these tickets at the venue ticket counter or through the official online ticket site (etix).
• Students and visitors with various certificates must present valid proof upon entry.
• Cancellations, ticket type changes, date/time changes, refunds, and reissues are not possible after purchase.
• Ticket sales methods and entry procedures may change. Please check the Tokyo exhibition’s official website for the latest information.
what_to_bring:
• Your e-ticket on a mobile device with internet connection (to redeem tickets, you must log in with the account used to book)
• Time-Specific Reservation QR Ticket (free)
After purchasing your e-ticket, please make a reservation for a specific date and time on the official online ticketing site. Japanese site: https://www.e-tix.jp/vangogh_tokyo/
meeting_point: [The Ueno Royal Museum] 1-2 Ueno Park, Taito Ward, Tokyo
schedule: Tickets purchased on this website are valid from May 29 to June 30, 2026. They cannot be used after July 1.
cancellation: Non-refundable after you booked
description:
One of the first people to recognize the value of Van Gogh (who received little recognition during his lifetime) and begin collecting his works was Helene Kröller-Müller (1869–1939), founder of the Kröller-Müller Museum in the Netherlands. The “Great Van Gogh Exhibition,” held in two phases, is composed entirely of works from this museum’s collection.




The first phase of the exhibition focuses on the early half of Van Gogh’s life. It begins with his early Dutch period influenced by the Barbizon School and the Hague School, continues through his time in Paris where he interacted with Impressionist painters, and culminates in Arles in southern France where he created the masterpiece Terrace of a Café at Night (Place du Forum).




The second phase, scheduled for 2027–2028, will feature The Langlois Bridge at Arles returning to Japan for the first time in approximately 70 years. The exhibition will tour Kobe, Fukushima, and Tokyo. The year 2025 marked 30 years since the Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake, and 2026 marks 15 years since the Great East Japan Earthquake. We sincerely hope that encountering Van Gogh’s works — often described as a “search of the soul” — will inspire visitors and serve as a source of strength for the future.
access:
The Ueno Royal Museum
1-2 Ueno Park, Taito Ward, Tokyo 110-0007




3-minute walk from JR Ueno Station (Park Exit)
5-minute walk from the Tokyo Metro or Keisei Electric Railway Ueno Station
how_it_works:
• Using tickets: your e-ticket voucher requires an internet connection to use, and should only be redeemed by staff (click here for how to use).
• Tickets can only be redeemed with the user account that booked them; please ensure you can access the same account on the day you visit.

title: Yokohama Belgian Beer Weekend 2026 [ 5/21 (Thu) - 5/24 (Sun) ] [E-Ticket]
overview:
【BBW2026 Yokohama】
Enjoy Belgian Beer Weekend Yokohama at Yamashita Park, right on the waterfront. Belgian beers, gourmet food, live music, and specialty booths come together in a refreshing seaside setting — a unique beer festival by the bay.




【The Belgian Beer Weekend】
The event made its debut in Japan in 2010 as a three-day festival welcoming 15,000 visitors. Today, the Belgian Beer Weekend is held in major metropolitan areas across Japan and has grown into one of the country’s leading outdoor events, attracting more than 1.8 million visitors over the past 16 years.




Yamashita Park
5/21 (Thu) - 5/24 (Sun)
Thursday 16:00-21:00
Friday & Saturday 11:00-21:00
Sunday 11:00-20:00
(last order 30min before closing time)

Authentic Belgian Gourmet
Live Music

title: Hida Great Limestone Cave & Ohashi Collection Museum Advance Tickets [100 JPY Discount VS Same-Day Tickets Onsite]
overview:
The Hida Great Limestone Cave lies between Hida Takayama and Okuhida-Onsengo in Gifu Prefecture. Totaling about 800 meters in length, these limestone caves simply abound with sights to see and marvel at.




At an altitude of 900 meters, it is the highest tourist limestone cave in Japan. Temperatures in the cave are stable year-round, averaging a cool 12°C, making it a great retreat from the summer heat!




Visitors can also enjoy the Ohashi Collection Museum, displaying some 1,000 works of art and decorative objects from around the world.




Buy tickets from Rakuten Travel Experiences to get 100 JPY off for adults (high school students and older): 1,000 JPY vs 1,100 JPY for same-day tickets sold onsite, and for children (elementary school and junior high school students): 450 JPY vs 550 JPY for same-day tickets sold onsite.
highlights: Pets are also welcome
You can visit the Ohashi Collection Museum on the same trip
Explore a mystery that Mother Nature created over ~250 million years
At an altitude of 900 meters, it is the highest tourist limestone cave in the country
Temperatures in the cave are stable year-round averaging a cool 12°C, making it a choice retreat from the summer heat!
important_info:
.
In order to preserve the natural environment there is no elevator, therefore the site is not wheelchair-accessible.
.
If you plan to drive to the site during winter snowfall, please use studless winter tires or chains.
.
Please note that opening hours vary depending on the season.
.
The Hida Great Limestone Cave is divided into three sections from Cave 1 to Cave 3.
Each section has its own exits. The sections after Exit 2 have steep pathways, so if you have any concerns about your stamina or lower-body strength, we recommend leaving the cave via Exit 1 or 2.
.
Approximate time needed from the Limestone Cave entrance to Exit 1 is 10 minutes. To Exit 2 is 20 minutes and to Exit 3 is 30 minutes. From Exit 3 to the parking lot is about an 8-minute walk (all times are rough estimates).
.

*Please make sure that your smartphone connects to the internet as you will need to launch the website from your smartphone.
*Please note that screenshots or printed-out tickets will not be acceptable.
.
Free parking available.
.
Discounts are available for the disabled. Please present your disability certificate (physical disability certificate or rehabilitation certificate) when you purchase tickets onsite. An accompanying person can receive a 100 JPY discount and pay 1,000 JPY (multiple tickets are available).

description:
Highlights of the Hida Great Limestone Cave:




• Ryuuguu No Ya Kei — Cave 1
(Nightscape of the Dragon's Palace)
This surreal scene greets you upon entering the Hida Great Limestone Cave. It is deliberately lit with red and blue lights to prevent moss from growing.




• Dream Palace — Cave 2
The icicle-like soda-straw stalactites are one of the trademark features of the Great Hida Limestone Cave and can be seen here in large quantities.




• Aishin Spot — Cave 2
An underground tunnel that connects Cave 1 to Cave 2. Colorful LED lights stage a mystical atmosphere along the way.




• Tsuki No Sekai — Cave 3
(Moon World)
Formed of cave coral, the dramatic cleft in the rock is a sight to behold! But beware — the pathway to this spot is very narrow.




Ohashi Collection Museum:




An art museum established to complement the Hida Great Limestone Cave. Around 1,000 artworks and decorative items collected from around the world are on display here. The Ohashi Collection Museum is located in front of the Limestone Cave entrance — be sure to take a look on your visit to the cave!

overview:
Umekita Onsen REN Wellbeing Park is an urban-style hot spring facility newly opened in the Umeda area of Osaka.




With various kinds of baths, saunas, and the latest health and wellness programs, it offers a relaxing space to rejuvenate both body and mind.




Designed in harmony with a lush green park, it provides a wellbeing experience where you can enjoy nature while still being in the heart of the city.

title: MIRACLEWORLD Admission Pass
overview:
Osaka’s first permanent indoor digital art facility has opened in Namba.
Step underground and immerse yourself in a world surrounded by 360° LED visuals and mirror-reflected immersive projections, where an entirely new realm unfolds before your eyes.




In the interactive zone, the visuals change in response to each visitor’s movements and interests, allowing you to fully experience a fusion of reality and creativity through interactive art that engages all your senses.




MIRACLE WORLD is a fantastical space where art, imagery, and technology come together.
An entirely new experience awaits you here, one that can only be found in this place.
Escape from the ordinary and step into a world beyond reality.
Discover an unseen world before your eyes through your own senses and imagination.

title: Dejima Admission E-ticket in Nagasaki　This is a place you definitely can't miss when sightseeing in Nagasaki.
overview: Travel back in time to Dejima, a historic island built in 1636 has served as Japan’s only window to the Western world and it has played a vital role in Japan’s modernization for 218 years during the Sakoku (national isolation) period . In the Meiji era Dejima lost the original shape of the island due to the land reclamation. In 1951 Nagasaki City began restoration work and today 16 buildings and landscapes have been faithfully restored, offering visitors an immersive glimpse into this significant era.
highlights: Book with a Rakuten account to earn and use Rakuten Points for every ticket!
important_info:
E-tickets are valid only on the selected date and cannot be changed or canceled.
Tickets are available for groups of 1–14 people. For 15 or more, group discounts are offered onsite. (※Note: until March 31, 2026)
Re-entry is not permitted.

Highlights of Restored Buildings



First Ship Captain’s Quarters
Once home to Dutch ship captains and Dutch employees, this building showcases the living spaces furnished with the beds and tables on its second floor, while the first floor replicates the warehouse.







Deputy Factor's Quarters
This building served as the home of the deputy chief factor of the Dutch Trading Post. The first floor is now a museum shop offering “Dejima goods,” while the second floor is a kimono rental shop "Dejima Hoppen."

title: Factory tour of giant robot “INGRAM” in Tokyo [PATLABOR LAB]
overview:
You can feel like you are the main character of the anime by visiting INGRAM, the 5 meters class giant robot that appears in the anime.




Step into the world of Mobile Police Patlabor at the facility in Tokyo where you can find INGRAM. Explore our human-operated robot research and development facility!

The AVX-S30 INGRAM (prototype) has been through numerous tests to collect data for its 128-bit CPU upgrade. By gathering piloting data from people all over the
world, Ingram is learning to move more smoothly and effectively!

title: Osaka Rinku Fireworks 2026 Area Admission Ticket [Saturday, June 6, 2026]
overview:
Rinku Fireworks was started in 2012 in Izumisano City as a “fireworks festival for children.” Featuring the popular Smile Fireworks and a wide variety of music and performances, it’s an event everyone from kids to adults can enjoy!




Some past events were canceled due to typhoons, lack of funding, and the COVID-19 pandemic. However, thanks to the passionate support of the local community, it made a comeback and now celebrates its milestone 10th edition!




Come and experience a special evening filled with smiles at the venue!




Event Date: Saturday, June 6, 2026
Event Hours: opens 16:00, closes 21:00
Fireworks Display: 19:30–20:10 (scheduled)
Venue: Rinku Park Marble Beach (Rinku Premium Outlets Seaside Park) at 7-1 Rinku Ourai Minami, Izumisano City, Osaka Prefecture
Note: Event will be held in case of rain; canceled in case of severe weather (no postponement)
highlights: Don’t miss “Fireworks Fantasia,” a spectacular show combining 7,000 fireworks with music!
A fireworks festival packed with music and activities that everyone from small children to adults can enjoy
Purchase with a Rakuten account to earn or use Rakuten Points
important_info:
• Child tickets for free seating apply to elementary and junior high school students. High school students and above are charged the adult rate.
• Free seating does not include chairs. Please bring a mat, cushion, or similar item.
• To prevent overcrowding and accidents due to strong winds, reserving space with self-standing equipment such as tents or sunshades is strictly prohibited.
• The use of folding chairs, large chairs, or reclining mats that may obstruct the view of others is prohibited.
• Important: Internet connectivity at the venue may be unstable. Please save or print your PDF voucher in advance before arrival.
• Unauthorized use of drones is prohibited.
• Photography using tripods is prohibited.
• Marble Beach consists of large stones. Please wear comfortable, suitable footwear.
• Heavy congestion is expected around the venue on the day. Please arrive early.
• Access by car is strictly prohibited. Please come by public transportation.
• In case of rain, please use a raincoat and refrain from opening umbrellas.
• Animals are not allowed, except for guide dogs and service animals.
• Smoking is prohibited within the venue.
• Tickets are non-refundable for any reason unless the event is canceled by the organizers.
description:
Rinku Fireworks is a fireworks event held at Marble Beach in Rinku Town, Izumisano City, Osaka. This year marks the memorable 10th anniversary edition!




Set against Osaka Bay at night, with early summer sea breezes and a sky full of stars, enjoy a spectacular fusion of light and music. This unique setting is what makes Rinku Fireworks truly special.




On June 6 this year, get together on Marble Beach! The 2026 Rinku Fireworks Festival is the perfect fireworks show for the whole family to enjoy!




At the venue, you’ll find plenty to enjoy including “KIDS JOB,” where children can try hands-on work experiences, “ENJOY! Stage” featuring local performers, and “Umaimon! Kuidaore Festa,” a food event showcasing delicious regional specialties




And nowhere else will you see so many “Smile Fireworks” lighting up the sky! Let’s all smile together!




The fully synchronized fireworks show set to music features a diverse lineup, from programs created together with local children and filled with their dreams, to spectacular professional displays sure to amaze adult audiences. The organizing committee has carefully curated a rich and dynamic program through extensive experimentation.




Not just “gentle fireworks”...
Not just “ordinary fireworks”...




Won't you visit Rinku Park Marble Beach, and bask in the magic of fireworks crafted with heart and soul?

▶ Assembly and Reception
Each booking (one group) will be accepted at the reception. Reception can be completed by one representative (adult). Please show proof of reservation. The reception time and flow for the first and second shifts differ. For details, please check the following URL: https://www.kidzania.jp/guide/flow/tokyo.html




▶ Preparation and Moving
At the reception, you will receive a JOB Schedule Card and 50 kidZos, the exclusive currency of KidZania, which are required for the experience.




▶ During Experience








Information on activity availability, meeting times, etc., is displayed on large digital signage in four locations around the venue. You can also check the information from your smartphone using the KidZania Official App. Please make full use of this.








Once you have chosen the job you want to do, go to the pavilion and use the JOB Schedule Card to register for the activity (job) and start your work.








Some pavilions offer "part-time activities" (around 15 minutes), which require a shorter experience time. Please make good use of your time when waiting, etc.








▶ End and Dismissal
Receive payment for your work. Also, any items you make yourself will be passed to you as deliverables. Please take them home as a memento of your work.

title: Limited-Time Event: Unzen Volcano Night Walk Experience [Nagasaki]
overview:
At Unzen, in Japan's longest-established national park, a magnificent story spanning 500,000 years unfolds.




This spring, an all-new digital night event debuts, blending Unzen’s stunning natural beauty with an enchanting storybook world. Experience a spectacle of light and sound created through dynamic projection mapping, and the powerful breath of the volcano rising from the Earth’s core with all five senses.




Some 500,000 years ago, “Unzen Jigoku" (Unzen Hell) was born from the eruption of Mt. Unzen. Volcanic gases — the essence of Earth's life force — continue to rise in a dynamic landscape ever-changing since time immemorial.




Enjoy an immersive night walk tracing the path of the “hells” as they have shifted over the vastness of time. Along the way, yokai (supernatural beings) of ancient legend appear as stunning digital art. Discover stories of mystical beings living alongside the volcano, and step into a fantastical world to captivate visitors of all ages.
highlights: An immersive night walk to discover legendary Unzen yokai
Experience the origins of Mt. Unzen through a storybook world brought to life with projection mapping
Step into a one-of-a-kind experience where ancient romance meets modern art in Unzen’s nighttime setting
Book with a Rakuten account to earn and redeem Rakuten Points!
important_info:

• Pets are allowed if kept on a leash.

If there are no updates on official social media on the day, the event will proceed as scheduled. In case of cancellation or changes, announcements will be made on the official website and social media at 17:00 on the day.

description:

Sometimes called “Hello Kitty Land,” Sanrio Puroland Tokyo is an indoor Hello Kitty theme park where you’ll find not only Hello Kitty, but also other popular Sanrio characters like My Melody, Cinnamoroll, and Little Twin Stars.




Step into a world of kawaii to enjoy whimsical attractions and live performances, and meet your favorite characters. Don’t forget to take lots of photos — this Tokyo Hello Kitty theme park is filled with incredibly photogenic themed areas. Even the food looks great in photos!

how_it_works:
FAQ



How much does Sanrio Puroland cost?




The Sanrio Puroland ticket price varies from day to day if you buy tickets at the park. The walk-up price for adults ranges from 3,900 JPY in the low season all the way to 5,900 during peak times (such as Golden Week or Obon). However, e-tickets from Rakuten Travel Experiences are one low flat rate-price, and can be redeemed on any day the park opens until their expiry.




What are Sanrio Puroland’s opening hours?




The Sanrio Puroland schedule varies from day to day, opening between 08:30 and 10:00, and closing at 17:00 or 18:00. You can check the hours for the current month, and today’s show schedule, on the Puroland Japanese website here.




How to get to Sanrio Puroland in Tokyo?




The nearest train station to Sanrio Puroland is Tama Center (about 5 minutes’ walk), which is on the Keio Line, Odakyu Line, and Tama Monorail. From Shinjuku Station, ride the Keio Line; it takes about 30 minutes on a limited express service. Find the park on a map here.




What are the Sanrio Puroland rides?




The actual rides at Sanrio Puroland are “Sanrio Character Boat Ride” and “~ My Melody & Kuromi ~ Mymeroad Drive.” See a full list of the park attractions on the Japanese website here, or view the park map here




Are reservation required to enter Sanrio Puroland?




As of April 1, 2024, local residents must make a reservation to enter Sanrio Puroland. For foreign visitors though, instead of a reservation you will be asked to fill out a short questionnaire at the entrance on the day of your visit.

title: Special Exhibition - Unraveling the Mysteries of Ancient Egypt from Brooklyn Museum Admission E-Ticket
overview:
A selection of masterworks from the Brooklyn Museum’s renowned collection of ancient Egyptian art comes together in this exhibition.




Through approximately 150 artifacts—including sculptures, coffins, jewelry, ceramics, pottery, papyri, and mummies of both humans and cats—the exhibition explores the lives of the people who created a highly sophisticated culture that continues to captivate our imagination.




From lesser-known facts about ancient Egypt to the latest pyramid research using cutting-edge technology, the exhibition is presented with the support of video and audio materials.




All images courtesy of the Brooklyn Museum.

description:
Take your Osaka sightseeing experience to new heights and be awed by panoramic views right across the city. The Umeda Sky Building is an imposing futuristic landmark right in the heart of downtown Osaka. It soars 173 meters above the city, and actually consists of two separate towers, joined together by a ring-like observation deck.




Also known locally as Kuchu Teien, this is the Floating Garden Observatory, but you will find no green lawns, flowers or trees here – what a Umeda Sky Building ticket offers is sublime 360-degree views across the city, from the breezy open deck.




This innovative, in-the-round vantage point means every single corner of the city can be seen, providing magnificent sweeping vistas far and wide. Beyond the modern city skyline, clear day views stretch all the way to Awaji Island, some 100 km away.




The Floating Garden offers an incredibly natural feeling, looking out over the city in the warm sunshine and breezes. As such, local couples flock here to stroll along the ‘Sky Walk’ to gaze at the sunset and leave love locks at the beautifully illuminated Lumi Deck.




Open throughout the day until late in the evening, the Umeda Sky Building offering contrasting views of the skyline as day transforms into night. It is undoubtedly one of Osaka’s main landmarks and certainly among the most distinctive. It has been a fixture on Osaka’s ever-changing skyline for over 20 years, and from a distance resembles a single, towering arch of sleek glass and steel.




To get up to the observation deck, visitors access the East Tower for a swift elevator ride up to the 35th floor. From here the ‘floating escalator’ takes you across the open air from the East to West Tower, arriving just below the observation deck. There are a casual restaurant and cafe available on the 39th floor where you can grab a bite, and gaze out over the city through the large picture windows.




The Umeda Sky Building also houses a range of other dining options. Head to the basement and walk down a specially recreated gourmet restaurant area, showcasing period architecture from the turn of the century Showa era. Choose from around 20 quaint eateries serving local delicacies such as 'okonomiyaki' savory pancakes and 'takoyaki' octopus dumplings. There is also a multi-screen cinema screening International and Japanese art-house movies.




Surrounding the tower are a number of appealing green spaces, such as the Island Garden Forest, lying directly beneath the two towers offering a serene and lush spot to relax amid trees and rock pools.




The Floating Garden Observatory and towers can be seen from virtually anywhere across greater Osaka and are in a prime location for easy public transport access. The train station is just minutes away on foot, and a number of major international hotels are situated close by.

title: 【Up to 600yenOFF!】VIE presents ZEN NIGHT WALK Kyoto Tohukuji
overview:
Premium night viewing at Tofukuji Temple, one of the great head temples of the Rinzai school. While feeling the "fresh early summer breeze," experience walking meditation through the temple grounds adorned with fresh green maples, enveloped in neuro music that harmonizes the brain.




Featuring an illuminated display of approximately 1,000 fresh green maples viewed from the iconic Tsutenkyo Bridge, a grand sea of clouds appearing in the karesansui garden designed by Mirei Shigemori, a sound corridor, and more. A sound art night event celebrating sound and silence, light and shadow.
highlights: Premium night viewing at Tofukuji Temple, one of the great head temples of the Rinzai school.

description:
①Sound Corridor "Neuro Music"




VIE's scientifically proven brain-balancing music "Neuro Music" is placed throughout the temple. Listen to the music while walking through the corridors and viewing art installations to achieve a mindful state.




②Tsutenkyo Bridge "Fresh Green Maples": Valley of Refreshing Breeze




Looking down from the iconic Tsutenkyo Bridge upon more than 1,000 fresh green maples, a vast sea of clouds and the rustling of trees beautifully expresses the movement of the wind. Enveloped in Neuro Music and the breeze, this special experience harmonizes the tranquility of early summer through sight, sound, and touch.




③Hojo Garden "Hakkai": Dance of the Wind Dragon




In the Hojo Garden surrounded by illuminated foliage, a vast sea of clouds appears and the movement of the wind is beautifully expressed. The ensemble of insect sounds and Neuro Music creates a special experience that harmonizes tranquility through sight and sound.




④Hatto: "Shakyamuni Statue & Sōryū-zu"




A night viewing experience unlike any other. The majestic form of the principal image, the Shakyamuni Statue, and the grand Sōryū-zu emerge from the darkness of night.




⑤Zendo: Sound Zazen "Neuro Music Zazen"




In Japan's oldest and largest Zen meditation hall, wear a brainwave sensor and experience an immersive meditation space where your mental state is visualized in real-time through sound and imagery. The digital patterns and brainwave sounds create a tranquil environment for deep relaxation and self-reflection.

Discover a slice of Europe in Japan at Huis Ten Bosch, a captivating theme park in Nagasaki Prefecture. Wander through the charming Dutch-inspired streets, complete with winding canals, scenic windmills and enchanting gardens that transport you straight to the heart of the Netherlands.







Unleash adventure in the dynamic Theme Park Zone, where you can enjoy attractions and entertainment. And enjoy the picturesque scenes of the Harbor Zone, strolling through a European port town in Japan!




In spring, witness the park come alive with vibrant color with 1 million tulips in bloom! Celebrate summer under a sky lit with a stunning fireworks display. Enoy the colorful fall foliage every autumn. And in winter, the park turns into a magical spectacle with the world's largest illumination display — 13 million lights cast their mesmerizing glow across the park!







Experience the exotic charm of European culture in japan. Visit Huis Ten Bosch, one of Asia's largest resort theme parks, where you can enjoy seasonal flowers blooming along Eurpean streets!

description:
The seas surrounding Okinawa are a mysterious world home to a wide variety of marine life, boasting one of the richest diversities of species in the world.
At Okinawa Churaumi Aquarium, the magnificent world of Okinawa’s fascinating marine life is faithfully recreated. In the “Coral Sea” tank, bathed in sunlight, visitors can view a large-scale coral cultivation and exhibition. The powerful giant tank “Kuroshio Sea” allows close observation of the world’s largest fish, the whale shark, as well as the southern manta ray, which was successfully bred for the first time in the world. The experience continues in the “Deep Sea” tank, which recreates the mysterious deep waters surrounding Okinawa, allowing visitors to experience the ocean of Okinawa in its entirety.
With special exhibitions held several times a year, the aquarium aims to be a place where visitors can discover something new with every visit and never lose their fascination with the sea.

overview:

Uzu no Michi: Walk & Experience the Naruto Whirlpools 45m Above Sea Level

[In-museum services]
・Elevator
Anyone can use the elevator up to the 5th floor. However, you must take the stairs to reach the 8th floor from the 5th floor, and also to descend from the 8th floor to the 1st floor. Wheelchair users and those with walking difficulties can use the elevator for all floors.
Toyotomi Ishigaki Museum:
Wheelchair users and people with walking difficulties can use the elevator.
・Strollers
As the viewing area becomes narrower on the upper floors, it can get very crowded. Therefore, we generally ask that strollers not be brought inside. Strollers can be checked in free of charge at the ticket gate entrance, so please inform the ticket gate staff if you need assistance.
・Wheelchair
Seven wheelchairs are available for rent at Osaka Castle Tower but can only be used within the building.
Two wheelchairs are available for rent at Toyotomi Ishigakikan Museum but can only be used within the building.

Our team includes multilingual staff to assist you with any questions.
At Osaka Castle Tower, we offer free rental of audio guide devices in Japanese, English, Korean, and Chinese.
Our reception is open from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM, and we kindly ask that you return any rented items by 5:30 PM. Please note that we have a limited number of rental items available.
・Photography
Osaka Castle Tower: Photography is prohibited on the 3rd and 4th floors due to the display of important cultural properties. However, you may take photographs anywhere else.
Toyotomi Ishigaki Museum: Photography is allowed for the stone walls, but please refrain from photographing video content to protect copyrights.
[The following are prohibited]
Both Osaka Castle Main Tower and Toyotomi Ishigaki Museum are museum facilities. Please refrain from actions that may harm cultural properties or disturb the experience of other visitors:
・Photographing in restricted areas (unless permitted by the museum)
・Using filming equipment that obstructs others’ viewing
・Eating or drinking
・Loud conversations
・Sitting on the floor
・Walking while using a smartphone
・Engaging in other disruptive or dangerous actions
.
Please refrain from bringing pets, except for guide dogs, service dogs, and hearing assistance dogs.

title: 100th Anniversary of the Tokyo Metropolitan Art Museum ANDREW WYETH Boundaries or Windows – Exhibition E-Ticket
overview:
Andrew Wyeth (1917–2009) was a leading figure in 20th-century American figurative painting. Rejecting major postwar movements such as Abstract Expressionism and Pop Art, he focused on depicting people and scenes from his surroundings.




His works reflect not only reality but also his inner world. Motifs like doors and windows, symbolizing boundaries, appear frequently—serving as connections or thresholds to a private realm. This exhibition explores these themes to reveal the world he portrayed.




Tokyo Metropolitan Art Museum 100th Anniversary: Andrew Wyeth Exhibition
Venue: Tokyo Metropolitan Art Museum (8-36 Ueno Park, Taito City, Tokyo)
Dates: 4/28 – 7/5, 2026
Hours: 09:30–17:30 (Fridays until 20:00) last admission 30 min. before closing
Closed: every Mon, Tue May 7 (open 5/4 and 6/29)




+++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

description:
Andrew Wyeth (1917–2009)
Andrew Wyeth was born on July 12, 1917, in Chadds Ford, Pennsylvania, as the fifth child of the renowned illustrator Newell Convers Wyeth (N.C. Wyeth). He began studying under his father from an early age and quickly gained recognition, with all works sold out at his first solo exhibition in 1937. Throughout his life, he remained distant from contemporary avant-garde movements, instead continuing to paint the familiar world around him with remarkable precision, primarily in Pennsylvania and Maine, where he spent his summers. Wyeth’s works vividly portray the landscapes of the United States, the history embedded within them, and the lives of the people who inhabit them, earning him widespread acclaim across the country. In 2007, he was awarded the National Medal of Arts by President George W. Bush. He has also enjoyed strong popularity in Japan, with numerous exhibitions held since his first retrospective in 1974. Although he passed from natural causes on January 16, 2009, he remains one of America’s most beloved painters.



Highlights



The long-awaited first retrospective in Japan since Wyeth’s passing
Since his first exhibition in Japan in 1974, which attracted 330,000 visitors in Tokyo and Kyoto, subsequent exhibitions in 1995 and 2008–2009 have solidified his popularity in Japan. This exhibition marks the first major retrospective held in the country after his death.




Theme: “Boundaries” — a journey into Andrew Wyeth’s inner world
Motifs such as windows and doors, representing “boundaries,” frequently appear in Wyeth’s work. These elements are thought to connect life and death, as well as the artist’s inner world and the external world. This exhibition reexamines his works through this central theme.




Numerous works on view in Japan for the first time
Offering a rare opportunity to rediscover Wyeth’s artistry, more than 10 works are exhibited in Japan for the first time including "Winter Fields" (1942) from Whitney Museum, "Cooling Shed" (1953) from Philadelphia Museum of Art, and "Boarding Party" (1984) from Philbrook Museum of Art.

description:
Since its establishment in 1959, the Keisei Rose Garden has won numerous awards in rose competitions around the world, including the "Award of Garden Excellence" at the World Convention of Rose Societies (2015). The Keisei Rose Garden is committed to spreading rose culture throughout the world by promoting the roses created by the garden, and also by introducing renowned roses from around the world to the Japanese domestic market.




The 30,000-square-meter rose garden boasts 10,000 blooming roses of approximately 1,600 varieties, including original creations, brightly colored roses, and fragrant roses. The roses are planted according to their lineage, and are presented with arches, screens, pergolas, poles, and other features to convey the charm of beautiful roses.




Come enjoy the colorful roses twice a year, in the spring and fall, when they are at their best!

overview:
Step into a breathtakingly beautiful, otherworldly underwater world.




Surrounded by the gently shimmering Seto Inland Sea and the Pacific Ocean, where the timeless Kuroshio Current — known as the “Mother Current” — flows, Shikoku Aquarium is filled with engaging exhibits designed for both adults and children to enjoy and learn from.

description:
Shikoku Aquarium is filled with engaging exhibits designed for both adults and children to enjoy and learn from.




One of the aquarium’s defining features is its immersive lighting design, which creates a dreamlike atmosphere that transports you beyond the everyday.




Through the dolphin pool, you can gaze out at the stunning, world-renowned island-dotted scenery of the Seto Inland Sea. Relax as you watch dolphins swim against the backdrop of a magnificent sunset sinking beyond the horizon, creating a truly unforgettable twilight scene.




Inside, the aquarium is divided into the Pacific Ocean Zone, the Seto Inland Sea Zone, and the Clear Streams & Lakeside Zone, where you can observe a wide variety of marine life native to each area. In the Water Play Zone, you can watch feeding sessions and enjoy rare interactive experiences with the animals.




Located in a setting that makes the most of the expansive beauty of the Seto Inland Sea, Shikoku Aquarium offers an open, fantastical space where you can share unforgettable moments with marine life — an inspiring experience found only here.

description:
Kobe is famous for its “Ten-Million-Dollar Night View” from the top of Mt. Rokko. But it’s not just after dark that you’ll want to visit. Kobe Nunobiki Herb Gardens sits at the top of the Shin-Kobe Ropeway cable car, and is replete with 75,000 herbs and flowers from 200 species.




Japan’s largest herb garden is a dramatic 10-minute cable car ride from Sanroku Station, during which you’ll be able to take in the sights of Japan’s famous port city. When you arrive at the top, you’ll be presented with a picturesque cafe and restaurant serving creations made with the garden’s herbs, including an original herb tea.




The garden itself is split into 12 areas, which follow a winding path down the mountain. The Rose Symphony Garden features around 60 roses, many hailing from England, in all shapes, colors, and sizes. The Fragrant Garden focuses on the best-smelling herbs, and is reminiscent of the European countryside. The Herb Museum includes meticulous labeling to teach visitors about the plants’ characteristics.




Experience a classic kitchen garden with vegetables and herbs in the Potager area, then visit the Glasshouse for a greenhouse full of fruits and spices. The Lavender Garden pays tribute to the “Queen of Herbs,” with lavender-picking available every June.




Enjoy a new sight every time you visit the Four Seasons Garden, which also features benches and hammocks for a quick break on the lawns. The Waterfall Patio mirrors the nearby Nunobiki Falls, which are visible from the Ropeway.




Next, the Oriental Garden introduces you to indigenous Japanese and Asian plants which have been used for centuries in cooking, medicine, and crafts. The Lily Garden features blooms of this famous flower, and finally the Kaze no Oka Flower Garden lets you gaze out upon Kobe from a gentle field of flowers.

description:
Kawaguchiko Music Forest Museum (formerly Kawaguchiko Music Box Forest) is located in a nature-rich area overlooking Mt. Fuji. The interior of the museum, which has been dubbed ""a little Europe,"" offers an atmosphere as if you have stepped back in time to medieval Europe, with one of the world's largest dancing organs and featuring a karakuri puppet and water fountain show.







Visitors can also enjoy a medieval European townscape, a garden that changes from season to season. Make reservations in advance at Rakuten Travel Experiences and enjoy concerts and events on the day of your visit!




The park has one of the best rose gardens in Japan, with 720 varieties of roses, a restaurant where you can enjoy lunch concerts, and a museum where valuable musical instruments are on display. One of the most popular attractions of the Kawaguchiko Music Forest Museum is one where visitors can see an automatic musical instrument that was to be installed on the Titanic itself and is one of the world's largest dance organs.

description:
Kadokawa Culture Museum (KCM), also known as Kadokawa Musashino Museum, is a cultural complex in Higashi-Tokorozawa, Saitama. This facility is not only a museum, but also a library, art gallery, theater, and event space all in one. Though it only opened in 2020, it has been gaining attention for being a space where visitors can enjoy a variety of art, from pop culture to contemporary art.




The museum was designed by world-famous architect. Its facade looks very futuristic, and inside, the charming and whimsical Bookshelf Theater awaits. This recent social-media sensation is a hall with eight-meter-high walls and surrounded by towering bookshelves. It's not just a display, though — projection mapping and other experiences and events are held here, too.




The floor map of KCM is as follows:
• 1st floor: Grand Gallery, Manga & Light Novel Library, Genyoshi Garden, Da Vinchi Store Satellite
• 2nd floor: Lobby (information and ticket counter), KadoCafe, Rock Museum Shop
• 4th floor: Edit Town — Book Street, Edit & Art Gallery, Aramata Wunderkammer Museum, Bookshelf Theater, Attic Step
• 5th Floor: Musashino Gallery, Sacula Diner

description:

Studio Tour Tokyo is a brand-new walking tour that will take visitors behind-the-scenes of the Harry Potter and Fantastic Beasts films to learn about the remarkable world of film production. Showcasing iconic sets such as the Great Hall, Diagon Alley and the Hogwarts Express on Platform 9 ¾, fans will also enjoy lots of fun activities including riding a broomstick, drinking a refreshing Butterbeer, appearing in a portrait by one of Hogwarts’ moving staircases and shopping from a huge range of products, many of which are only available at the Studio Tour. This will be the largest indoor Harry Potter attraction in the world and visitors can expect to spend around four hours exploring the only Warner Bros. Studio Tour to open in Asia. Building on the success of Warner Bros. Studio Tour London - the Making of Harry Potter which has maintained outstanding visitor ratings since opening in 2012, Warner Bros. Studio Tour Tokyo will feature exclusive sets only available in this location.

title: Tottori Flower Illumination “Tottori Flower Corridor” Admission Ticket
overview:
Experience one of Japan's largest flower parks, boasting a magnificent view that incorporates the majestic Mount Daisen – the highest peak in the Chugoku region and one of Japan's 100 Famous Mountains – as its borrowed landscape. Here, you can enjoy seasonal blossoms amidst this spectacular backdrop.




At the heart of the park stands the Flower Dome, a colossal glass greenhouse with a diameter of 50 meters and a height of 21 meters. Inside, tropical and subtropical plants, including vibrant orchids and hibiscus, provide a year-round display of brilliant color.




Furthermore, the park features a 1-kilometer-long covered observation corridor, allowing visitors to enjoy the park's views without needing an umbrella, even on rainy days. For a convenient tour, the Flower Train circles the park, stopping at key points and providing informative announcements (paid service).
highlights: Japan's largest flower park, set against majestic Mount Daisen, blooms with seasonal flowers year-round
Earn and use Rakuten Points when you shop with your Rakuten account!

Bringing in dangerous items or similar items that may cause misunderstanding among other guests is prohibited. For details, please contact the facility.
Bringing in your own lunch boxes and drinks is permitted, but please be sure to take home any trash from containers or other items related to the food and drinks you bring.
If you plan to visit the park for photography purposes (such as cosplay), please be aware that there are specific guidelines and precautions regarding such activities. Be sure to check the Tottori Hanakairo official website beforehand. If you have any questions or require use of the changing rooms, please contact the facility directly.

title: EDO WONDERLAND Nikko Edomura
overview:
Get discount tickets for Edo Wonderland Nikko Edomura with Rakuten Travel Experiences!




Step back in time and immerse yourself in the fascinating world of Japan’s Edo period at a cultural theme park like no other. This expansive park recreates the charm and vibrancy of feudal-era Japan with incredible attention to detail.




Stroll through meticulously crafted streets featuring traditional inns, bustling merchant districts, ninja villages, and stately samurai residences. Every corner of Edo Wonderland offers a glimpse into the daily life, architecture, and culture of this iconic period in Japanese history.




The park also boasts seven theaters where you can enjoy captivating performances, from ninja action shows to traditional plays. At the Exhibition Hall, uncover fascinating stories and artifacts that bring Edo culture to life. And don’t forget to tre




Travel back to the Edo-era
highlights: Watch acrobatic ninja performances, water magic shows, and an oiran parade
Stroll around old Edo streets in kimono and other period costumes
Try horseback archery, samurai and ninja training, and grilling senbei

At Nasu Animal Kingdom, which is spread over 43 hectares of lush greenery on the Nasu Highlands, you can interact with friendly animals in a peaceful environment. Cute animals will soothe you. It's a place you can take a day trip from Tokyo, and you can spend a wonderful and memorable time with your family or couple.

description:
Nasu Animal Kingdom, spread across a vast 43-hectare site in the Nasu Highlands, is home to approximately 600 animals and birds, ranging from friendly, approachable animals to adorable cats and dogs.




Unlike a typical zoo, Nasu Animal Kingdom is designed not only for viewing and observation, but also for enjoying close, hands-on interactions with the animals. Visitors can feed animals such as penguins, play with cats and dogs, and in winter, even watch capybaras relaxing in open-air hot spring baths filled with natural spring water.




In addition, a variety of performances are held daily. One highlight is the lively cat performance, where agile cats skillfully navigate obstacle courses.




The park also features restaurants and gift shops. Nasu Animal Kingdom offers fun for visitors of all ages and is an ideal destination for a day trip from Tokyo. Why not spend a relaxing day surrounded by animals and nature?

title: Enoshima Aquarium Admission E-Ticket [Rakuten Travel Experiences]
overview: Enoshima Aquarium boasts an excellent location with Sagami Bay in front, Mt. Fuji on the right, and scenic Enoshima on the left. The themes of the aquarium are Sagami Bay and the Pacific Ocean, the multitude of creatures that live there, and the interconnectedness of life. title: Cosmo Clock 21 Ferris Wheel E-Ticket
overview:
Located in the heart of Minatomirai, Cosmo Clock 21 is a 112.5-meter-tall Ferris wheel that offers a panoramic view of the Yokohama cityscape!




Certified by Guinness World Records as the world's largest Ferris wheel with a clock — book your Cosmo Clock ticket online now for a breathtaking ride and panoramic views you’ll never forget!
highlights: Enjoy panoramic views of Yokohama's Minatomirai
Experience the giant Ferris wheel certified by Guinness World Records
At night, you can enjoy the sparkling city of Yokohama and the Cosmo Clock illumination shows
important_info:
Tickets cannot be cancelled, changed, or refunded after booking.
Tickets are required for children aged 3 or older.
Children of elementary school age or younger must be accompanied by a paying passenger aged 16 older.
Discount tickets for people with disabilities cannot be booked online. Please purchase at the ticket counter.
After booking your ticket, you will receive an email with an e-ticket link to confirm your reservation.
On the day of use, please present the e-ticket in your email at the entrance of the Ferris wheel, then board the Ferris wheel.
Please note that Cosmo Clock 21 is expected to be crowded during the year-end and New Year's holidays, and there may be a queue.
The Ferris wheel reception closes 15 minutes before the Cosmo World park closes.
Please be in line by 19:45 on weekdays and by 20:00 on weekends and holidays. Note that operation may end earlier than scheduled due to congestion.
Please refrain from bringing pets to the amusement park (except guide dogs).
There is a smoking area in the Wonder Amusement Zone (Ferris wheel side). There are non-smoking areas in the Burano Street Zone and Kids Carnival Zone. Please observe physical distancing and other safety precautions at each facility.

cancellation: Non-refundable after you booked
description:
Cosmo Clock 21 is one of the world's largest Ferris wheels equipped with a clock, standing 112.5 meters high and seating up to 480 people, and is the face of Yokohama.




It is located in Yokohama Cosmo World, a multi-level urban amusement park in Minatomirai, Yokohama.




It was originally one of the exhibits at the 1989 Yokohama Expo, but after the expo was over, it was decided to keep it as well as the Giant Ferris Wheel “Cosmo Clock 21,” and in 1999 it was relocated and renovated to its current form.




Since its opening, Yokohama Cosmo World has been loved by many visitors as an amusement park in the city, along with Cosmo Clock 21.




The Ferris wheel ride takes 15 minutes and provides a magnificent 360-degree view of the city.




Especially at night, the brightly illuminated buildings look like jewels, and the illumination show of Cosmo Clock 21 that takes place every 15 minutes is also wonderful!




Additional information about Cosmo Clock 21



Total height: 112.5 meters
Diameter of Ferris wheel: 100.0 meters
Ride duration: 15 minutes
Capacity (per cabin): 8 people
Number of cabins: 60 cabins
Capacity (overall): 480 people
Illumination schedule: 6-minute illumination shows are held every 15 minutes from sunset until 00:00



Operating period: all year
Days: daily except Thursday (but may open during long holidays and golden week, etc.)
Start time: 11:00–21:00/22:00 (business days and hours are subject to change, so please be sure to check the official website before visiting)
Duration: about 15 minutes (actual ride time only)




Note: The closing time may change depending on the day, and it may open during long holidays and golden week, etc. Please be sure to check the official website for operating dates and times before your visit.

overview:
Would you like to experience Picasso’s creativity at the YOKU MOKU MUSEUM in Minami-Aoyama?
As its sixth commemorative exhibition, the museum is presenting “Devotion to Catalunya: Ceramics of Picasso, Miró, Barceló”




Through the ceramic works of Picasso, Miró, and Barceló, the exhibition highlights their deep connection to—and affection for—the land of Catalonia.

description:
Nago Pineapple Park in Nago City, Okinawa Prefecture, is a popular tourist spot where you can eat, enjoy, learn, and buy all things pineapple! Known as a pineapple paradise, Nago Pineapple Park introduces its visitors to as many as 200 varieties of sweet and juicy pineapples as well as other tropical fruits.




Feel free to tour Nago Pineapple Park in any way you like. Some visitors choose to explore the entire park on foot, while others take a slow, easy 10-minute cart tour in a self-driving cart. Those who prefer to enjoy each area at their own pace are encouraged to see it on foot.




There are various photo spots throughout the park, including in the Botanical Gardens. For families with small children, a ride on the Mega Animal Garden x PineappleSL pineapple train is recommended. This is a fun spot for both young and old.




And definitely don't forget to try the actual pineapple fruit! Be sure to check out the lineup of sweets and drinks including fresh pineapple juice, pineapple wine, pineapple tarts and parfaits, and other pineapple sweets.




The facility's restaurant, Palm Tree, also offers classic Okinawan dishes such as Okinawan soba noodles and taco rice.




Before leaving Nago Pineapple Park, don't forget to check out the souvenir shop! Unique sweets such as sponge cakes filled with pineapple pulp and 'chinsuko' (Okinawan shortbread cookies), as well as cosmetics like pineapple soap and facial wash made with pineapple charcoal are available for purchase.




For visitors to Okinawa, Nago Pineapple Park is not to be missed!

title: Kyoto "Masterpieces From Tokyo Fuji Art Museum: 400 Years of Western Paintings Admission E Ticket
overview:
Exhibition of Around 80 Masterpieces by Artists Such as Monet and Renoir
Opened in 1983 in Hachioji, Tokyo, the Tokyo Fuji Art Museum houses approximately 30,000 works, including paintings, prints, sculptures, photographs, and ceramics.




Its Western painting collection, in particular, spans from 16th-century Italian Renaissance to 20th-century modern art, boasting one of the most comprehensive collections in Japan.




This exhibition features over 80 selected masterpieces from the museum’s collection. In addition to Monet, Renoir, Van Gogh, and Chagall, works by old masters such as Tintoretto and Van Dyck are on display, allowing visitors to enjoy 400 years of Western painting history, from mythological and religious scenes to innovations in modern artistic expression.

title: Takarazuka City Tezuka Osamu Manga Museum Admission E-Ticket [Rakuten Travel Experiences]
overview:
Takarazuka City is where Osamu Tezuka, known as the "God of Manga," spent his years from age 5 to 24. At the Osamu Tezuka Manga Museum, you'll find not only his works but also a chronological exhibition of Tezuka's own history. A vast collection of materials has been gathered, allowing visitors to learn about Tezuka himself and his works. There's also a corner where you can watch animated works, a corner where you can experience animation production called "Animation Studio" (same-day registration required), and a library with approximately 2,000 books, including not only Japanese but also foreign language versions in English, Chinese, and Korean, as well as Braille books.




This ticket is valid for one month from the date of purchase.
highlights: You can learn about Osamu Tezuka's childhood, his history as a writer, and see recreations of his workspace.
The special exhibitions showcase works by Osamu Tezuka and creators influenced by him.
Read freely from 2,000 books in the Tezuka library!
This ticket includes a novelty item (clear file)!
Earn and use Rakuten Points when you purchase with your Rakuten account!

cancellation: Non-refundable after you booked
description:
Osamu Tezuka, the creative mind behind iconic works like Phoenix, Princess Knight, Astro Boy, and Black Jack, left an indelible mark on the world of manga. His philosophies, subtly woven into his stories, continue to captivate fans both in Japan and internationally, even after his passing.




Located in Takarazuka City, Hyogo Prefecture, where Tezuka spent nearly 20 years of his life from the age of 5 to 24, the Osamu Tezuka Manga Museum offers a deep dive into his universe. You can explore nearly all of his 700 works through books and animated films, all available for your enjoyment.



[Basement 1] In the Animation Workshop, you can bring your own drawings to life by creating animations on screen. The wall displays reveal how Tezuka pioneered the concept of weekly broadcast television animation. The diorama exhibit showcases Takarazuka as it was when Tezuka lived there, along with locations featured in his stories.


[1st Floor/Ground Floor] Enter through the entrance hall, inspired by Princess Knight, one of Tezuka's most beloved works. The display capsules, modeled after the life-support system in Phoenix (Future Chapter), showcase personal items and documents related to Tezuka. You can also watch videos of Tezuka himself on this floor.




[2nd Floor] Learn about Tezuka's life and works, and browse our information and animation search terminals to watch his animated films. The 2nd floor also houses the Museum Shop, where you can purchase exclusive, museum-only merchandise. The rotating special exhibition gallery, which changes three times a year, features works by Tezuka and other creators who were influenced by him.




The museum also displays valuable materials such as Tezuka's early works and concept notebooks from before he became a professional manga artist, as well as animation scripts and original manuscripts. Whether you're a long-time fan or new to Tezuka's world, there's something for everyone to enjoy!

title: Kyoto Aquarium Admission Ticket
overview:
Kyoto Aquarium is located in Umekoji Park, where forests and lawns spread out. This is a special aquarium where you can feel a deep connection with living creatures and nature. You will be amazed by the power of dolphins. You will be impressed by the personalities of penguins.




If you are interested in any living creature, please talk to the breeding staff. They will tell you the best information with their feelings for the living creatures. The exhibition of giant salamanders that have lived since ancient times, the reproduction of satoyama, etc., The coexistence of life by the blessing of water in Kyoto is also an important theme of Kyoto Aquarium.




A place where you want to stop by anytime, like an oasis in your life. You will find something new every time you come. You will feel energetic. You will feel gentle. We hope you feel that way.

overview:
From the rich collection of the Wallraf-Richartz Museum & Fondation Corboud in Cologne, Germany, this exhibition features 70 works by 42 masters associated with Impressionism, including Monet, Renoir, and Pissarro.




One of the highlights is Van Gogh’s “The Drawbridge,” a standout work within the Post-Impressionist movement.




• Exhibition period: Jul 4, 2026 to Sep 9, 2026

description:
Manet, Monet, Renoir, Cézanne, Signac, Utrillo…
From Germany: a lineage of innovation in modern painting history.




The Wallraf-Richartz Museum & Fondation Corboud in Cologne, Germany boasts one of Europe’s most significant collections in both quality and scale, comprising approximately 70,000 works spanning from the Middle Ages to Post-Impressionism. Its origins trace back to 1824, when the collection of Ferdinand Franz Wallraf, a professor and theologian at the University of Cologne, was bequeathed to the city. The museum officially opened in 1861 following a donation from businessman Johann Heinrich Richartz.




Despite suffering damage during World War II, the collection was rebuilt and expanded through the efforts of local citizens. In 2001, the museum relocated to a new building, and with the addition of Impressionist and Post-Impressionist works donated by the Fondation Corboud, the collection gained even greater depth.




All works are from the collection of the Wallraf-Richartz-Museum & Fondation Corboud, Cologne
Photos: © RBA, Cologne
This exhibition is held with the cooperation of the Wallraf-Richartz Museum & Fondation Corboud, Cologne, Federal Republic of Germany.

description:
MIKASA Historic Memorial Warship is a museum utilizing a battleship built in England in 1902, and is recognized as a component of Japan's heritage.




Originally used as the flagship of the Allied Fleet with Commander-in-Chief Heihachiro Togo aboard, the Mikasa won a great victory in the Battle of the Sea of Japan during the Russo-Japanese War in 1905, and after retiring from active service in 1923, was preserved as a memorial ship in Yokosuka in 1926. After World War II, the main guns and masts were removed by order of the occupying forces, but the ship was restored to its original appearance in 1961.




Inside the ship, visitors can enjoy learning about history through exhibits of historical memorabilia from the time of the Russo-Japanese War and VR.

title: Kanagawa Yokohama Sankeien Garden Admission E-ticket
overview:
Sankeien Garden, located in Yokohama City, is a Japanese garden designated by the national government as a place of scenic beauty, created by the industrialist and tea master Hara Sankei.




It has 17 buildings, including 10 national important cultural properties and 3 tangible cultural properties designated by the City of Yokohama, as well as three-story pagodas, tea houses, and other historical buildings collected from Kyoto, Kamakura, and other areas and skillfully arranged.




The garden is in perfect harmony with the undulating terrain of the expansive grounds.




The seasonal blooms of plum blossoms, cherry blossoms, wisteria, lotus, and autumn leaves, as well as the bright green foliage, accentuate the historical buildings, and allow visitors to enjoy the harmony of the old buildings and nature.




Sankeien Garden is also famous for its cherry blossoms in the spring and autumn leaves in the fall. visitors can view seasonal flowers throughout the year.
highlights: Enjoy seasonal flowers such as Japanese plum, cherry blossom, Iris, and lotus.
Do not miss the historically valuable buildings that were relocated from Kyoto and Kamakura.
important_info:

• Pets (except guide dogs, service dogs, and hearing dogs) are not allowed in the garden.
• Wheelchairs are available for rent (there are some steps and slopes in the garden).
• Please do not take plants, trees, or animals in the garden (insect nets, etc. are not permitted in the garden).
• No bicycles, skateboards, etc. may be ridden in or brought into the garden.
• When photographing or sketching, please be considerate of people in the vicinity.
【Food and beverages】
• No food or beverages are allowed in the Naien (Inner garden) or the former Yanohara family residence.
• Please take any food and beverages brought in from outside with you and help us keep the garden clean.
【Photo Shooting in the garden】
• Photography is allowed within the scope of personal use. However, please refrain from wearing wedding costumes or cosplay costumes, using multiple pieces of equipment, or shooting in narrow aisles or in a single location that may cause inconvenience to other visitors.
• Please refrain from using tripods, monopods, selfie sticks, etc. in the inner garden and the former Yanohara family residence.
• Drones are not permitted in the garden.
• For commercial use, only businesses that have registered in advance may use the space for a fee. Advance reservations for the date and time of shooting are also required.

description:
■Gaien (Outer Garden)
This area was opened to the public in 1906. The three-storied pagoda, a Muromachi period building moved from Tōmyō-ji Temple in Kyoto, stands as a landmark, and visitors can enjoy seasonal flowers such as Japanese apricots, cherry blossoms, Japanese honeysuckles, and lotus. The view of the three-story pagoda over the pond that opens up as soon as you step into the garden from the main gate is truly breathtaking.




■Naien (Inner garden)
The Hara family used this area as their private residence. The garden is centered around the Rinshunkaku, a row of three buildings built in the early Edo period in the shape of geese, and is a delicately designed garden composed of old buildings. Rinshunkaku, a 350-year-old villa of the Kishu Tokugawa family, is the centerpiece of the inner garden landscape.




■Sankei Memorial Hall
The museum's exhibition rooms display materials and artifacts related to Sankeien and Hara Sankei.




Visitors can also enjoy Japanese sweet dishes at three cafe restaurants in the garden.




■Business Hours: 9:00~17:00 (last admission 30 minutes before closing)
■Closed: December 26th - 31st

There are a number of attractions visitors can enjoy with their families, such as touring a LEGO® brick factory, attending a LEGO class to learn how to make special LEGO® creations, an enormous diorama of the city of Tokyo created using LEGO® bricks, and a Duplo® playground where even babies can play safely.
Come and experience a variety of games that stimulate the imagination including the LEGO® Ninjago City Adventure, where you can train to become the most powerful ninja, 4D cinema with special-effects that will make you jump out of your skin in surprise, and the shooting ride to rescue the captured princess.

An exciting new attraction "Dino Explorer" is now open! Discover a world of LEGO® dinosaur adventures!
Let your imagination run wild on Dino Island where you can build original LEGO® creations and create your own stop-motion movie clip.
Don’t forget to have your photo taken with the giant LEGO® T-Rex!

title: Kujukushima Aquarium Umi Kirara E-Tickets
overview:
There are approximately 20,000 species of fish on the earth and 3,500 species of fish in the seas of Japan.
Of these, approximately 1,000 species of fish live in the waters around Kujukushima.
The complex topography of Kujukushima has also fostered a large number of creatures, and it has been confirmed that there are many species and numbers of creatures other than fish.
Areas with such diversity as Kujukushima are becoming rare in Japan.
Umi Kirara is an aquarium that exhibits the creatures that live in the sea of Kujukushima.
highlights: Watch A program to introduce the intelligence and physical abilities of dolphins
Observe and learn about 120 species of aquatic animals, including fish, stingrays, and turtles
See ethereal jellyfish on display at the Jellyfish SymphonyDome, Western Japan’s largest jellyfish exhibit
Get an up-close look at the marine creatures that live in Kujukushima in Saikai National Park

title: Tokyo Special Exhibition "Antarctica" E-ticket at Miraikan – The National Museum of Emerging Science and Innovation
overview:
70 Years of Antarctic Research!! This summer, Antarctica comes to Tokyo!




Commemorating the 70th anniversary of Japan’s Antarctic Research Expedition, this exhibition explores 70 years of activities and challenges in Antarctica—an unknown and extreme frontier.




Visitors become members of a “Special Antarctic Research Team,” enjoying immersive visuals and interactive exhibits unique to Antarctica while experiencing the diverse missions carried out by Japan’s Antarctic research teams.




Event Period: Wed, July 1– Sun, Sep 27, 2026
Hours: 10:00–17:00 (last admission 30 minutes before closing)
Closed: July 7 (Tue), July 14 (Tue), September 1 (Tue), September 8 (Tue), September 15 (Tue) *hours and closing days are subject to change
Venue: The National Museum of Emerging Science and Innovation 1F Special Exhibition Zone, 2-3-6 Aomi, Koto Ward, Tokyo
highlights: Discover Antarctica’s role in understanding Earth’s environment and the future revealed by Japanese research teams
Earn and use Rakuten Points when you book with your Rakuten account!
Celebrating 70 years of Antarctic research! Experience Japan’s challenges and contributions to the future
important_info:
• Each ticket is valid for one person and one entry only during the event period.
• Free admission for children aged 0–2 years old.
• Admission includes access to the permanent exhibition. The Dome Theater requires a separate payment (booking required)
• Admission may be restricted or timed entry tickets distributed depending on crowd levels.
• Tickets cannot be changed, refunded, or reissued after purchase.
• Re-entry is not permitted (except for the permanent exhibition, which allows same-day re-entry).
• Visitors with a disability certificate and one accompanying person are admitted free of charge.
• Students and certificate holders may be required to present valid ID upon entry.
• Resale of tickets is prohibited. The organizer assumes no responsibility for issues arising from unauthorized purchases.
• Weekends, public holidays, and the final days of the exhibition are expected to be crowded. Visiting on weekdays or earlier in the event period is recommended. You may be required to wait during busy times.
• Exhibition details, dates, opening hours, and closing days are subject to change due to unforeseen circumstances.
• Please check the official website for the latest updates.
Inquiries: "Antarctica" Office 03-8820-4071
what_to_bring:
• Your e-ticket
• A mobile device with internet connection (to redeem tickets, you must log in with the account used to book)
meeting_point: The National Museum of Emerging Science and Innovation, 1F Special Exhibition Zone
venue_address: 2-3-6 Aomi, Koto Ward, Tokyo, Japan, Tokyo 135-0064
schedule:
Event Period: July 1–Sep 27, 2026
Hours: 10:00–17:00 (last admission 30 minutes before closing)
Closed: July 7 (Tue), July 14 (Tue), September 1 (Tue), September 8 (Tue), September 15 (Tue) *hours and closing days are subject to change
Venue: The National Museum of Emerging Science and Innovation 1F Special Exhibition Zone, 2-3-6 Aomi, Koto-ku, Tokyo
cancellation: Non-refundable after you booked
description:
The first time Japanese explorers set foot in Antarctica was in 1912 — just one year after a Norwegian expedition became the first in human history to reach the South Pole.




In 1956, the first Japanese Antarctic Research Expedition departed Tokyo’s Harumi Wharf aboard the icebreaker Soya, marking the beginning of Japan’s Antarctic challenge. In 2026, Japan celebrates the 70th anniversary of its Antarctic research efforts.



Ticket Prices



Early-Bird Tickets:
Adults (19+): 1,800 JPY
Ages 18 and under (elementary school students and above): 1,100 JPY
Preschool children (ages 3+): 700 JPY
Sales Period: Apr 15, 2026 at 10:00–Jun 30 2026 at 23:59




Same-Day Tickets:
Adults (19+): 2,000 JPY
Ages 18 and under (elementary school students and above): 1,300 JPY
Preschool children (ages 3+): 900 JPY
Sales Period: July 1, 2026 at 00:00–Sep 27, 2026 at 16:30




Highlights




Rare real specimens collected in Antarctica on display!
This exhibition features extraordinary real-world samples, including “Antarctic Ice,” a time capsule preserving records of past climate change. Visitors can also explore over 30 meteorite samples collected in Antarctica — often called a “holy ground” for meteorite discovery — including iron meteorites you can touch and hold, as well as rare specimens believed to have originated from the Moon and Mars.




Also on display is the real “Touchable Mars Meteorite (Slice)” previously exhibited at the Osaka-Kansai Expo. Experience these invaluable and authentic samples up close!




Immersive large-scale visuals of glaciers and auroras, plus interactive exhibits!
Enjoy powerful large-format visuals of glaciers and auroras, along with hands-on experiences such as a “penguin census” and a “blizzard simulation.”




Through these exhibits, visitors can explore global environmental changes and Earth’s connection to space, while discovering scientific insights and future implications derived from decades of Antarctic research data.

title: Book Blue Cave Okinawa Diving Tour at Cape Maeda in Onna Village
overview: Book a Blue Cave Okinawa diving tour and dive into the beautiful, glowing Blue Cave located at Cape Maeda (Maeda Point) in Okinawa Island's Onna Village. Led by experienced guides, this activity is suitable even for those with no diving experience or license!
highlights: Fun-filled, action-packed experience at Okinawa's famous glowing Blue Cave
Activity led by instructors with over 10 years of experience — even beginners can join
Private group diving tour perfect for groups of friends or family
Fish feed provided to feed tropical fish underwater
Leave with digital souvenirs of your experience with our GoPro photography included
important_info:
Target age: 10 to 60 years old (Those aged 0–17 must be accompanied by a guardian, or have their consent).
A minimum of 2 participants is required
A medical history form must be submitted prior to participation. You will be contacted about the form after your reservation is confirmed
You will not be allowed to board an airplane on the same day after diving, to avoid the risk of decompression sickness. Please allow a pre-flight surface interval of at least 24 hours
Diving after drinking is not allowed. Please refrain from drinking the day before to avoid a hangover
Note that there may be cases where the dive site is closed without warning right before the start of the activity. Since the dive site may open/close right before the start of your activity without prior notice, we are not able to provide you with information on its opening/closing status
The experience price includes approximately 20 photos and a 1-minute of video of the feeding. Unlimited video is available for an additional 3,000 JPY per GoPro (to be paid on-site)
About the meeting time
The parking lot at Cape Maeda is often congested on weekends and during certain peak seasons
If you are coming by car after the 10:00 flight from March to October, please arrive at least one hour before to line up to enter the parking lot (if the activity starts at 10:00, please arrive at the Cape Maeda parking lot by 09:00)
If you are arriving after the 10:00 flight from November to February, please allow a 30-minute buffer between your arrival time and activity start time
Please note that rental car navigation systems may guide you to the wrong place
If you are late, other participants may be affected. In some cases, we may not have enough time for your activity, and we may have to charge you a cancellation fee on the day of your activity. Please be sure to arrive early
About location change
Depending on the sea conditions on the day of the activity, we may change or cancel the dive sites
If swimming is prohibited in the Cape, you will not be able to go to the Blue Cave. In that case, we will guide you to the nearby Maeda Beach
Reservations are accepted on the assumption that the information in this "Location change" section has been agreed upon. If you cancel on the day of the tour due to a location change, you will be charged a cancellation fee
About weather conditions
The tour may be canceled due to inclement weather. In the event of cancellation, we will not be able to compensate for the cost of transportation to the site
The tour itinerary may change due to natural conditions such as ocean waves and wind
If the tour is to be canceled due to weather or other circumstances, the tour operator will contact you at least one day in advance

description:
The Sanyo Shinkansen is a high-speed bullet train line in West Japan that runs between Hakata in Kyushu and Shin-Osaka in Kansai. The Shinkansen network is world-famous, offering fantastic speed and superb comfort. Plus, if you ride the Kodama service No. 842 or 849, you can expect a cute and fun Hello Kitty experience!




Ideal for long-distance journeys, the Shinkansen eats up the miles for easy inter-city travel across Japan. With Osaka-to-Hakata bullet train tickets (or Hakata-to-Osaka bullet train tickets), you’ll cruise along the Sanyo Shinkansen at incredible speed on a ride like no other.




If your itinerary covers Osaka and Hakata, book now so nothing gets in the way of your perfect Japan adventure!




Book your flexible tickets now, use the QR Code* to collect them in Japan, and make a seat reservation at the station in Japan, so nothing gets in the way of your bullet train adventure!




*QR Code is a registered trademark of Denso Wave, Inc.
how_it_works:
This ticket can be used on a variety of services. See the table below for the validity, and any other tickets needed.






Purchasing a "Discounted One-way Ticket"



*Available from select overseas travel agencies.
Purchasing Exchange Order through a Travel Agency outside Japan




Step 1: Purchase an exchange order (E-TICKET) at sales locations outside Japan
• Once issued, the E-TICKET must be exchanged within 3 months




Step 2: Exchange the E-TICKET for a "Discounted One-way Ticket" at an exchange location in Japan
• E-TICKET exchange locations: Click here for details
• Receiving an E-TICKET: Click here for details
• The E-TICKET that could not been exchanged can be refunded at the overseas travel agency where the pass was originally purchased (a separate handling fee will be applied)
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is not available for purchase within Japan



Availability and Use



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is available and usable all year around



Changes



• Changing the validity period start date is allowed only once
• The ticket cannot be changed for a different ticket type; the ticket must instead be refunded, and the desired ticket purchased separately
• Changing the departure or arrival station within a section is allowed only once for unused tickets within the validity period
• Changing trains is allowed only once before the departure of the designated train, and only if tickets for the train being changed to are available within the validity period



Refunds



• Refunds are accepted only at the "Discounted One-way Ticket" exchange location.
• Click here for information on refund locations for refunds due to non-availability of services, delays, or other circumstances
• The sales price minus a handling fee will be refunded.




Handling fee:
If no reserved seating ticket has been issued: 560 YEN
If a reserved seating ticket has been issued:
• Up to 2 days before departure: 560 YEN
• Between day before departure and time of departure: 30% of original undiscounted Express ticket (based on original ticket purchase date) + 220 YEN (minimum handling fee: 560 YEN)




• A handling fee equivalent to the Limited express ticket for each train will be applied to a reserved seating ticket after departure time.
• Refunds cannot be provided if the "Discounted One-way Ticket" has been used or the validity period has ended.
• A refund of an unexchanged E-TICKET is available from the overseas travel agency where the ticket was purchased. Please note that an additional handling fee will apply.



Related information



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is only valid for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" holder
• Presentation of a passport (of the person using the "Discounted One-way Ticket") is required to exchange an E-TICKET
• No more than one E-TICKET can be exchanged per passport
• A passport is required when using a "Discounted One-way Ticket" and must be presented to the JR staff upon request
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" will not be reissued even if lost or stolen
• The conditions of use for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" are stipulated by the JR-WEST carriage conditions and the laws of Japan
• Conditions of use for an exchange order are stipulated by regulations and contracts of issuing companies
• If any discrepancies arise between the foreign language and Japanese versions of the carriage conditions, the Japanese version has precedence
• The information displayed (including prices) is current as of August 1, 2024, and is subject to change

overview:
Ryukyu Village is a theme park where you can experience the feeling of time-traveling back to the good old days of the Ryukyu Kingdom era in Okinawa!
Within the park, Ryukyu folk songs, traditional performing arts, and powerful Eisa dance shows are held daily, captivating visitors.
You can also tour sugar carts recreating the traditional scene of water buffalo pulling sugarcane for processing, as well as precious old houses designated as National Registered Tangible Cultural Properties, allowing you to experience Ryukyu's history and culture firsthand.




You can also enjoy diverse Okinawan cultural experiences such as painting shisa figurines and trying on Ryukyu costumes. With a restaurant on-site serving local specialties like Okinawan soba and taco rice, this well-equipped facility allows you to fully immerse yourself in the island's culinary culture.

overview: Nagasaki opened its port to foreigners along with Yokohama, Kobe, Hakodate, and Niigata. The Nagasaki Foreign Settlement was established there.
