description:
What is Glover Garden?
In 1858 (Ansei 5), the Edo Shogunate signed treaties of amity and commerce with five countries: America, the Netherlands, Russia, the UK, and France. Foreign merchants with dreams of a new Japan arrived. The Dutch trading post in Dejima, which had been in operation for 218 years since 1641 (Kan'ei 1), closed its doors, and Nagasaki, which opened its port to foreigners under the treaty, was born as a "foreign settlement." Western-style buildings covered with Japanese-style roof tiles stand on the hills overlooking Nagasaki Port, and foreigners from around the world live their daily lives there. Glover Garden is located here. This area is home to the homes of foreigners from the foreign settlement period, including that of the adventurous merchant Thomas Blake Glover, and Western-style buildings scattered throughout Nagasaki City. You can feel the history of Nagasaki from the end of the Edo period to the Meiji period here. Enjoy a special time surrounded by the aroma of history and culture, with stone pavements, stone steps, and a panoramic view of Nagasaki Port.

description:
“Art Fireworks” — a one-night story that weaves unforgettable emotion




What “Art Fireworks” delivers is the ultimate entertainment experience: nearly 50 minutes of nonstop fireworks choreographed to music.




Set to a lineup of beloved classics enjoyed across generations and genders, the fireworks bloom across the entire night sky to express not only the rhythm, tempo, and melody of the music, but the emotion in the lyrics and vocals. With launches calculated to 1/30 of a second, and delicate control of hang time and lingering glow, the production is truly master craftsmanship.




“Art Fireworks” also has cinematic pacing, with a beginning, development, twist, and conclusion — plus the full range of emotions. A high-quality story, precise direction, moving music, with the fireworks themselves as the “actors” come together to create a single work that stirs the hearts of all viewers.




This special night will be etched in your memory as a deeply moving experience that never fades.




Note: this website only sells the Nishishiba Area, Toshiba Area, and Lawn Free Seating Area (Upper Level) tickets.




About Lawn Free Seating Area (Upper Level):
Trees in the foreground may obstruct your view of low-flying fireworks. While you will be able to see most of the fireworks, the music may be harder to hear than from standard seats.

title: Kyoto–Kanazawa Hokuriku Shinkansen & Limited Express Train Ticket[E-Ticket]
overview:

Exclusive to those with temporary visitor status, take the Hokuriku Shinkansen for less! This one-way ticket is valid up to 90 days after booking. Ride the Shinkansen between Kanazawa and Tsuruga Station, connecting to Kyoto with the Limited Express Thunderbird!

highlights: Exchange for reserved-seat ticket at no extra charge!

Book with a Rakuten account to earn points for every ticket!
important_info:

4: Please go to green ticket-vending machines and pick up physical ticket by scanning QR Code of e-ticket and non-Japanese passport (click here to search)

Reserving a Reserved Seat Ticket
• Reserved seat tickets can be reserved at a ticket vending machine or a station ticket office.
• Click here for information on reserving reserved seat tickets from a ticket vending machine.




Eligibility:
• "Discounted One-way Ticket" user must be holding a passport issued by a foreign government（外国での永住権を持っているお客様を含め、日本国籍のお客様は使用できません）
• "Discounted One-way Ticket" user must be entering in Japan as a "短期滞在" (Temporary Visitor)"

• Individual customers are not allowed to purchase or exchange more than one of the same ticket for the same use period

description:
The Hokuriku Shinkansen is a high-speed bullet train line that serves some of Japan's beautiful, less-visited cities like Fukui, Toyama, and Kanazawa. Change trains at Tsuruga Station for the Limited Express Thunderbird, which connects the travel hubs of Osaka and Kyoto!




With a Kyoto-to-Kanazawa (or Kanazawa-to-Kyoto) Hokuriku One-Way Ticket for the Shinkansen and Thunderbird Express, you’ll cruise between cities on a ride like no other!




Book your flexible tickets now, use the QR Code* to collect them in Japan, and make a seat reservation at the station in Japan, so nothing gets in the way of your bullet train adventure!




*QR Code is a registered trademark of Denso Wave, Inc.
how_it_works:
This ticket can be used on a variety of services. See the table below for the validity, and any other tickets needed.






Purchasing a "Discounted One-way Ticket"



*Available from select overseas travel agencies.
Purchasing Exchange Order through a Travel Agency outside Japan




Step 1: Purchase an exchange order (E-TICKET) at sales locations outside Japan
• Once issued, the E-TICKET must be exchanged within 3 months




Step 2: Exchange the E-TICKET for a "Discounted One-way Ticket" at an exchange location in Japan
• E-TICKET exchange locations: Click here for details
• Receiving an E-TICKET: Click here for details
• The E-TICKET that could not been exchanged can be refunded at the overseas travel agency where the pass was originally purchased (a separate handling fee will be applied)
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is not available for purchase within Japan



Availability and Use



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is available and usable all year around



Changes



• Changing the validity period start date is allowed only once
• The ticket cannot be changed for a different ticket type; the ticket must instead be refunded, and the desired ticket purchased separately
• Changing the departure or arrival station within a section is allowed only once for unused tickets within the validity period
• Changing trains is allowed only once before the departure of the designated train, and only if tickets for the train being changed to are available within the validity period



Refunds



• Refunds are accepted only at the "Discounted One-way Ticket" exchange location.
• Click here for information on refund locations for refunds due to non-availability of services, delays, or other circumstances
• The sales price minus a handling fee will be refunded.




Handling fee:
If no reserved seating ticket has been issued: 560 YEN
If a reserved seating ticket has been issued:
• Up to 2 days before departure: 560 YEN
• Between day before departure and time of departure: 30% of original undiscounted Express ticket (based on original ticket purchase date) + 220 YEN (minimum handling fee: 560 YEN)




• A handling fee equivalent to the Limited express ticket for each train will be applied to a reserved seating ticket after departure time.
• Refunds cannot be provided if the "Discounted One-way Ticket" has been used or the validity period has ended.
• A refund of an unexchanged E-TICKET is available from the overseas travel agency where the ticket was purchased. Please note that an additional handling fee will apply.



Related information



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is only valid for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" holder
• Presentation of a passport (of the person using the "Discounted One-way Ticket") is required to exchange an E-TICKET
• No more than one E-TICKET can be exchanged per passport
• A passport is required when using a "Discounted One-way Ticket" and must be presented to the JR staff upon request
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" will not be reissued even if lost or stolen
• The conditions of use for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" are stipulated by the JR-WEST carriage conditions and the laws of Japan
• Conditions of use for an exchange order are stipulated by regulations and contracts of issuing companies
• If any discrepancies arise between the foreign language and Japanese versions of the carriage conditions, the Japanese version has precedence
• The information displayed (including prices) is current as of August 1, 2024, and is subject to change

overview: It is a popular spot in Hida Takayama that is visited by about 100,000 people a year! While walking through the streets that make you feel like you have traveled back in time to the 1940s to 1950s, you can actually see, take photos of, and play with figurines, magazines, posters, toys, candy store games, and retro Showa pachinko machines. Please bring a device that can display the “QR code” or print out the “QR code”.

・Please note that we do not offer partial exchanges of tickets. Each purchase will have a unique QR code, even if multiple tickets are bought. When you scan the QR code on the Tokyo Metro or Toei Subway, all the tickets you purchase will be exchanged and valid starting from the day of exchange.
・If the magnetic field on your Tokyo Subway Ticket becomes damaged or weakened, it may not be usable at automatic ticket gates. Please avoid placing the ticket near objects that emit strong magnetic fields (such as smartphones, home appliances, magnetic fasteners on wallets, etc.).
.
In case of bad weather or other emergencies, the facilities will be temporarily closed. Please check the website of each facility before visiting.
.
[Notes of Purchasing TOKYO DMM Tickets]
- All prices include tax.
- Please be aware that you may have to wait 30 to 90 minutes before entering.
- Purchased tickets cannot be canceled or refunded.
- If you are entering with a "junior high/high school student" ticket, present your student ID.
- It is strictly prohibited to resell tickets, and tickets purchased through resale will not be accepted. The facility will not be held responsible for any such cases.
.
[Notes for visit teamLab Planets TOKYO DMM]
Tickets and Admission
Ticket and Admission
・Please arrive at the venue entrance in time for the admission date and time stated on your ticket and line up in the admission queue.
・The time stated on the ticket does not indicate the duration of your stay inside the facility.
・If you leave the waiting line, please ensure that someone from your group remains in line. Guests visiting alone should speak with nearby staff.
・The waiting line is outdoors. Please prepare appropriately for weather conditions at your own discretion.
・Strollers must be placed in the designated area before entering.
・Children under the age of 13 are not permitted to enter alone. They must enter with a guardian aged 18 or older. Including free children, one guardian aged 18 or older is required for every three children under the age of 13.
・Some artworks may not be experienced by guests who are pregnant due to safety considerations.
・Re-entry after admission is not permitted. Refunds cannot be issued after entry.
Inside the Facility
・In Water, guests will experience the artwork barefoot. Please remove your shoes before entering the locker area.
・Some artworks involve water and may cause you to get wet. Please remove socks or stockings in the locker area.
・Some areas may involve water reaching up to knee height even for adults.
・For artworks using water, chlorine is used and adjusted to maintain appropriate safety standards.
・Guests with small children are recommended to bring clothing that can get wet or a change of clothes.
・If a guest’s clothing becomes wet, staff will guide you to change clothes. For safety reasons, guests wearing wet clothing cannot proceed to the next artwork. If you do not have a change of clothes, towels are available so you can dry your clothes before proceeding to the next artwork.
※Please note that this is not a swimming area.
・Some floors have mirrored surfaces. Guests concerned about reflections may use rental half pants or waist cloths.
・Free half pants are available upon request. Sizes range from XS to 6L. Please return them when leaving.
・Some artworks cannot be experienced while wearing sandals that do not secure the heel or high-heeled footwear. Free rental shoes are available.
・Some areas contain strong light stimulation or dark environments.
・Guests visiting with children must not let go of their children’s hands as it may be dangerous.
・Detour routes are available for guests who wish to skip certain artworks, including those with disabilities or those accompanied by small children. Please speak with nearby staff if needed.
・Guests with babies may enter while using a baby carrier.
・Diaper changing tables are available in the restrooms. If you require nursing or rest, please use the first aid room and speak with nearby staff.
・During busy periods, staff may ask guests who have stayed in one area for a long time to move on.
【Drawing on the Water Surface Created by the Dance of Koi and People – Infinity】
・For safety and hygiene reasons, guests with an inseam of less than 40 cm cannot experience this artwork. However, they may participate if carried by a guardian (18 years or older) using a baby carrier or by holding them.
【Moss Garden of Resonating Microcosms – Solidified Light Color, Dusk to Dawn】
・This artwork can be viewed in light rain. However, it may be suspended during heavy rain accompanied by strong winds such as typhoons.
Regarding Personal Belongings
・Inside the facility there are dark corridors, unstable floors, and artworks involving water. For safety and hygiene reasons, bringing personal belongings into the artwork areas is prohibited. If you must carry necessary items due to special circumstances, please consult staff.
・Free lockers are available. After passing the entrance gate, please place your belongings in the locker area.
Locker size: Width 23 cm × Depth 34 cm × Height 37 cm
・Belongings exceeding the above size cannot be stored as there is no cloakroom service.
・Large items such as suitcases exceeding the above size will be secured with a wire lock at the stroller area.
Photography
・Photography and video recording are permitted, but please be considerate and avoid disturbing other guests.
・Flash photography is not allowed.
・Monopods, tripods, selfie sticks, or any photography support equipment longer than 30 cm are not permitted to be brought in or used.
For Guests with Disabilities
・Guests arriving in wheelchairs will be required to transfer to the wheelchair provided by teamLab Planets when entering the facility. Please note that these are standard wheelchairs without fixation devices.
※The number of in-house wheelchairs is limited.
※For senior carts with handles or electric wheelchairs that cannot be controlled to a speed of 2 km/h or less, transferring to the facility’s wheelchair is mandatory for safety and accident prevention.
・Guests entering with their own wheelchairs must attach tire covers.
・For artworks with mirrored floors, guests with a total weight exceeding 250 kg may be asked to refrain from viewing. Staff will guide you and determine whether viewing is possible depending on the floor conditions.
・For safety reasons, guests using wheelchairs may be guided through a detour route or may only view certain artworks from designated viewing areas. Please refer to the “Frequently Asked Questions about Wheelchairs” for details.
・Entry with a cane or prosthetic leg is permitted. Depending on the guest’s condition and the nature of the artwork, some artworks may not be accessible. Staff will explain the viewing route and usage rules before entry according to your situation.
・Guests who require assistance for mobility should visit with a companion.
・If you are accompanied by an assistance dog, it will be kept in a cage before entry.
Please Note if You Fall Under Any of the Following
・Those who have experienced seizures or loss of consciousness due to light stimulation
・Those with extreme fear of confined spaces, darkness, or heights
・Those experiencing palpitations or shortness of breath
・Those who are pregnant or may be pregnant
・Those carrying children
・Anyone with concerns about physical fitness or health condition
Prohibited Activities
・Entry by intoxicated persons or those with inappropriate behavior
・Eating, drinking, or smoking inside the facility
・Bringing dangerous items or items deemed inappropriate by management
・Re-entry after exiting through the exit gate
・Commercial photography or interviews without prior permission
・Accompanying any animals including assistance dogs
・Accompanying animals other than assistance dogs
Other Information
・The facility assumes no responsibility for injuries, losses, damages, or other troubles that occur during use of the facility.
※For details of the venue and additional notices, please check the official website https://planets.teamlab.art/tokyo/#notice
・The facility assumes no responsibility for the loss or theft of valuables.
・Media coverage or photography may take place without prior notice. If you do not consent to being photographed, please inform staff. This applies regardless of the purpose of the footage.
・Some artworks may not be available due to unforeseen circumstances.
※Please note that refunds will not be issued to guests who are asked to leave the facility at the discretion of management, including for the reasons stated above.
.
The expiration date of the voucher:
[teamLab Planets TOKYO DMM Admission Ticket]
It is valid only on the date and time specified at the time of booking.

The paper ticket can be collected within 90 days from the date of purchase.

*If the ticket is not used by the expiration date printed on it, it will become invalid.
*If the exchanged ticket cannot be used at the automated ticket gates, please inform a station attendant. After verification, a new ticket will be issued.

title: Asano Kajiya Knife Making: Forge Your Own Samurai Knife with a Certified Swordsmith in Gifu
overview:
Book a samurai knife making experience with Rakuten Travel Experiences at the best rates online!* Make a Japanese knife at Asano Kajiya, the atelier of a genuine Japanese swordsmith. Based in Gifu Prefecture, Asano Taro is certified by the Japanese government, and one of the few artisans still practicing authentic Japanese sword forging today. Visit a Gifu sword making expert for the closest thing to forging your own knife in Japan in 1 day!




While you’re exploring Gifu Prefecture, don't miss the breathtaking ride up the Shinhotaka Ropeway!
highlights: Book online to secure one of the limited available dates at Asano Kajiya
You can bring your own materials for a more personalized experience
Learn 'kajiya,' the ancient art of the samurai blacksmith, with a certified artisan
Easily accessible at just about 30 minutes by bullet train from Kyoto
Book with a Rakuten account to earn or use Rakuten Points!
important_info:
*As of May 2025, our price of 41,000–58,000 JPY per participant and 5,000 JPY for spectators is equal or less than any other booking site. Please note direct bookings are not possible.



If you come up at a different time than you reserved, you will be charged an additional fee for the experience
We refer to the email from Rakuten to confirm your reservation time
We can accept only a limited number of people each month, so we recommend making your reservations early
The "Add-on for Spectators" option is not available for more people than will participate in the knife-making experience. (e.g., 1 person to experience, 3 visitors, etc.)
Please arrive at Gifu-Hashima Station via Tokaido Shinkansen before 08:50
You will be greeted at the North Exit by someone holding a sign reading "Make Your Own Samurai Knife"
If you cannot arrive before 8:50, please contact emergency contact number. Please arrive before 10:00 at the latest to participate from that point; after that time, it will be automatically canceled
There is always some risk of getting burnt, so please be very careful and follow all instructions; we cannot assume responsibility in case of injury
To participate during holiday seasons, a private charter booking is required: choose an option marked "Holiday Season" and the required add-on "Private Plan Upgrade"
— New Year holidays: December 28, 2024–January 3, 2025
— Golden Week holiday: April 29–May 5, 2025
We will close the workshop during August 3–24, 2025 due to overseas travel

*Please contact the host directly for any urgent matters, such as being late. (+81) 090-9944-1792 (ASANOKAJIYA)
what_to_bring:
Long-sleeved outfit
Long pants made of cotton
meeting_point: North Exit of Gifu Hashima Station on the Tokaido Shinkansen
venue_address: Gifu Hashima Station, 645-1 Fukujusho Hirakata, Hashima, Gifu Prefecture, Japan, 501-6257
schedule:
Sample Itinerary



08:50 — Pick-up at Gifu-Hashima Station*
09:00–12:00 — Forge the knife at the atelier
12:00–13:00 — Lunch
13:00–14:00 — Shape the knife at the atelier
14:00–15:00 — Complete the knife

*Please arrive at Gifu-Hashima Station by Tokaido Shinkansen before 08:50
cancellation:
No-show: You pay 100% of experience price
8 days before: You pay 100% of experience price
description:
Learning to make a knife usually takes about 10 years of practiced apprenticeship — making a katana or samurai sword takes even longer. But on this Japanese knife-making experience at Asano Kajiya you'll get a crash course in the basics in just a single day! Learn and experience the traditional craft of Japanese bladesmithing for yourself; then, when you're done, you can take your newly forged knife home with you.




You will use the same tools used for Japanese sword making to forge a knife in the morning. Take a lunch break at the smithy, then complete the activity by shaping the knife in the afternoon. Below is a sample itinerary for this experience:




08:50 — Pick-up at Gifu-Hashima Station


09:00–12:00 — Forge the knife at the atelier
12:00–13:00 — Lunch


13:00–14:00 — Shape the knife at the atelier


14:00–15:00 — Complete the knife
15:00–15:30 — Experience ends; drop-off at Gifu-Hashima Station




Your host will prepare a lunch for you. Please let us know if you have any dietary restrictions. If you would like to eat something special, you can bring it with you.




The number of lunch options purchased should be equal to or lower than the total number of participants as lunch is included in the price of the knife-making activity. Please book the lunch-only option for those who will not participate in the knife-making activity.



Transportation



Take the Shinkansen bullet train to Gifu-Hashima Station. A free pick-up service is available from Gifu-Hashima Station to transport you to the experience location. Please refrain from going direct to the venue, since we will meet all participants at the train station first.




Approximate times from major cities:



Kyoto: 30 minutes
Tokyo: 110 minutes
Nagoya: 10 minutes
Osaka: 50 minutes



If you're coming by car, exit highway at Meishin Highway Gifu-Hashima IC and drive for 3 minutes to the station. Be sure to contact us in advance if coming by car.

The traditional Yumomi technique of Kusatsu Hot Spring that cools down the hot source spring without diluting its effects. Adorable monkeys will challenge this Yumomi technique. Be happy and be impressed! What kind of story awaits? Do visit us to find out.

title: Otaru Tenguyama Ropeway E-Ticket Voucher[E-Ticket]
overview:
At 532.4 meters above sea level, Otaru Mt.Tengu is the symbol of Otaru.




At the top of the mountain, there are five observation decks, a restaurant, a tengu statue that is said to grant wishes if you stroke its nose, a slide and other playground equipment, and other attractions such as the Tengu-no-Yakata, Tenguyama Shrine, and a chipmunk park (open from May to the end of September).




The observatory overlooks the Sea of Japan and the city, and is considered one of the “Three Greatest Night Views of Hokkaido” for its beautiful nightscape that looks like a scattering of jewels, and was awarded a star in the Michelin Green Guide Japon 2009.




At TENGUU CAFE, is the closest to the sky in Otaru, you can enjoy delicious food and beverages while admiring the spectacular view.
highlights: Enjoy the spectacular view of Otaru from the Tenguyama Ropeway!
Earn points when you book with your Rakuten account!

title: Bonsai Experience and Tour of Shunkaen Bonsai Museum Tour in Tokyo
overview:
Book a bonsai experience and tour at Shunkaen Bonsai Museum in Tokyo! Enjoy a short lesson where you can try working on one of the miniature trees at the museum, then explore this hidden sanctuary with a bonsai expert as your guide. After the experience, stay as long as you like to enjoy the museum.




Visitors can also buy a Bonsai Museum E-Tickets or Experience a Tea Ceremony or Wearing Kimono at Bonsai Museum or wearing kimono at the museum!
highlights: Discover the Shunkaen Bonsai Museum, a true hidden gem of Tokyo
Price includes museum admission: stay as long as you like after the tour
Reserve with a Rakuten account to earn or use Rakuten Points for each booking

what_to_bring: Please wear clothes that you don't mind getting dirty, and bring gloves if you are concerned about getting your hands dirty

cancellation:
No-show: You pay 100% of experience price

description:
The Shunkaen Bonsai Museum opened in 2002 in Tokyo’s Edogawa Ward. Tucked away in a residential area, the museum offers a secluded atmosphere to enjoy bonsai in peace. But don’t let the name “museum” fool you — this is a place of living beauty!




In this activity, you will get to handle a bonsai yourself, and learn something about the great care and precision required to care for it.




After your bonsai experience, take a brief tour of the museum with your host, and learn about the miniature man-made forest that stands as a bubble of serenity in the frantic capital.




When the tour is done, spend as long as you wish in the musuem. Experience the splendor of these artful expressions of natural beauty and the harshness of nature hidden within it.




The beauty at Shunkaen Bonsai Museum is the fruit of Kunio Kobayashi, an award-winning bonsai master who has held many bonsai exhibitions in Japan and around the world.

overview: Located in Sakata City, Yamagata Prefecture, the Homma Museum of Art holds a wide range of exhibitions, from antiques to contemporary art and historical materials. With this ticket, you can also enter the Homma Villa Garden (Kakubu-en), a nationally designated Place of Scenic Beauty, and the villa Seienkaku. In addition to the permanent exhibition, special exhibitions are held　occasionally, and visitors can also enjoy these.
highlights: You can enter Homma Villa Garden (Kakubu-en), a Place of Scenic Beauty, and the Seienkaku villa
The art museum has been open 75 years and you can enjoy impressive exhibits ranging from calligraphy to sculptures and local materials
Special exhibitions are held here occasionally so you can enjoy various works of art!
important_info:
.
Junior high school students and younger: free admission
.
Student rates are available to those who have a High school or college student ID card. You will be required to show your student ID card when you enter the museum, so please be sure to bring it with you. If you do not bring it, you will be charged the difference at the local reception.
.
Even on days when the museum is open, there are dates when the art exhibition hall is closed. Please check with the venue before visiting.
.
You can enter the garden and Seienkaku even when the art exhibition hall is closed, but there is no refund for the difference in ticket price.
.
For this ticket, 1 ID will be issued for each booking.
.
Please click the link in the voucher sent after the purchase to open the website page, press the "Use" button, and present the screen to the staff.
*Please make sure that your smartphone connects to the internet as you will need to launch the website from your smartphone.
*Please note that screenshots or printed-out tickets will not be acceptable.
.
Tickets can be used at any time within 6 months from the purchase date
.
Business hours may be extended depending on the season or if there is an event.
description:
The Homma Museum of Art was founded in 1947 by the Homma family, who were wealthy merchants in the Edo period and were known as Japan's biggest landowners after the Meiji period.




The Homma family's policy has been to contribute to the local community since their ancestors opened their garden as an art museum. The operation of the museum at that time was not carried out solely by the founders, the Homma family, but also was a collaborative effort with those who cooperated with the opening of the museum, and entrusted their dreams to the post-war society.




While it's conveying the spirit of contributing to the local community, you can also enjoy exhibitions of antiques and contemporary art, the beauty of the four seasons woven by the garden "Kakubu-en," the beauty of the Kyoto-style wooden architecture of the villa "Seienkaku," of Sakata City, which prospered due to the Kitamae-bune shipping route, and how the museum fuses the charms of art, nature, and history.




• Nationally Designated Place of Scenic Beauty, Homma Villa Garden (Kakubu-en)
The garden was built in 1813 by the fourth generation Homma Mitsudo, with a path looping around its pond and the scenery of Mt. Chokai as a natural backdrop. The garden was named "Tsurumai-en" (Crane Dance Garden) by the feudal lord Sakai because of the cranes that landed on the island in the middle of the pond. The garden was created as a measure against unemployment for people working at the port during the winter. The garden is rich in taste with beautiful white azaleas in early summer and autumn leaves in fall, and is decorated with red stones from Sado and blue stones from Iyo brought on the Kitamae-bune shipping route




• Seienkaku
The villa was built as a place for the feudal lord, Marquis Sakai, to rest when he toured the territory. It is a comfortable building with refined Kyoto-style construction and modern furnishings reminiscent of Taisho-period romanticism.




•Tea room inside Seienkaku
There is a tea room in Seienkaku, which is also reminiscent of Taisho romanticism. From there, you can overlook the garden "Tsurumai-en," and the scenery will make you forget the time. They also offer original coffee, matcha, and sweets sets.

title: Kyoto–Toyama Hokuriku Shinkansen & Limited Express Train Ticket[E-Ticket]
overview:
Book now for flexible express train tickets between Kyoto and Toyama — at a special price only for visitors to Japan!




Exclusive to those with temporary visitor status, take the Hokuriku Shinkansen for less! This one-way ticket is valid up to 90 days after booking. Ride the Shinkansen between Toyama and Tsuruga Station, connecting to Kyoto with the Limited Express Thunderbird!







• For other Hokuriku Shinkansen routes click here
• For other bullet train routes and prices, see our check our Japan Shinkansen ticket page
• See the How it Works section for full details of ticket usage!
highlights: Get Hokuriku One-Way Ticket tickets at exclusive rates!
Exchange for reserved-seat ticket at no extra charge!
Flexible tickets valid up to 90 days after booking!
Book with a Rakuten account to earn points for every ticket!
important_info:
How to Use:
1: Choose the option for your direction of travel
2: Confirmation email will be sent to the appointed email address once the booking is made
3: The QR Code* voucher for your ticket is valid up to 90 days later
4: Please go to green ticket-vending machines and pick up physical ticket by scanning QR Code of e-ticket and non-Japanese passport (click here to search)
4: Use the physical ticket to pass through the automated ticket gate




Reserving a Reserved Seat Ticket
• Reserved seat tickets can be reserved at a ticket vending machine or a station ticket office.
• Click here for information on reserving reserved seat tickets from a ticket vending machine.




Eligibility:
• "Discounted One-way Ticket" user must be holding a passport issued by a foreign government（外国での永住権を持っているお客様を含め、日本国籍のお客様は使用できません）
• "Discounted One-way Ticket" user must be entering in Japan as a "短期滞在" (Temporary Visitor)"
• "短期滞在" (Temporary Visitor) is a status of residence defined under Japanese immigration law
• Individual customers are not allowed to purchase or exchange more than one of the same ticket for the same use period
• You must present your passport to receive a pass
description:
The Hokuriku Shinkansen is a high-speed bullet train line that serves some of Japan's beautiful, less-visited cities like Fukui, Toyama, and Kanazawa. Change trains at Tsuruga Station for the Limited Express Thunderbird, which connects the travel hubs of Osaka and Kyoto!




With a Kyoto-to-Toyama (or Toyama-to-Kyoto) Hokuriku One-Way Ticket for the Shinkansen and Thunderbird Express, you’ll cruise between cities on a ride like no other!




Book your flexible tickets now, use the QR Code* to collect them in Japan, and make a seat reservation at the station in Japan, so nothing gets in the way of your bullet train adventure!




*QR Code is a registered trademark of Denso Wave, Inc.
how_it_works:
This ticket can be used on a variety of services. See the table below for the validity, and any other tickets needed.






Purchasing a "Discounted One-way Ticket"



*Available from select overseas travel agencies.
Purchasing Exchange Order through a Travel Agency outside Japan




Step 1: Purchase an exchange order (E-TICKET) at sales locations outside Japan
• Once issued, the E-TICKET must be exchanged within 3 months




Step 2: Exchange the E-TICKET for a "Discounted One-way Ticket" at an exchange location in Japan
• E-TICKET exchange locations: Click here for details
• Receiving an E-TICKET: Click here for details
• The E-TICKET that could not been exchanged can be refunded at the overseas travel agency where the pass was originally purchased (a separate handling fee will be applied)
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is not available for purchase within Japan



Availability and Use



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is available and usable all year around



Changes



• Changing the validity period start date is allowed only once
• The ticket cannot be changed for a different ticket type; the ticket must instead be refunded, and the desired ticket purchased separately
• Changing the departure or arrival station within a section is allowed only once for unused tickets within the validity period
• Changing trains is allowed only once before the departure of the designated train, and only if tickets for the train being changed to are available within the validity period



Refunds



• Refunds are accepted only at the "Discounted One-way Ticket" exchange location.
• Click here for information on refund locations for refunds due to non-availability of services, delays, or other circumstances
• The sales price minus a handling fee will be refunded.




Handling fee:
If no reserved seating ticket has been issued: 560 YEN
If a reserved seating ticket has been issued:
• Up to 2 days before departure: 560 YEN
• Between day before departure and time of departure: 30% of original undiscounted Express ticket (based on original ticket purchase date) + 220 YEN (minimum handling fee: 560 YEN)




• A handling fee equivalent to the Limited express ticket for each train will be applied to a reserved seating ticket after departure time.
• Refunds cannot be provided if the "Discounted One-way Ticket" has been used or the validity period has ended.
• A refund of an unexchanged E-TICKET is available from the overseas travel agency where the ticket was purchased. Please note that an additional handling fee will apply.



Related information



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is only valid for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" holder
• Presentation of a passport (of the person using the "Discounted One-way Ticket") is required to exchange an E-TICKET
• No more than one E-TICKET can be exchanged per passport
• A passport is required when using a "Discounted One-way Ticket" and must be presented to the JR staff upon request
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" will not be reissued even if lost or stolen
• The conditions of use for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" are stipulated by the JR-WEST carriage conditions and the laws of Japan
• Conditions of use for an exchange order are stipulated by regulations and contracts of issuing companies
• If any discrepancies arise between the foreign language and Japanese versions of the carriage conditions, the Japanese version has precedence
• The information displayed (including prices) is current as of August 1, 2024, and is subject to change

title: Arima Onsen Taiko-no-yu & Rokkosan Athletic Park GREENIA Set Ticket

title: Airport Limousine Bus - Arrival at Haneda Airport
overview: Tokyo Airport Transportation is a comfortable and convenient limousine bus service that provides easy access to Haneda Airport from Tokyo City Terminal Station and major hotels in the city. You can reach Haneda Airport directly without any transfers. Additionally, we offer a secure trunk room where you can store your large luggage until you reach your destination. All our buses come equipped with free WiFi for your convenience.
highlights: Free Wifi is available on the bus.

title: Tokyo Rafting Tour in Okutama | AM & PM Tours | BBQ Available
overview: This is Crusoe Rafting's most basic tour, where you and your guide paddle down the clear waters of Okutama in an 8-person boat. We will share the precious experience of jumping off rocks into the clear water and viewing nature from the water's edge!
highlights: Enjoy the magnificent nature of Okutama!
During the tour, the guide will take pictures with a waterproof camera — they can be viewed and downloaded immediately
1 hour 10 minutes from Shinjuku or 1 hour 30 minutes from Shibuya by train — easy access from the center of Tokyo!
important_info:
About Participation



Participants must be at least 6 years old (elementary school students).
Children ages 6–12 years old must be accompanied by a parent or guardian (20 years old or older).
Minimum number of participants: 2
There are no restrictions on participation due to health conditions, but if you have any concerns please write a comment when booking. The tour operator will respond.
Please refrain from drinking alcohol before the rafting tour. Please be aware that you may not be allowed to board the boat if the guide deems it unacceptable.



Cancellation of the tour due to weather conditions, etc.



The tour may be cancelled due to weather conditions or other reasons. We will not be able to compensate for travel expenses even if the tour is canceled after you arrive at the destination. Please understand this in advance.
Rafting tours will be held even if it rains. In the event of cancellation due to inclement weather, the morning tour will be cancelled by 20:00 the day before the tour, and the afternoon tour will be cancelled by 08:00 on the day of the tour.



Other Information



Students are required to show their student ID card in order to apply for the student rate.
description:
Enjoy Okutama to the fullest — play in the river for summertime fun in deep rural Tokyo!




You can leave the hustle and bustle of the city behind and fully enjoy the magnificent nature of Okutama. Listen to birds chirping and enjoy the beautiful surface of the river. You can enjoy various views of nature as you float down the river together with your friends.




There is also a plan available for 1–4 people to charter a boat. You can enjoy rafting with a friend, a family of three, or even by yourself on a chartered boat.




Photos taken by Crusoe Rafting are uploaded to a password-protected web album immediately after the tour, and can be viewed and downloaded freely.




Insurance
Crusoe Rafting tours include insurance:



Personal Accident Insurance: Death, permanent disability: 8,000,000 JPY / hospitalization: 5,000 JPY per day / hospital visit: 3,000 JPY
Indemnity Insurance: Crusoe Rafting has liability insurance in addition to the above accident insurance, so you can rest assured that you will be covered in case of an emergency.



About Fees
Tour prices differ according to the season, and whether you book for a weekday or a weekend/national holiday. Please choose your date, and the price option for that day will be highlighted.




The "Junior" price applies to high-school or college students with a student ID. For others, please book the "Adult" option for those of high-school age and older.




About the BBQ set
BBQ set plan is available to enjoy BBQ after rafting. After the tour, we will drive you to the BBQ place. If necessary, we can make a 10-minute stop at a nearby supermarket to purchase ingredients, etc. Please apply for this option if you wish.
BBQ set: 3,600 yen/person
Contents: Meat and vegetable BBQ ingredients (270g meat and 5-6 vegetables)



You are free to bring your own food and alcohol.
You can also buy them at a nearby supermarket and bring them in.
Includes transportation to and from the BBQ site.

Total
5,000 JPY

Designed as a visually engaging experience for both adults and children, the exhibition invites visitors to step into the world of ukiyo-e through a fully immersive 3D visual space.

title: 【Iriomote Island / 1 Day】 Complete set of 3 islands from Iriomotejima! Mangrove SUP or Canoe & Barasu Island landing & Yubu Island sightsee
overview:
Barasu Island Snorkeling & Yubu Island Sightseeing
This is a package plan to enjoy two “islands”, Yubu Island Sightseeing and Barasu Island Snorkeling! You can visit Barasu Island, the “Miracle Island”, cross the sea on a buffalo cart, and visit the subtropical botanical garden!
highlights: More than 300,000 people have participated in this tour! Safe and secure tour!
Recommended for beginners!
Includes participant benefits (coupons for use at restaurants, etc., and information on popular spots)!
Free photo data, equipment rental, and hot shower!
Day trip participation from Ishigaki Island is available!
important_info:
-Meeting time may change depending on the season and field conditions on the day of the tour. The store will contact you the day before the tour with the meeting place and time.
-The tour will be held rain or shine. However, we may cancel the tour if we deem that the field is unsafe.
-Please change into clothes that you don't mind getting wet before the tour.
-Please use the restroom before coming to the tour. (There are no restrooms near the tour site.)
-Please note that due to the reduced size of Baras Island, there is a possibility that you may not be able to go ashore. Please understand this in advance.
-The tour is available for ages 6 and up to 59 years old.
-Tours are available in English or Japanese.
description:
Enjoy SUP and canoeing in the mangrove forest, sightseeing in Yubu Island, landing on Barasu Island, and much more!




Mangrove, miraculous island, ocean... Enjoy Iriomote Island, a candidate for the World Heritage in this one-day plan to enjoy the nature of the island to the fullest.




★Mangrove SUP/Canoe
Go through the mangrove forest by SUP or canoe.




Sightseeing in Yubu Island
You will ride on a buffalo cart from Iriomote Island to Yubu Island and cross over the sea. On the buffalo cart, you will be healed by the cute buffaloes pulling hard and enjoy the quiet island time leisurely. Yubu Island is a very small island, so you can enjoy sightseeing even in a short time.




Landing on Barasu Island
Barasu Island, said to be one of only two islands in the world, can only appear under certain conditions. You can land on this “miraculous island” made of only pieces of white coral reefs at the best time!

overview:

important_info:
[Reservation Process]
・Please let us know your preferred shooting location and date when making a reservation.
・Operation hours are from 08:00 until sunset. After your reservation is confirmed, the time and location will be adjusted based on mutual availability.
(For night shoots outside the mentioned hours, please contact us for further discussion.)
・Once the photographer and location are confirmed, we will send you the final details (including the exact meeting point) via a chat tool such as LINE. Please download and register on the LINE　app in advance.
(*Note: After the final details have been sent, no changes to the shooting time or location can be made.)
[In case of rain]
・If there is a forecast of rain three days before the scheduled shoot, or if unexpected rain occurs on the day, please choose one of the following options:
① Change the date
② Change the location (we will provide indoor options)
③ Cancel
For option ① Change the date:
Please inform us of your new preferred date.
(It is not guaranteed that the date can be changed according to your request, as it depends on the photographer's availability.)
For option ② Change the location:
You can choose from the indoor locations that we will provide. If the weather clears up, we will proceed with the shoot at your originally requested location.
For option ③ Cancel:
Your reservation will be canceled, and a full refund will be provided.
[Important Notes]
・If you are late for the meeting time, your shooting time may be shortened, so please arrive on time. No refunds will be provided in case of a no-show or late arrival.
・Please be mindful of your belongings, as they may be difficult to recover if lost during the session.
・Please be especially careful to avoid any injuries or accidents. Assistance may be limited.
・We recommend purchasing personal travel insurance.
・After your reservation is confirmed, our staff will provide information via WhatsApp about "cashless support for injuries or illnesses during the tour (tour date = insurance coverage date)." Please fill out the information sheet regarding insurance. Please note that if the form is not completed, the insurance cannot be registered.
・Retouching includes adjustments to color temperature, color balance, tone, light brightness, cloudiness, and composition. We do not offer body or facial feature adjustments.
・Retouching will reflect the mood of the shoot day. If you have specific preferences regarding the atmosphere, colors, etc., please let us know.
・The best shots from the session may be featured in our portfolio or on social media. If you prefer not to have your photos used, please inform us in advance.
description:

・Location: You can shoot anywhere in Nagoya! Please specify your preferred location when making a reservation.

If you wish to shoot in multiple locations, please use public transportation. Additionally, any separate expenses, including transportation costs and facility entrance fees (including the photographer's), will be your responsibility.




・Final Location:

・Props and Poses:

・Photo Delivery

・Retouching: We offer retouching for up to 10 selected photos upon request.

title: TOKYO BRT 1 day ticket
overview:
This convenient one-day ticket allows unlimited rides on all Tokyo BRT lines, connecting central Tokyo with the vibrant waterfront area. You can ride as many times as you like until the last bus on the day you activate the pass. Simply present the pass to the driver when boarding.




Major Stops Include:
Toranomon Hills, Shinbashi, Kachidoki BRT, Toyosu Market, Michino Terrace Toyosu (Toyosu Market), Ariake Tennis Forest, International Exhibition Center, Tokyo Teleport, Harumi Chuo, Harumi BRT Terminal, Toyosu, Harumi Mirai, Harumi Pier Park, HARUMI FLAG (Harumi 5-chome Terminal.
highlights: Book with a Rakuten account to earn or use points for every ticket!
Book now, use up to 3 months later!
important_info:
.
This ticket cannot be resold. A resold ticket will be invalid.
.
Children aged 6 to 12 are charged the child fare (including 12-year-old elementary school students). Children under 6 can ride for free when accompanied by an adult.
.
When using the voucher, open the usage screen from the link sent to you at the time of purchase, press the “Use” button, and show the screen to the driver.
*Please ensure you have a stable network connection, as you will need to access the web page on your smartphone.
*Screenshots and printed copies cannot be accepted.
*If you purchase multiple tickets in one transaction, they cannot be used separately, and all tickets will be activated simultaneously. To use the tickets individually, please purchase them separately.
*For security purposes, you may be required to authenticate using a one-time password (OTP). If prompted, please follow the steps below:
1. Enter the email address to receive the OTP and press the send button.
2. Check that the OTP has been received by the email address set up in step 1. (The email containing the OTP will be sent from the domain “@linktivity.co.jp.” Please also check your spam folder just to be sure. If you can’t find it there, your email address may be incorrect. Open the URL for the usage screen from the voucher again and try again by entering your email address.)
3. Enter the OTP listed in the received email into the ticket screen and press the Confirm button. If you enter it correctly, the usage screen will be displayed. (Once OTP authentication is complete, the OTP will be sent to the email address you set. This email address cannot be changed, so please set an email address that can be confirmed locally on the day of use.)
.
Carry-on restrictions:
You may bring personal items on board with a total weight of up to 10kg, a total volume of 0.027 cubic meters, and a maximum length of 1 meter.
.
Regarding the use of wheelchairs and strollers:
You may use the designated free space without folding your wheelchair or stroller. However, please note that boarding may be refused if the free space is already occupied by another wheelchair, stroller, etc., or if the vehicle is too crowded to allow safe boarding.
.
If your ticket cannot be displayed due to a malfunction of your device, a dead battery, or unstable communication service, you will not be able to use the service. In such cases, please pay the fare separately before using the service. Please note that fares paid during the period when your ticket cannot be displayed will not be refunded.
.
Recommended environment for using digital tickets: iOS 15 or later / Safari (latest version), Android 10.0 or later / Chrome (latest version).
*Please note that even with the recommended browsing environment, service availability may vary depending on your device or usage conditions.

title: Tokyo Grand Sumo Tournament Viewing Tour with Chair Seating (May/Sep 2026)
overview: Enjoy a unique experience that's simply not possible by just watching on TV! Come face to face with Japanese culture that has been kept alive through many years, on this tour guided by a National Government Licensed English Guide Interpreter knowledgeable about sumo wrestling. Watch sumo, Japan's national sport, from comfortable reserved seating!
highlights: Watch sumo, Japan's national sport, from comfortable reserved seating
All tour participants will receive "The Sumo," a booklet about sumo (in English)
Reserve with a Rakuten account to earn or use Rakuten Points for each booking!
important_info:
Itinerary



Meet at Ryogoku View Hotel (not Ryogoku Kokugikan)
— If a B-class Chair Seating option is booked: Start 13:50, end 14:00
— If an A-class or S-class Chair Seat option is booked: Start 12:30, end 12:40
• Please show your reservation confirmation sheet at reception
• Access to Ryogoku View Hotel:
— 1 min on foot from Ryogoku Station (JR Sobu Line)
— 7 min on foot from Ryogoku Station (Toei Oedo Line)
• After exiting from the JR Ryogoku Station West Exit ticket gate, cross the pedestrian crossing immediately on the left and it will be just ahead (opposite direction of Ryogoku Kokugikan)
• Please arrive by 10 minutes before departure; the tour will walk to Ryogoku Kokugikan
Lecture About Sumo
— If a B-class Chair Seating option is booked: Start 14:00, end 14:20
— If an A-class or S-class Chair Seat option is booked: Start 12:40, end 13:00
• Duration: 20 minutes
• The National Government Licensed English Guide Interpreter will conduct a lecture about sumo at Ryogoku View Hotel.
Transfer
• Transportation: On Foot
• Head to Ryogoku Kokugikan
Watch Sumo at Ryogoku Kokugikan
— If a B-class Chair Seating option is booked: Start 14:30, end 18:00
— If an A-class or S-class Chair Seat option is booked: Start 13:10, end 18:00
• Duration: 210 minutes or 290 minutes
• Enjoy the thrilling sumo matches:
— If a B-class Chair Seating option is booked: from 2nd floor B-class seating
— If an A-class Chair Seat option is booked: from 2nd floor A-class seating
— If an S-class Chair Seat option is booked: from 2nd floor S-class seating
• If a "Light Snack & Cheering Goods” option is booked: customers will be provided a light snack (Kokugikan specialty yakitori [grilled chicken skewer] and drink) and a cheering towel (1 per person).
Time Schedule:
• 15:40 - Makuuchi Wrestler Ceremonial Entrance, Yokozuna Grand Champion Ring Entrance (will not take place if there are no Yokozuna present) — The crowd comes alive at the sight of their favorite wrestlers and cheers them on. The atmosphere in the arena brightens instantly.
• 16:00 - Makuuchi Division Wrestlers' Sumo Matches
• 18:00 - Bow-twirling Ceremony
Things to Do:
1. Check out the arena entrance lobby to look at the championship flags and other items given to champion sumo wrestlers. The lobby is also a popular spot for photo taking.
*Not available on the last tournament day due to award ceremony preparations for the concluding program.
2. The arena houses a Sumo Museum where important sumo-related items such as woodblock prints, folding screens and ceremonial aprons from as far back as the Edo period are on display. Check out the exhibit during a break in the sumo matches.
3. There are shops on the 1st and 2nd floors of the arena. Enjoy shopping for fans, stationery, sweets, and other sumo-related merchandise.
*Bringing food and drinks including alcohol into Kokugikan is prohibited.
4. Visitors can also enjoy chanko hot pot, the traditional meal of sumo wrestlers, at the large hall on the arena basement floor (additional charges apply).
• All tour participants will receive "The Sumo," a booklet about sumo (in English); and Banzuke-hyo, a table of rankings, as a gift (1 per person).
Tour Ends
• Start 18:00
• Please head to your next destination on your own after the tour.
• The whole area around Ryogoku Station is filled with the atmosphere of the Grand Sumo Tournament. There are also many chanko hot pot restaurants run by former sumo wrestlers in this area.




Min. pax number to book tour: 1
Min. pax number to run tour: 1



Remarks



Operation Conditions/Tour Conditions



The number of customers for 1 group booking for this tour is 1-19. If booking for a group of 20 or more customers, please note that the group will be divided.
description:
1. Enjoy a unique experience that's simply not possible by just watching on TV! Come face to face with Japanese culture that has been kept alive through many years, such as the presence of a referee which began in the Sengoku period (about 1467-1603), and the use of vivid 'kesho-mawashi' ceremonial aprons and the ring entering procession which have continued from the Edo period (1603-1868), on this tour guided by a National Government Licensed English Guide Interpreter knowledgeable about sumo wrestling. Watch sumo, Japan's national sport, from comfortable reserved seating (chair seating).




Seating Map






2. All tour participants will receive "The Sumo," a booklet about sumo (in English); and Banzuke-hyo, a table of rankings, as a gift! It has easy-to-understand explanations about the origins of sumo wrestling, match rules, reading the banzuke list, enjoying the ceremony, and more, so feel free to use it during the sumo viewing.




3. There will only be booking reception for the 2026 May and September tournaments, and official booking confirmations will be from February 2026. Thank you for your understanding.

This tour promotes environment(eco)-friendly efforts and cross-cultural understanding as stated under Sunrise Tours' Sustainable Development Goals. We advocate for responsible travel, and urge one and all to become responsible travelers. Please check the following page for details: http://jtbgmt.com/en/g-web/sdgs

title: [Miyako Island] Enjoy snorkeling and Miyako blue! ★Beginners and families welcome★
important_info:
.
Age limit: 3~65
.
Activitty cancellation: If the waves and winds exceed our safety standards due to bad weather, the tour will be canceled.
.
Precautions: ・Meeting and Contact: The location and time may change depending on the sea conditions, so the shop will contact you with details the day before. Please be sure to check. ・Rentals: Masks, fins, and life jackets are free. Prescription masks and winter wetsuits can be rented on the day for 1,000 yen (tax included) each. ・Held Rain or Shine: The tour will be held even in the rain as the sea is still beautiful. However, it will be canceled if safety cannot be ensured due to strong winds, high waves, etc. ・Clothing and Preparation: Please wear your swimsuit beforehand and come dressed in clothes that you don't mind getting wet. Please use the restroom before coming. ・Lateness/Absence: If you will be late, please be sure to contact us in advance. ・Meeting Place: We will meet at the location. As we will be using a well-maintained beach, please note that a separate parking fee (approximately 1,000 yen) will be required. ・Encounter Rate: As these are wild animals, we cannot guarantee a 100% chance of encountering sea turtles. Our experienced guides will do their best to search and provide you with the best possible experience!
.
Notices: [Important: Who cannot participate?] To ensure the safety of our customers, we strictly prohibit participation in this tour for the following individuals. If it is discovered on the day of the tour, participation will be refused for safety reasons, and the prescribed cancellation fee will be charged. Individuals outside the target age range: Individuals under 3 years old and individuals over 66 years old. (This is a restriction based on safety standards.) Pregnant women: Regardless of whether they are in their second trimester or not, participation is prohibited for any reason, considering the possibility of accidents and the effects of water pressure. Individuals who have consumed alcohol or are hungover: Underwater activities while intoxicated are extremely dangerous and strictly prohibited. Individuals with specific medical conditions: Individuals with epilepsy, severe asthma, heart disease, respiratory disease, diabetes, mental illness, or suspected cases thereof. [Notes regarding health conditions] If you are currently receiving medical treatment or taking regular medication, please be sure to provide details when making your reservation. If you have a history of major injuries or surgeries (lungs, ears, heart, etc.), a doctor's certificate may be required. Please inquire in advance. [Safety and Manners] No contact with wild animals: Touching, chasing, or obstructing the path of sea turtles is prohibited. Strictly follow the guide's instructions: For your safety, please follow the guide's instructions while at sea. Failure to do so may result in the immediate cancellation of the tour. Cancellation decision: If we determine that the weather conditions or high waves exceed our safety standards, we will cancel the tour.

title: Osaka–Toyama Hokuriku Shinkansen & Limited Express Train Ticket[E-Ticket]
overview:
Book now for flexible express train tickets between Osaka and Toyama — at a special price only for visitors to Japan!




Exclusive to those with temporary visitor status, take the Hokuriku Shinkansen for less! This one-way ticket is valid up to 90 days after booking. Ride the Shinkansen between Toyama and Tsuruga Station, connecting to Osaka with the Limited Express Thunderbird!







• For other Hokuriku Shinkansen routes click here
• For other bullet train routes and prices, see our check our Japan Shinkansen ticket page
• See the How it Works section for full details of ticket usage!
highlights: Get Hokuriku One-Way Ticket tickets at exclusive rates!
Exchange for reserved-seat ticket at no extra charge!
Flexible tickets valid up to 90 days after booking!
Book with a Rakuten account to earn points for every ticket!
important_info:
How to Use:
1: Choose the option for your direction of travel
2: Confirmation email will be sent to the appointed email address once the booking is made
3: The QR Code* voucher for your ticket is valid up to 90 days later
4: Please go to green ticket-vending machines and pick up physical ticket by scanning QR Code of e-ticket and non-Japanese passport (click here to search)
4: Use the physical ticket to pass through the automated ticket gate




Reserving a Reserved Seat Ticket
• Reserved seat tickets can be reserved at a ticket vending machine or a station ticket office.
• Click here for information on reserving reserved seat tickets from a ticket vending machine.




Eligibility:
• "Discounted One-way Ticket" user must be holding a passport issued by a foreign government（外国での永住権を持っているお客様を含め、日本国籍のお客様は使用できません）
• "Discounted One-way Ticket" user must be entering in Japan as a "短期滞在" (Temporary Visitor)"
• "短期滞在" (Temporary Visitor) is a status of residence defined under Japanese immigration law
• Individual customers are not allowed to purchase or exchange more than one of the same ticket for the same use period
• You must present your passport to receive a pass
description:
The Hokuriku Shinkansen is a high-speed bullet train line that serves some of Japan's beautiful, less-visited cities like Fukui, Toyama, and Kanazawa. Change trains at Tsuruga Station for the Limited Express Thunderbird, which connects the travel hubs of Osaka and Kyoto!




With a Osaka-to-Toyama (or Toyama-to-Osaka) Hokuriku One-Way Ticket for the Shinkansen and Thunderbird Express, you’ll cruise between cities on a ride like no other!




Book your flexible tickets now, use the QR Code* to collect them in Japan, and make a seat reservation at the station in Japan, so nothing gets in the way of your bullet train adventure!




*QR Code is a registered trademark of Denso Wave, Inc.
how_it_works:
This ticket can be used on a variety of services. See the table below for the validity, and any other tickets needed.






Purchasing a "Discounted One-way Ticket"



*Available from select overseas travel agencies.
Purchasing Exchange Order through a Travel Agency outside Japan




Step 1: Purchase an exchange order (E-TICKET) at sales locations outside Japan
• Once issued, the E-TICKET must be exchanged within 3 months




Step 2: Exchange the E-TICKET for a "Discounted One-way Ticket" at an exchange location in Japan
• E-TICKET exchange locations: Click here for details
• Receiving an E-TICKET: Click here for details
• The E-TICKET that could not been exchanged can be refunded at the overseas travel agency where the pass was originally purchased (a separate handling fee will be applied)
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is not available for purchase within Japan



Availability and Use



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is available and usable all year around



Changes



• Changing the validity period start date is allowed only once
• The ticket cannot be changed for a different ticket type; the ticket must instead be refunded, and the desired ticket purchased separately
• Changing the departure or arrival station within a section is allowed only once for unused tickets within the validity period
• Changing trains is allowed only once before the departure of the designated train, and only if tickets for the train being changed to are available within the validity period



Refunds



• Refunds are accepted only at the "Discounted One-way Ticket" exchange location.
• Click here for information on refund locations for refunds due to non-availability of services, delays, or other circumstances
• The sales price minus a handling fee will be refunded.




Handling fee:
If no reserved seating ticket has been issued: 560 YEN
If a reserved seating ticket has been issued:
• Up to 2 days before departure: 560 YEN
• Between day before departure and time of departure: 30% of original undiscounted Express ticket (based on original ticket purchase date) + 220 YEN (minimum handling fee: 560 YEN)




• A handling fee equivalent to the Limited express ticket for each train will be applied to a reserved seating ticket after departure time.
• Refunds cannot be provided if the "Discounted One-way Ticket" has been used or the validity period has ended.
• A refund of an unexchanged E-TICKET is available from the overseas travel agency where the ticket was purchased. Please note that an additional handling fee will apply.



Related information



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is only valid for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" holder
• Presentation of a passport (of the person using the "Discounted One-way Ticket") is required to exchange an E-TICKET
• No more than one E-TICKET can be exchanged per passport
• A passport is required when using a "Discounted One-way Ticket" and must be presented to the JR staff upon request
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" will not be reissued even if lost or stolen
• The conditions of use for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" are stipulated by the JR-WEST carriage conditions and the laws of Japan
• Conditions of use for an exchange order are stipulated by regulations and contracts of issuing companies
• If any discrepancies arise between the foreign language and Japanese versions of the carriage conditions, the Japanese version has precedence
• The information displayed (including prices) is current as of August 1, 2024, and is subject to change

title: Osaka Solaniwa Onsen Admission Ticket
overview:
Get tickets for Solaniwa Onsen in Osaka Bay Tower, and experience happiness from the depths of your soul. Even travelers with tattoos can enjoy traditional onsen culture here, with our special concealing tattoos!




Solaniwa Onsen is a hot-spring bathhouse offering fun and relaxation to visitors in Osaka. With a traditional atmosphere, it also has a beautiful rooftop garden of about 3,000 square meters: the Tenku Garden. With a thousand torii gates, waterfalls, and other displays that create a sense of openness, it seems hard to believe that you are in the heart of Osaka. Enjoy the view of seasonal trees and flowers while relaxing in the footbath area!




The hot bath area is like a theme park where you can enjoy the alkaline natural hot spring water, known as "Beautiful Hada no Yu," bubbling up from 1,000 meters underground. There are nine types of bath, including an open-air bath, a garden-view bath, and a carbonated spring bath. Visitors can also enjoy saunas and scrubbers.
highlights: Even travelers with tattoos can now visit, with our special concealing stickers (see How it Works)
When you purchase with your Rakuten account, you can earn and use Rakuten points!

description:
To experience true happiness that comes from within your soul. Solaniwa Onsen OSAKA BAY TOWER




Solaniwa Onsen is a hot spring town that aims to provide relaxation and fun to visitors. It offers the atmosphere of traditional Osaka and has a beautiful garden that is worth noticing.




The Tenku Garden is about 3,000 square meters on the rooftop. Visitors can enjoy the view of seasonal trees and flowers while relaxing in the footbath area. The garden has a thousand torii gates, waterfalls, and other displays that create a sense of openness, making it hard to believe that you are in the heart of Osaka.




The hot bath area is a theme park where visitors can enjoy the slightly alkaline natural hot spring water, known as "Beautiful Hada no Yu," that bubbles up from 1,000 meters underground. There are nine types of baths, including an open-air bath, a garden-view bath, and a carbonated spring bath. Visitors can also enjoy saunas and scrubbers.




Solaniwa Onsen provides a traditional ryokan experience in Osaka, making it an ideal spot for a relaxing hot spring getaway.




▼The Solaniwa Onsen is an attraction suitable for everyone, from children to adults, to enjoy throughout the day!
…………………………
[1] Garden & footbath
→Enjoy a footbath in a Japanese garden spanning about 1,000 tsubos while wearing a stylish yukata and admiring the seasonal flowers and trees!
…………………………
[2] Hot spring
→Experience 9 different types of baths, including an open-air option, using natural hot springs sourced from 1,000m below ground!
…………………………
[3] Private open-air bath Fee required/reservation required
→We have ten private open-air baths with natural hot spring water. Each bath features a small garden, perfect for families.
…………………………
[4] Bedrock bath Fee required
→Experience the ultimate relaxation with our seven types of rock baths that will leave you feeling comfortable and rejuvenated.
…………………………
[5] Relaxation (Massage) Fee required/ reservation recommended
→In our relaxation space, we offer a wide range of services, including Japanese-style body care and full-fledged beauty treatments.
…………………………
[6] Meal
→Indulge in some delectable cuisine at Kuidaore Yokocho, an Izakaya-style restaurant.
…………………………
[7] Benten festival
→Experience the festival daily at our Benten Fair game corner stall!
…………………………
[8] Iminemachi
→We have a rest area with a recliner chair where you can take a nap.
how_it_works:
【Reservation period】
Within 2 months
【Reservation deadline】
Please make a reservation by 23:00 1 day before the participation date.
【Ticket expiration date】
7 days from activity date




How to visit with tattoos
Like most onsen, Solaniwa has a general rule against tattoos for cultural reasons.




However, we have launched a new system using tattoo-concealing stickers, to cater for international visitors!




If you buy the special stickers sold at Solaniwa Onsen to completely cover all tattoos, you can enter and enjoy. Sizes are 145 x 100 mm, 95 x 75 mm, and 75 x 28 mm. Stickers sold elsewhere may not be accepted.




▶︎Steps from purchase to admission!
[For Admission Ticket Plan]
Step 1
After purchasing on Rakuten Travel Experiences, an e-ticket will be sent to your registered email address.
Step 2
When you arrive at the facility, please present the two-dimensional code (displayed on your smartphone) to the reception staff at the on-site counter using the link included in the e-ticket email sent to you at the time of purchase.
Step 3
Please choose one of the in-house clothes with your favorite design and change into it.
Step 4
Please enjoy the inside of the hotel, including the garden and public bath (additional fees may be required depending on the content).




[For Admission Ticket + Bedrock Bath + Totoi Drink Plan]
▼Introduction to bedrock bath
In the bedrock bath, the far infrared rays of the dry sauna warm your body from the core, and the deep sweating effect brings about health, beauty, and healing effects. At Solaniwa Onsen, we have a total of 7 rooms that can be used by couples as well as a tent sauna area in the garden on the 5th floor. Please enjoy your stay in our special bedrock bathrobes.

title: Osaka–Kanazawa Hokuriku Shinkansen & Limited Express Train Ticket[E-Ticket]
overview:
Book now for flexible express train tickets between Osaka and Kanazawa — at a special price only for visitors to Japan!




Exclusive to those with temporary visitor status, take the Hokuriku Shinkansen for less! This one-way ticket is valid up to 90 days after booking. Ride the Shinkansen between Kanazawa and Tsuruga Station, connecting to Osaka with the Limited Express Thunderbird!







• For other Hokuriku Shinkansen routes click here
• For other bullet train routes and prices, see our check our Japan Shinkansen ticket page
• See the How it Works section for full details of ticket usage!
highlights: Get Hokuriku One-Way Ticket tickets at exclusive rates!
Exchange for reserved-seat ticket at no extra charge!
Flexible tickets valid up to 90 days after booking!
Book with a Rakuten account to earn points for every ticket!
important_info:
How to Use:
1: Choose the option for your direction of travel
2: Confirmation email will be sent to the appointed email address once the booking is made
3: The QR Code* voucher for your ticket is valid up to 90 days later
4: Please go to green ticket-vending machines and pick up physical ticket by scanning QR Code of e-ticket and non-Japanese passport (click here to search)
4: Use the physical ticket to pass through the automated ticket gate




Reserving a Reserved Seat Ticket
• Reserved seat tickets can be reserved at a ticket vending machine or a station ticket office.
• Click here for information on reserving reserved seat tickets from a ticket vending machine.




Eligibility:
• "Discounted One-way Ticket" user must be holding a passport issued by a foreign government（外国での永住権を持っているお客様を含め、日本国籍のお客様は使用できません）
• "Discounted One-way Ticket" user must be entering in Japan as a "短期滞在" (Temporary Visitor)"
• "短期滞在" (Temporary Visitor) is a status of residence defined under Japanese immigration law
• Individual customers are not allowed to purchase or exchange more than one of the same ticket for the same use period
• You must present your passport to receive a pass
description:
The Hokuriku Shinkansen is a high-speed bullet train line that serves some of Japan's beautiful, less-visited cities like Fukui, Toyama, and Kanazawa. Change trains at Tsuruga Station for the Limited Express Thunderbird, which connects the travel hubs of Osaka and Kyoto!




With a Osaka-to-Kanazawa (or Kanazawa-to-Osaka) Hokuriku One-Way Ticket for the Shinkansen and Thunderbird Express, you’ll cruise between cities on a ride like no other!




Book your flexible tickets now, use the QR Code* to collect them in Japan, and make a seat reservation at the station in Japan, so nothing gets in the way of your bullet train adventure!




*QR Code is a registered trademark of Denso Wave, Inc.
how_it_works:
This ticket can be used on a variety of services. See the table below for the validity, and any other tickets needed.






Purchasing a "Discounted One-way Ticket"



*Available from select overseas travel agencies.
Purchasing Exchange Order through a Travel Agency outside Japan




Step 1: Purchase an exchange order (E-TICKET) at sales locations outside Japan
• Once issued, the E-TICKET must be exchanged within 3 months




Step 2: Exchange the E-TICKET for a "Discounted One-way Ticket" at an exchange location in Japan
• E-TICKET exchange locations: Click here for details
• Receiving an E-TICKET: Click here for details
• The E-TICKET that could not been exchanged can be refunded at the overseas travel agency where the pass was originally purchased (a separate handling fee will be applied)
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is not available for purchase within Japan



Availability and Use



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is available and usable all year around



Changes



• Changing the validity period start date is allowed only once
• The ticket cannot be changed for a different ticket type; the ticket must instead be refunded, and the desired ticket purchased separately
• Changing the departure or arrival station within a section is allowed only once for unused tickets within the validity period
• Changing trains is allowed only once before the departure of the designated train, and only if tickets for the train being changed to are available within the validity period



Refunds



• Refunds are accepted only at the "Discounted One-way Ticket" exchange location.
• Click here for information on refund locations for refunds due to non-availability of services, delays, or other circumstances
• The sales price minus a handling fee will be refunded.




Handling fee:
If no reserved seating ticket has been issued: 560 YEN
If a reserved seating ticket has been issued:
• Up to 2 days before departure: 560 YEN
• Between day before departure and time of departure: 30% of original undiscounted Express ticket (based on original ticket purchase date) + 220 YEN (minimum handling fee: 560 YEN)




• A handling fee equivalent to the Limited express ticket for each train will be applied to a reserved seating ticket after departure time.
• Refunds cannot be provided if the "Discounted One-way Ticket" has been used or the validity period has ended.
• A refund of an unexchanged E-TICKET is available from the overseas travel agency where the ticket was purchased. Please note that an additional handling fee will apply.



Related information



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is only valid for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" holder
• Presentation of a passport (of the person using the "Discounted One-way Ticket") is required to exchange an E-TICKET
• No more than one E-TICKET can be exchanged per passport
• A passport is required when using a "Discounted One-way Ticket" and must be presented to the JR staff upon request
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" will not be reissued even if lost or stolen
• The conditions of use for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" are stipulated by the JR-WEST carriage conditions and the laws of Japan
• Conditions of use for an exchange order are stipulated by regulations and contracts of issuing companies
• If any discrepancies arise between the foreign language and Japanese versions of the carriage conditions, the Japanese version has precedence
• The information displayed (including prices) is current as of August 1, 2024, and is subject to change

title: Kujukushima Zoo & Botanical Garden Mori Kirara E-Tickets
overview: Kujukushima Zoo & Botanical Garden Mori Kirara is home to many animals, including giraffes, red pandas, and meerkats. There are also rare animals such as the Tsushima horse, one of the few remaining in Japan, and the Tsushima wildcats. At the penguin pavilion, you can see penguins swimming vigorously across the ceiling!
highlights: Gaze up at the overhead penguin tank, where penguins seem to fly past you
See a diverse range of flora and fauna: animals from 45 species and some 14,000 flowers
Admire animals such as giraffes, lesser pandas, ring-tailed lemurs, and more
Stroll around the botanical garden, where you'll find seasonal and evergreen blooms and plants

description:
Located on a hill overlooking Kujuku Islands, [Mori Kirara] is a zoo and botanical garden with the theme of healing and nature of Kujuku Islands. Visitors can see animals living lively in the park surrounded by seasonal flowers and trees, as well as their adorable expressions.




The zoo exhibits 173 animals of about 45 species, including a penguin pavilion with one of the largest ceiling tank in Japan, lions, and Tsushima wildcats. In addition to petting and feeding the small animals, the park also offers a variety of hands-on programs, such as a horseback riding experience with the rare and scarce Tsushima horses and backyard tours. The park also has an ornamental greenhouse and approximately 1,400 species of plants, with spring and fall being the best times to see the beautiful roses blooming in the rose garden, the largest of its kind in western Japan.
