title: Aomori “A Missing Exhibition” Admission Ticket [Sat, Apr 25–Sun, May 31, 2026]
overview:
“Missing” — a word that lurks within our everyday lives. Sometimes evoking unease, at other times stirring our imagination.




Vanishing without a trace, disappearing suddenly and leaving behind only fragments… This exhibition unravels the many facets of the phenomenon we call "missing.”




Inside the venue, you’ll find posters, personal belongings left behind, urban legends, and even unsolved mysteries buried in history—all related to “missing.” These items and pieces of information are carefully curated and displayed from a unique perspective. Take a moment to listen to the stories each exhibit has to tell.




Through this exhibition, we hope to spark your curiosity and spirit of inquiry.




Please Note:
All “missing” cases presented in this exhibition are fictional. They are not intended to encourage real-world search efforts.
highlights: A unique exhibition themed on the concept of “missing”
A heart-pounding experience akin to urban legends of disappearance or being “spirited away”
Book with your Rakuten account to earn and use Rakuten Points!

• Umbrellas and rain gear are not permitted inside the museum. Please place umbrellas in the stands and leave rain gear at reception.
• Large luggage (such as suitcases or backpacks) must be stored at reception or in coin lockers.
• Animals are not permitted, except for service animals.
• Smoking is strictly prohibited throughout the premises.
• Writing implements other than pencils may not be used inside the exhibition rooms.
• Please follow all instructions provided by museum staff.

title: Tochigi: Nikko Tamozawa Imperial Villa Memorial Park Admission Ticket
overview: Discover the rich history and exquisite beauty of Nikko Tamozawa Imperial Villa Memorial Park. This park boasts a collection of historical buildings, including the magnificent Nikko Tamozawa Imperial Villa, designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan. Marvel at the grandeur of the largest remaining Imperial Villa from the Meiji and Taisho periods, and immerse yourself in the serene atmosphere of the surrounding gardens, where you can admire seasonal flowers and the tasteful tea house.
highlights: Take in the stately appearance of the 100-year-old Nikko Tamozawa Imperial Villa
Explore the buildings and gardens, and envision the intricate architectural techniques and imperial culture of the time
Immerse yourself in the seasonal beauty of the gardens, where you can admire vibrant flowers and stunning autumn foliage
Indulge in a delightful dining experience at Nikko Tamozawa Imperial Villa "Saryo" and savor an array of refreshments

description:
The Nikko Tamozawa Imperial Villa Memorial Park is located near the Nikko Toshogu Shrine, which is registered as a World Heritage Site, and other two shrines and one temple in Nikko, and is bordered on the north by the Nikko mountain range including Mt. It is located in a quiet and scenic area surrounded by a grove of cedar trees.




The main attraction is the former imperial villa, which was built in 1899 as a retreat for Crown Prince Yoshihito (Emperor Taisho). The Imperial Villa was used by three generations of Emperors and Crown Princes, including Emperor Taisho, until it was closed in 1947. After the war, it was used as a museum, lodging, and training facility, and was restored and maintained by Tochigi Prefecture over a period of three years, and was revived as a memorial park in 2000. The buildings are a group of buildings with architectural styles from the late Edo, Meiji, and Taisho periods, and are the largest of the existing imperial residences from the Meiji and Taisho periods. These buildings and gardens offer a glimpse into the architectural techniques and imperial culture of the period. In 2003, it was designated as a "National Important Cultural Property" as a valuable building, and in 2007, it was selected as one of the "100 Best Historical Parks in Japan.




At the Nikko Tamozawa Imperial Villa "Saryo" located in the outdoor rest area, visitors can enjoy a set meal of green tea and sweets, "Nikko Kirifuri Kogen Gyu Curry", Omelet Rice or others. You can spend a relaxing time in the calm Japanese-style restaurant. (Open only from April to November)




Please allow 1 hour and 30 minutes for a full tour of the park.

Manufacturing starts from the raw material, cotton.
You can see our commitment in action.




We take pride in such as
Aiming for “pure made in Japan production,” we make our unique fabrics from home-grown Japanese cotton by using traditional Japanese dyeing and weaving techniques. We aim to create fabrics and products with a texture and atmosphere unique in SAMURAI JEANS.




※ For a limited time only, a “SAKURA” pink dye color is also available.
highlights: Organic cultivation of ancient Japanese varieties!
It's an intellectual education! Souvenirs are also included to make memories with your children!
During harvest time, the cotton that covers the entire field is a sight to behold!
Free design dyeing using local specialty black beans and Tanba chestnuts!
Experience and observe machines that you would not normally touch or see!
important_info:
Conditions for participation: Elementary school students and older
Maximum of 4 students per experience slot (approx. 2 hours)
Cancellations must be made by 6:00 p.m. 2 days prior to the experience. After that time, the default cancellation fee will apply.

The cotton ginning experience uses a machine. Visitors with long fingernails or nail polish may get scratched or peeled off by the machine. The machine is designed to rotate, so we may ask you to remove long earrings, necklaces, and scarves, and to tie up long hair to prevent it from getting caught in the machine.
The dyeing experience involves the use of dyes. Aprons, etc. are available for rent (free of charge), but we recommend that you wear clothes that you do not mind getting dirty, as there is a possibility that dye may get on your clothes. After dyeing, each item will be dried using a dryer, etc., and then the names will be sewn on. In that case, our staff may attach the name to the item and ship it to you later.
Sewing machines are used in the sewing experience. This is a dangerous experience, so our staff will accompany you and help you with the sewing. If you have any concerns, please do not hesitate to ask our staff to help you.

Please be aware that our staff may ask you to leave the venue for safety reasons.
Please refrain from any activities that may disturb the neighborhood.
We will not be responsible for any problems that may occur at the parking lot during your visit or after you leave the venue.
description:
At the facility, in addition to experiencing “cotton ginning”, “weaving”, ‘dyeing’, and “sewing”, visitors can observe the fields and yarn spinning using a principle invented in the early Meiji era.
No preparation is required! You can experience it on a whim without any preparation.




[Start time] 10:00, 13:00, 16:00
[Duration] Approx. 2 to 2 hours and 20 minutes




[Flow of the experience]
Cotton ginning (20-30 minutes) --- Experiencing to separate cotton into seeds and fibers using a special machine. Mini-cushion included!
　↓
Weaving (approx. 10 min.) --- Hand weaving experience using a loom.
　↓
Dyeing (80-90 min.) --- Dyeing experience using locally produced black beans and Tamba chestnuts as dyes, with a choice of items to be dyed from a course of your choice.
　↓
Sewing (approx. 10 minutes) --- Experience sewing the facility's original name on the dyed items using a sewing machine (*Staff can also sew on behalf of the customer).




*You can chose a course from A or B.
　Course A: Bandana or socks
　Course B: Tote bag or T-shirt
*When making a reservation, please inform us of the course (A or B), the item to be dyed (bandana, socks, tote bag, T-shirt), and the dyeing color (black bean or chestnut) in advance.
*For a limited time only, a “SAKURA" pink dye color is also available.




[Visiting Tours]
*Please contact us separately for a tour and experience of the fields.
Fields --- You can also visit our organically grown cotton fields. If you wish, you can also experience it for yourself! (free of charge).
*The content of the tour/experience will change depending on the time of year.
Yarn Spinning --- See the special machines (carding machine and gara-spinning). Simple operation is also available.

title: Miyagi Okura River Valley SUP experience One-day tour
overview:
The Okura River Valley SUP experience will be held at the Okura Dam, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture!




Please enjoy the Okura River Valley to the fullest!




Anyone is welcome to join, from one person to friends, family and couples!
highlights: Beginners are welcome! Instructors will guide you carefully and thoroughly.
Instructors and staff will take photos of you and give you the data!
Enjoy the great outdoors!
important_info:
・Age for participation　Elementary school children - 65 years old.
・Please arrive at the meeting point 10 minutes before the reserved time with your clothes changed.
・Please inform us in advance if you have any pre-existing medical conditions or are taking any medication.
・Pregnant women are not permitted.
・Please do not participate if you are not feeling well or have been drinking or have had a drink.
・Wetsuits are available to rent (at an additional fee). Please let us know if you wish to use a wetsuit.
・If we judge it to be dangerous due to weather conditions, etc., it may be changed or cancelled regardless of whether you experience it beforehand or during the experience.
・Please participate on the assumption that you will fall into the water because it is an activity on the lake.
・Please be aware that the staff will be watching you at all times.
description:
Experienced and beginners alike are welcome to join, as they will be accompanied by a SIJ (SUP Instructors of Japan) certified instructors and SUPA (Stand Up Paddleboard Association) certified instructors.




[POINT 1] Beginners welcome! Enjoy the great outdoors!
Only approx. 35 minutes by car from Sendai city towards Mt Jyoge.
Okura Dam is a rare double-arch concrete dam in Japan that plays an important role as a water reservoir for the city of Sendai.




During the event hold is the best season, when there is plenty of water storage! Enjoy the submerged forests, waterfalls and natural formations to your heart's content on a SUP as you head upstream through the Okura River Valley!




For beginners, instructors will carefully guide you through the basics, such as where to stand on the board and how to hold the paddle.




[POINT 2] Rest stop possible at Okura Furusato Centre!
The meeting point is the Okura Furusato Centre car park.




This facility was established by the city of Sendai to provide a place to nurture a rich humanity through 'nature experiences' and 'life experiences' that make use of the rich nature and history of life in the Okura area.




There is also an open space and camping area, which you are welcome to use in conjunction with the SUP experience! (Separate application required).




Free rest is available in an old house estimated to have been built in the early to mid-19th century.
Pay shower facilities are also available for a fee in the centre's administration building, which you can use after your SUP experience.




[POINT 3] We will provide photo data of the experience.
We will provide you with photographic data of your experience.
'I don't feel comfortable taking photos on the board' or 'I can't take my camera with me as it would get wet'
You don't need to worry about this! Our instructors and staff will take good photos of you!

title: Make a Japanese Hunting Knife or Kitchen Knife at the Seki City Knife Museum in Gifu Prefecture
overview: Visit the Seki City Knife Museum in Gifu, and the factory of one of the country's leading knife makers, G. Sakai. Experience making your very own Damascus Japanese hunting knife, or kitchen knife, and write your name on it!
highlights: Visit the modern factory of leading Japanese knife manufacturer G. Sakai
Write your name or a pattern of your choice on the surface of the knife
Make your own Damascus hunting or kitchen knife from assembly to polishing
See the unique Knife Museum in Seki City, Japan's center of cutlery manufacturing
important_info:
• Participants must be aged 12 or older
• Please note this experience is conducted mainly in Japanese; the host also welcomes overseas guests using simple English, and non-verbal communication
description:
Seki City in Gifu Prefecture developed as a major center for sword making as early as the 13th century thanks to rich deposits of iron sand in the local Nagaragawa River. During the Edo period, there were several hundred blacksmiths producing a large number of samurai swords and knives. Today, Seki is considered one of the world's three best places for cutlery manufacturing, together with Sheffield, UK, and Solingen, Germany.




G. Sakai is a leading Japanese knife producer with trade partners all over the world. The variety of their products and their high-quality knives have been acknowledged by many customers around the globe, in fact they exhibited at the Milan trade fair 'Salone del Mobile.Milano' in 2017—the global benchmark event for the furnishing and design sector.




G. Sakai's knives are now used in various professional fields, such as cookery, hunting, outdoor sports, and even military training.




In this activity, you will visit the Knife Museum, situated in an attractive Canadian-style log house just next to G. Sakai's factory. There you will see interesting rare items and cutlery exhibits from all over the world. Next, you will enter the factory and see how quality Japanese knives are made nowadays. Finally, you will construct your own knife in the atelier by assembling, polishing, sharpening and writing your name on it!




Please choose between the following four options:
1: Make a Damascus Hunting Knife "Kimun Kamuy II" (whose shape resembles a bear fang and can be used even for bear hunting)
2: Make a Damascus Kitchen Knife for practical use
3: Make a Damascus New Folding Hunter
4. Make a Damascus Chinese Kitchen Knife

overview:
Book an incredible Japanese knife-making experience in Gifu with expert guidance from master samurai swordsmiths!




Make your own customized Japanese kitchen knife at a real forge, to take home and treasure forever. Includes English support, with pick-up and drop-off from the nearest train station.




We also offer these in-depth bladesmithing experiences:
• Real Samurai Sword Making Full-Day Experience
• Samurai Kozuka Knife Making Experience
• Sword Forge Tour and "Otsuchi" Hammering Experience
highlights: English-speaking staff support you at each step

Learn to make a Japanese kitchen knife using traditional techniques

important_info:

Children must be accompanied by an adult

Cautions



This experience takes place at a real forge: please follow the instructions of staff at all times
Sparks and charcoal dust may fly within the forge: please wear clothes that are easy to move in, which you don't mind getting dirty or damaged
We assume no liability for any injury or damage to belongs

description:
Join us for an immersive workshop where you'll learn to craft a traditional Japanese kitchen knife under the guidance of a master swordsmith. Using the same high-quality "tamahagane" steel as Japanese swords, you'll shape the blade with a hammer, apply clay for the unique blade pattern, and finish by sharpening and engraving your name on the knife.




This hands-on experience is both challenging and rewarding, but don't worry — our expert swordsmith will assist you throughout the process, making it accessible even for those who may not be confident in their strength. Feel free to capture the entire process with photos and videos to remember your unique creation.




The workshop fee covers the creation of one knife, but multiple participants can work together on a single knife if desired. For groups making three or more knives, please allow extra time or consider an earlier start. We also offer a convenient pick-up service from JR Mino Ota Station to make your visit hassle-free.

Canoe tour On the day Please note that cancellations cannot be refunded. * Depending on the reservation status, On the day You can pay with. However, canoe tour On the day In case of cancellation, you may be charged the full amount. Please understand.
description:
Soyokyo Nakayama Dam is located in the upper reaches of the Gokase River, Japan's cleanest river. It is part of the Aso Geopark. You can enjoy it according to the season from spring to autumn. The meeting place is the former Soyo Town Hall, now the Soyokyo General Branch Office. It is about 30 minutes from Minamiaso and 40 minutes from Takachiho. The boarding point is a little difficult to drive because it is a narrow road that winds down, so we will travel by our company's car. Morning departures are at 9:30 and afternoon departures are at 13:00. There is almost no current, so it is perfect for first-time canoeing experience. We set out from the edge of the dam lake and head for the waterfall downstream. We take photos with the spray of the waterfall and the pleasant breeze of negative ions right in front of us! There we take a drink break while still in the canoe (one drink per person). From there, we go back upstream and go further upstream than the boarding point! It is said that Aso was formed 90,000 years ago, and in that time it has become full of power spots, such as the rock surfaces carved by the river, trees that stand embracing large stones, and an unnamed waterfall that is stepped but about 80m high. Experience the charm of Soyo Gorge, a hidden area in the southeast of the outer rim of Mt. Aso, upstream of Takachiho Gorge, the village of the gods, through a canoe.

title: Kyoto Gion Matsuri Festival Reserved Seat Ticket — July 17 & 24 ONLY!
overview:
Witness the captivating Kyoto Gion Festival, a vibrant celebration of Japanese tradition. Marvel at colorful floats, traditional music, and mesmerizing performances.




Reserve seating tickets before they sell out!
highlights: Enjoy the spectacular Yamaboko Junko procession from the secure comfort of your designated, paid seating area
important_info:
• This is a physical paper ticket, not an e-ticket.
• On the day of the festival, please come to the designated ticket pickup location to receive your ticket directly from our staff during the pickup time window.



About Ticket Pickup



• From the TKP Kyoto Shijo Conference Center (ticket pickup location) to the Gion viewing seats, it takes one train stop or approximately 15–20 minutes on foot. Please allow sufficient time and arrive early to pick up your tickets on the day of the event.
• If you are unable to pick up your ticket during the designated time, we will be unable to issue a refund.
• We will provide you with specific reception hours individually at a later date.



Notes for the Day



1: We expect extreme heat, so please take care to stay hydrated and in good physical condition. Even if you are not thirsty, please drink water frequently. Please take precautions against the heat, such as bringing a hat (parasols are not permitted).
2: We recommend that you bring a raincoat in case of rain (umbrellas are not permitted).
3: Please be aware that the restroom facilities are limited and may not be sufficient.
4: The use of tripods is prohibited in the bleacher area. Photography and videography are not permitted.
5: Persons who behave in a manner that disturbs other patrons inside or outside the venue and who do not follow the instructions of the staff will be forcibly asked to leave the venue. Tickets will not be refunded in such cases.
6: The event may be subject to change or cancellation due to the effects of new coronavirus infections, etc.
7: Please act in accordance with the policies of the national and local governments regarding infection control measures.
8: The organizers will not be liable for any damage caused inside the bleachers.

description:
Experience the Iconic Yamaboko Junko float procession of the Gion Matsuri!




Witness one of Japan’s most legendary festivals, the Gion Matsuri Yamaboko Float procession, from a reserved seat with excellent visibility.




This centuries-old tradition is held twice, only on these specific days:
• Saki Matsuri (featuring 23 Yamaboko): July 17
• Ato Matsuri (featuring 10 Yamaboko): July 24




Both processions showcase magnificent floats, traditional music, and Kyoto’s rich cultural heritage. With a reserved seat, you can relax and enjoy unobstructed views — no need to worry about crowds or long hours of standing.



Time & Location



Saki Matsuri: Jul 17 10:20 AM – 11:20 AM
Ato Matsuri: Jul 24 9:30 AM – 9:50 AM

description:
This tour's greatest feature is that it combines two programs: kayaking and nature experiences. Kayaking offers stunning panoramic views of Hokkaido's most iconic landmarks, including Mount Yotei and Mount Usu, from Lake Toya. There are also plenty of breathtaking viewpoints, such as the crystal-clear blue lakebed and forest tunnels. The nature experiences include seasonal activities like searching for creatures, playing in the water, and exploring the forest. There's even a relaxing tea time while enjoying the view of Lake Toya. This tour is based on the shores of Lake Toya and is run by guides who have a deep understanding of the local nature and climate. Friendly service and a safety-first approach ensure that even beginners and children can enjoy the tour with peace of mind. A photo taken by the guide is provided as a gift. Clothing rental services (at a low cost) are available, so you can easily participate even without bringing anything. Easily accessible from Toyako Onsen (about 5 minutes), the tour takes place on the eastern shore of Lake Toya, an area particularly rich in natural beauty. Enjoy the clarity and quality of the water, as well as the beautiful scenery of Mount Usu, Showa Shinzan, and Mount Yotei. This tour is designed to be enjoyable for families, groups, couples, and anyone else. [Guide] Fumiaki Sakai [Qualifications] Hokkaido Governor Certified Outdoor Guide (Canoeing & Mountaineering), Lake Toya & Usu Volcano Master, WAFA Wilderness Medical First Aid 40-hour Completion *English guiding available

description:
After paddling across the sea, you'll arrive at a place so beautiful it's truly like paradise, leaving you speechless. Sea kayaking is highly recommended for a trip to Uni Beach, where you can feel at one with nature, experience a sense of accomplishment and wonder. You'll pass under the Irabu Bridge, admire the magnificent scenery, and head towards the less crowded edge of Uni Beach! It's full of adventure! If you're lucky, you might even see sea turtles surfacing for air or schools of small fish leaping by! After landing, you can take photos, stroll around, or enjoy the breathtaking scenery with a drone. The drone footage will be taken by a nationally certified guide, so we'll give you a video that will become a lifelong memory that can be used for anniversaries and events. There's a sense of accomplishment that only human-powered kayaking can provide, as well as the refreshing feeling of moving your body in nature, and you can enjoy the beautiful sea even more because you're so close to nature! Be sure to go to Uni Beach by sea kayak and enjoy the legendary beach of Uni Beach! Note) We will send you details of the tour via Activity Japan or by text message to the phone number provided by the day before, so please be sure to check it. The start time may change depending on the tide level, so please check our information rather than the information automatically sent via this site the day before. If you do not receive a message, please contact Jomon Tribes Miyakojima by phone. ★If you are not confident in your physical fitness, we may refuse your participation, so please consult with us. Our guides are well-trained, so you can rest assured about safety♪ ★If you are bringing a child between 0 and 2 years old, please be sure to let us know. If you do not let us know, they will not be able to participate.

title: JR 5-Day Kansai-Hiroshima Area Pass [E-Ticket]
overview:
Get the JR 5-Day Kansai-Hiroshima Area Pass by JR West for five consecutive days of unlimited travel in the Kansai Region and along the Sanyo Coast all the way to Hiroshima! This pass is available exclusively for overseas tourists, and is ideal for those visiting Osaka and planning a side trip to Hiroshima.




Ride the Sanyo Shinkansen Line with a JR pass from Rakuten Travel Experiences, and earn points for every booking!
highlights: A great way to explore the Kansai region and the Sanyo Coast up to Hiroshima!
Valid for 5 consecutive days after the first use
Available exclusively for non-Japanese passport holders — overseas tourists only
important_info:
• All ticket activities are non-refundable once the payment is successful or in review status
• If the venue is closed due to unexpected circumstances, we will process a refund for your booking
• Please note that there is no exchange office in the Kanto area (including Tokyo)
• Please be aware that luggage space is sometimes limited on busy trains
• On some busy journeys (free) seat reservations may be required, but they are not usually needed. Consider reserving seats if traveling during national holidays, or with a large group to be seated together. Green Car seats and certain special trains require a seat reservation. Note that an extra payment is required to board a Green Car
• A few Shinkansen trains have smoking carriages, and some seating may be near to separate smoking rooms. For a guaranteed smoke-free ride, a seat reservation is recommended
Tickets for Young Children:
Infants (ages 1–5) and babies (younger) travel for free on JR trains except in the following cases, when a child ticket is required:
1: When 3 or more infants accompany 1 adult or child (an Adult or Child rail pass holder can bring up to 2 infants for free)
2: When an infant or baby occupies a reserved seat, Green Car seat or berth, etc.
3: When an infant travels alone
Conditions for Use:
1: Valid only for the person whose name appears on the pass – must be a foreign national with a non-Japanese passport¹
2: Use the physical JR pass to pass through the automated ticket gates
3: You must carry your passport at all times while using the pass, and be prepared to present it to conductors or station attendants if requested.
4: Valid for use on: Sanyo Shinkansen (Mihara–Hakata) and Express Trains (maximum 6 redemptions for reserved seats and unlimited redemptions for non-reserved seats), Special Rapid Services, Rapid Services, Local trains on JR-WEST Conventional lines, JR West Miyajima Ferry, and Chugoku JR buses within the valid area
- Not valid on JR Group expressway buses, or certain local bus routes
5: The pass itself doesn't ensure specific seating options – to obtain a reserved-seat ticket at no additional cost, present your pass in the valid area. Certain trains require a reserved-seat ticket. Additional fares apply to travel in Green Cars. An extra charge must be paid in advance to ride local trains requiring a numbered ticket or liner ticket.
6: The pass can only be used within its validity period, which cannot be changed
7: The pass cannot be exchanged for other kinds of pass or ticket
8: The pass cannot be reissued in the event of loss or theft
9: Exchange Orders can only be refunded by the travel agency from which you purchase them, and any application for a refund must be made before the start of the Kansai-Hiroshima Area Pass validity period. Any refund will be for the value of the pass in JPY, less a 10% handling fee
10: Once you have started using the pass the validity period cannot be extended, nor can any refund be given, even if trains or other transportation services are suspended or delayed for any reason. The JR Group will not bear additional costs due to changes in your route, such as alternative transportation or accommodation charges
11: In addition to these conditions, you must obey the JR Group's regulations, terms and conditions, and the laws of Japan while using the pass
12: Violation of these conditions may result in the invalidation and confiscation of the pass, as well as a penalty equal to twice the basic fares and charges for all transportation for which the pass was used
13: The official terms and conditions of the pass are written in Japanese, and the text of the Japanese version shall be given priority of interpretation, in the event of questions regarding transportation conditions that may arise regarding conditions that are written in other languages
¹Japanese nationals who have lived overseas for 10 years or more may use the pass under certain conditions. You can read more here: https://rakuten-travelexp.net/jr-pass-for-jp-expats
The details above are current as of April 2023 and subject to change. Please confirm the latest terms and conditions with a JR attendant.

description:
Exclusively for use by foreign tourists, the Kansai-Hiroshima Area Pass is a transportation pass that allows five consecutive days of unlimited travel on designated JR train and bus lines (including shinkansen and limited express trains) in the Kansai Region and along the Sanyo Coast up to Hiroshima.




The Kansai-Hiroshima Area Pass is valid for unlimited travel on these trains and buses:
• Non-reserved seats on Sanyo Shinkansen (Shin-Osaka–Hiroshima)*
(The pass can be used for the Hello Kitty Shinkansen)
• Non-reserved seats on Express Trains (Haruka, Kuroshio, Thunderbird, Kounotori, Super Hakuto (Kyoto–Kamigori), etc.)*
*A separate fare is required for traveling on Super Hakuto (Kamigori–Tottori) and Super Inaba (Kamigori–Tottori)
• Special Rapid Services, Rapid Services, and Local trains on JR-WEST Conventional lines
• JR West Miyajima Ferry (Miyajimaguchi–Miyajima)




• The pass is only valid on WEST JAPAN JR BUS route buses within the valid area. The pass cannot be used on expressway buses.




(WEST JAPAN JR bus routes - For more information, please click the link: https://www.nishinihonjrbus.co.jp/route/)
(Chugoku JR bus routes - For more information, please click the link: https://www.chugoku-jrbus.co.jp.e.aec.hp.transer.com/)




*Seats may be reserved for free for up to 6 times; for the 7th time onward, separate tickets will be required




The pass cannot be used for these trains:
• Sanyo Shinkansen (Hiroshima–Hakata)
• Tokaido Shinkansen (Shin-Osaka–Tokyo)
• Kyushu Shinkansen (Hakata–Kagoshima-Chuo)
• The Special Rapid "A Seat" paid seating service




Discover the beauty of Hiroshima and the Kansai region with the Kansai-Hiroshima Area Pass. Book now!
how_it_works:
How to Receive Your JR Pass
1: Choose the date you want to redeem the JR Pass and book. A lead guest may book on behalf of a group, but note that all guests must be foreign nationals with non-Japanese passports, with a valid 'Temporary Visitor' entry status stamp
2: Receive your QR code e-voucher with your confirmation email
3: Present your QR code e-voucher, printed or displayed on your smartphone, at the green ticket-vending machine at a valid exchange location, and pick up physical ticket by scanning the 2D barcode code of your e-ticket and non-Japanese passport. You can redeem it up to 90 days from the issue date of your e-voucher. See pickup locations here




How to Use Your JR Pass
1: Your JR pass can be used for 5 consecutive days and must be redeemed within 90 days after the e-voucher is issued
2: The usage period must be within one month of exchanging your e-voucher for the physical pass
3: You need to specify the date that you want to start using the pass at the exchange office




• The usage period begins when you use your physical pass to pass through the automated ticket gates

title: [WEB Ticket] SUNQ Pass - an unlimited bus ticket for Kyushu
overview: Get a Kyushu SUNQ Pass to enjoy unlimited rides on almost all buses and ferries in Kyushu! Plus, the Shimanoseki and Nagato areas of Yamaguchi Prefecture. This "free-pass" style Kyushu Sunq bus ticket covers about 2,400 routes, including highway buses, so you can roam free around the island with just one ticket!
highlights: Go anywhere in Kyushu as long as buses are running
Ride as much as you like for 3 or 4 days
Also gives benefits at partner stores in the coverage area
No need to handle coins — just show the driver your SUNQ Pass and away you go!
important_info:
.
An environment with communication capabilities is required both when you start using the service and when displaying the ticket screen.
.
If the clock on the ticket screen is not running, the ticket is considered invalid.
.
If multiple people are using the service, tapping “Use” for one ticket will mark multiple tickets as “In Use” at the same time.
.
If multiple people are using one screen, boarding and disembarking within the same section is only valid if the ticket holder and the other passengers are traveling together.
.
If you use your first ticket within the expiration date (within 3 months), the remaining tickets will be valid for 3 days from the date of first use.
.
Activation can only be done once per day.
If you press the “Activate” button between 00:00 and 02:59, the ticket will only be valid until 02:59 on the same day. Furthermore, no other tickets can be activated until 23:59 on the same day.
Examples:
・Activation at 00:30 on August 21 → Valid until 02:59 on August 21
・Other tickets cannot be activated until 23:59 on August 21
・Activation is possible again from 00:00 on August 22
.
The validity period of the ticket after it has been used extends until 2:59 a.m. the day after the customer taps the “Start Use” button. If the ticket is used between 12:00 a.m. and 2:59 a.m., it will be considered to have been used on the previous calendar day.
.
Buses that accept the SUNQ Pass display “SUNQ Pass” stickers on the front and next to the boarding entrance. Additionally, boats that accept the SUNQ Pass have stickers at the ticket sales counter and in the waiting area. (*Please note that some vehicles may not have these stickers. In that case, feel free to ask the crew for assistance.)
.
When using the ferry, please present your bus ticket that is valid within the applicable period at the ticket counter to receive your boarding pass. Then, hand it over to the attendant when boarding the ferry.
.
When boarding a route bus, please be sure to take a numbered ticket. When getting off, clearly show the ticket to the bus driver.
.
Please do not print the voucher. Instead, save the PDF file to your smartphone for easy access.
.
Show the voucher screen on your smartphone when you arrive at the counter. If the voucher cannot be displayed, we will be unable to assist you with boarding.
.
If you are taking an express bus, please arrive at the counter at least 30 minutes before departure to allow time for processing. Arriving just before departure may result in not being able to board your desired bus.
.
There will be no refunds or extensions to validity dates due to the suspension or delay of bus or ferry services. Additionally, no alternative services will be provided.
.
Once even one ticket has been used, no refunds will be issued.
.
Refunds can only be processed before 18:00 on the day before to the start date. A cancellation fee of 110 yen per ticket will apply.
.
As of July 1, 2025, the SUNQ Pass will no longer be valid for express bus services between Fukuoka and Nagato Yumoto Onsen. For travel on this route, please make a separate reservation and purchase a regular fare ticket. We apologize for any inconvenience this may cause.
.
Nagato can also be accessed via a regular Sanden Kotsu route bus from Shimonoseki.
description:
The SUNQ Pass is an unlimited travel ticket that offers free access to almost all express buses and local buses throughout Kyushu, as well as in Shimonoseki and Nagato cities in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and on certain ferries. With access to approximately 2,400 bus routes, this pass allows for extensive travel across Kyushu with just one ticket.




*Note: From July 1, 2025, the SUNQ Pass will no longer be accepted for the express bus service between Fukuoka and Nagato Yumoto Onsen.
Until June 30, 2025, the SUNQ Pass may still be used as usual, with prior reservation.

description:
★☆ Plan Features ☆★★ Experience the great outdoors through "play and relaxation"♪ Relax in the popular, breathtaking location of Nagura Bay! ★ Enjoy a 360-degree view of the island, sky, sea, and mountains♪★ Same-day reservations accepted until 3:00 PM! ★ Free tour photos and equipment♪★ Beginners welcome! From solo travelers to large groups★ Accident-free and fully insured since our founding★☆ Plan Details ☆★ Relax with a dynamic sunset and starry sky on this kayak tour. Conveniently located about 20 minutes by car from downtown, Nagura Bay offers a spectacular view of the sky and sea interweaving. Why not immerse yourself in the majestic scenery that can only be found on Ishigaki Island? As you paddle out of your kayak, a sunset-tinged sky and twinkling stars unfold before your eyes. A magnificent panorama unique to Ishigaki Island awaits, where the island, sky, sea, and mountains join together. We offer the best itinerary based on the day's weather and sea conditions. The natural illuminations, which change with the passing of time, are breathtakingly magical. As the sky turns dark, a starry sky emerges amid the sounds and scents of the waves. Enjoy a moment with a shooting star...and the sounds of the waves echoing along the shore and the cries of the Ruddy Kingfisher. Depending on the season, bioluminescent plankton glow faintly in the ocean, creating a nighttime atmosphere completely different from daytime. Enjoy a "rewarding time" that soothes both body and mind, amid the slow-paced pace of island life. ∴‥∵‥∴‥∵‥∴‥∴‥Beginners Welcome∵‥∴‥∵‥∴‥∴‥∵‥∴‥∴‥∵‥∴ Half of our tour participants are inexperienced. We provide thorough instruction on how to use the equipment used in each tour, and then guide you safely. With a focus on safety, we primarily operate within the bay, where the waves are relatively calm, so you can join us with peace of mind. Furthermore, since our founding, we have been accident-free and violation-free, and are fully insured for your peace of mind. ∴‥∵‥∴‥∵‥∴‥∴‥∵‥∴‥∴‥∵‥∴‥∴‥∵‥∴‥∴‥∵‥(Meeting place: Ishigaki no Shio *Parking available Address: 1145-57 Shinkawa, Ishigaki City) ∴‥∵‥∴‥∵‥∴‥∴‥∵‥∴‥∴‥∵‥∴‥∴‥∵‥∴‥∴‥∵‥∴‥∴‥∵‥

title: Book a Shinjuku Bar-Hopping Nightlife Tour in Tokyo
overview: Book a Tokyo bar hopping tour around Shinjuku’s nightlife hubs: Kabukicho, Omoide Yokocho, and Shinjuku Sanchome! Navigate hidden alleys, savor local street food, and enjoy vibrant izakaya bars with friendly locals. Experience Tokoy nightlife like a local and relish an authentic bar crawl adventure!
highlights: Discover the hidden bars of Shinjuku normally hard for tourists to find
Hop between 3 local bars and pubs with a friendly local food tour guide
Savor drinks and street food recommended by locals
An all-inclusive bar-hopping tour you can enjoy empty-handed, with food & drinks included
Reserve with a Rakuten account to earn or use points for each booking!
important_info:
The legal drinking age in Japan is age 20; please bring a valid ID for proof of age (alcohol will not be served to ages 0–19)
Participants must be aged 15+
Please note this is shared tour
Please arrive early at the meeting point; the tour will depart on time and latecomers will not be refunded due to missing the tour
Vegetarian options are available, but please note vegetarianism is uncommon in Japan and choices are limited
Vegan and gluten-free menu is not available
The guide will pay for their own food and drinks
Please tell us any dietary requirements during booking so we can do our best to prepare for you
Some stops may allow indoor smoking, and we may be unable to adjust the itinerary around this
Note that the restaurant industry still faces post-pandemic staff shortages, which may lead to delays serving food and drinks, and cause the tour to be extended; thank you for understanding
meeting_point: In front of the black pillar next to Uniqlo Shinjuku Nishiguchi shop (map)
schedule:
Tours are offered at 17:00, 18:00 and 19:00 daily and last for 3.5 hours



Meet at Uniqlo Nishiguchi Shinjuku Shop
Omoide Yokocho Alley (1 bar)
Kabukicho (1 bar)
Another bar in the Shinjuku area
Return to Shinjuku Station (stay at the last bar if you prefer)
cancellation:
No-show: You pay 100% of experience price
2 days before: You pay 100% of experience price
description:
Begin your Tokyo nightlife experience in the bustling district of Shinjuku, where your knowledgeable guide will lead you through vibrant food alleys lined with traditional izakaya bars. The evening starts at small bars where you can indulge in popular local delights such as yakitori (grilled chicken skewers), fried tofu, gyoza (dumplings), and grilled vegetables, all while sipping on local beer or Japanese sake selected specifically for this tour.




As the night progresses, we'll explore more of Shinjuku's hidden gems in Kabukicho and Shinjuku 3-chome areas. These spots are popular with local office workers and can be hard to access without reservations. Fear not — reservations are made in advance to ensure you have the perfect spot to continue your culinary journey. This all-in-one tour includes all your food and drink, so you can arrive empty-handed and leave full and happy!




Immerse yourself deeper in Tokyo's drinking culture as we visit additional izakayas, engaging in local games, quizzes, and meaningful interactions with Tokyoites. This part of the tour isn't just about food and drink — it's about connecting cultures, breaking down barriers, and experiencing the city's nightlife through the eyes of its residents.




On this tour, not only will you taste exquisite yakitori, gyoza, and other local specialties, but you'll also enjoy special combos of food and drinks that represent the best of Tokyo's culinary scene. With over 30 types of food and drink to choose from, including vegetarian options, everyone can tailor their experience to their taste. Join us for a memorable evening where you can socialize with locals, enjoy a plethora of dishes and drinks at a special price, and truly absorb the authentic atmosphere of Tokyo's nightlife. This small-group tour promises an intimate and friendly experience, perfect for solo travelers, couples, and families alike. Let's make unforgettable memories together in Tokyo!

title: Hakata–Hiroshima Sanyo Shinkansen Flexible Bullet Train Ticket[E-Ticket]
overview:
Book now for flexible Sanyo Shinkansen tickets between Hakata and Hiroshima — at a special price only for visitors to Japan!




Exclusive to those with temporary visitor status, take the Sanyo Shinkansen for less! This one-way ticket is valid up to 90 days after booking. Ride the Sanyo Shinkansen between Hakata and Hiroshima for a super-smooth high-speed journey and a chance to ride the Hello Kitty Shinkansen!







• For other Sanyo Shinkansen routes click here
• For other bullet train routes and prices, see our check our Japan Shinkansen ticket page
• See the How it Works section for full details of ticket usage!
highlights: Book with a Rakuten account to earn points for every ticket!
Get Sanyo Shinkansen One-Way Ticket tickets at exclusive rates!
Exchange for reserved-seat ticket at no extra charge!
Flexible tickets valid up to 90 days after booking!
important_info:
How to Use:
1: Choose the option for your direction of travel
2: Confirmation email will be sent to the appointed email address once the booking is made
3: The QR Code* voucher for your ticket is valid up to 90 days later
4: Please go to green ticket-vending machines and pick up physical ticket by scanning QR Code of e-ticket and non-Japanese passport (click here to search)
4: Use the physical ticket to pass through the automated ticket gate




Reserving a Reserved Seat Ticket
• Reserved seat tickets can be reserved at a ticket vending machine or a station ticket office.
• Click here for information on reserving reserved seat tickets from a ticket vending machine.




Eligibility:
• "Discounted One-way Ticket" user must be holding a passport issued by a foreign government（外国での永住権を持っているお客様を含め、日本国籍のお客様は使用できません）
• "Discounted One-way Ticket" user must be entering in Japan as a "短期滞在" (Temporary Visitor)"
• "短期滞在" (Temporary Visitor) is a status of residence defined under Japanese immigration law
• Individual customers are not allowed to purchase or exchange more than one of the same ticket for the same use period
• You must present your passport to receive a pass
description:
The Sanyo Shinkansen is a high-speed bullet train line in West Japan that runs between Hakata in Kyushu and Shin-Osaka in Kansai. The Shinkansen network is world-famous, offering fantastic speed and superb comfort. Plus, if you ride the Kodama service No. 842 or 849, you can expect a cute and fun Hello Kitty experience!




Ideal for long-distance journeys, the Shinkansen eats up the miles for easy inter-city travel across Japan. With Hakata-to-Hiroshima bullet train tickets (or Hiroshima-to-Hakata bullet train tickets), you’ll cruise along the Sanyo Shinkansen at incredible speed on a ride like no other.




If your itinerary covers Hakata and Hiroshima, book now so nothing gets in the way of your perfect Japan adventure!




Book your flexible tickets now, use the QR Code* to collect them in Japan, and make a seat reservation at the station in Japan, so nothing gets in the way of your bullet train adventure!




*QR Code is a registered trademark of Denso Wave, Inc.
how_it_works:
This ticket can be used on a variety of services. See the table below for the validity, and any other tickets needed.






Purchasing a "Discounted One-way Ticket"



*Available from select overseas travel agencies.
Purchasing Exchange Order through a Travel Agency outside Japan




Step 1: Purchase an exchange order (E-TICKET) at sales locations outside Japan
• Once issued, the E-TICKET must be exchanged within 3 months




Step 2: Exchange the E-TICKET for a "Discounted One-way Ticket" at an exchange location in Japan
• E-TICKET exchange locations: Click here for details
• Receiving an E-TICKET: Click here for details
• The E-TICKET that could not been exchanged can be refunded at the overseas travel agency where the pass was originally purchased (a separate handling fee will be applied)
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is not available for purchase within Japan



Availability and Use



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is available and usable all year around



Changes



• Changing the validity period start date is allowed only once
• The ticket cannot be changed for a different ticket type; the ticket must instead be refunded, and the desired ticket purchased separately
• Changing the departure or arrival station within a section is allowed only once for unused tickets within the validity period
• Changing trains is allowed only once before the departure of the designated train, and only if tickets for the train being changed to are available within the validity period



Refunds



• Refunds are accepted only at the "Discounted One-way Ticket" exchange location.
• Click here for information on refund locations for refunds due to non-availability of services, delays, or other circumstances
• The sales price minus a handling fee will be refunded.




Handling fee:
If no reserved seating ticket has been issued: 560 YEN
If a reserved seating ticket has been issued:
• Up to 2 days before departure: 560 YEN
• Between day before departure and time of departure: 30% of original undiscounted Express ticket (based on original ticket purchase date) + 220 YEN (minimum handling fee: 560 YEN)




• A handling fee equivalent to the Limited express ticket for each train will be applied to a reserved seating ticket after departure time.
• Refunds cannot be provided if the "Discounted One-way Ticket" has been used or the validity period has ended.
• A refund of an unexchanged E-TICKET is available from the overseas travel agency where the ticket was purchased. Please note that an additional handling fee will apply.



Related information



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is only valid for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" holder
• Presentation of a passport (of the person using the "Discounted One-way Ticket") is required to exchange an E-TICKET
• No more than one E-TICKET can be exchanged per passport
• A passport is required when using a "Discounted One-way Ticket" and must be presented to the JR staff upon request
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" will not be reissued even if lost or stolen
• The conditions of use for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" are stipulated by the JR-WEST carriage conditions and the laws of Japan
• Conditions of use for an exchange order are stipulated by regulations and contracts of issuing companies
• If any discrepancies arise between the foreign language and Japanese versions of the carriage conditions, the Japanese version has precedence
• The information displayed (including prices) is current as of August 1, 2024, and is subject to change

title: Soka Senbei Garden Rice-Cracker Making Experience E-Ticket
overview:
Soka Senbei Garden, a rice cracker theme park where visitors can try making rice crackers, has opened in Soka City, Saitama Prefecture: the home of Soka Senbei.




The Soka Senbei Garden, which has been established in 1971, was built alongside the store of Yamako Senbei and making Soka Senbei. In addition to hand-baked rice crackers, visitors can enjoy beverages such as coffee and green tea, as well as light meals such as rice cracker donuts and rice balls. There is also a rare Soka Senbei soft-serve ice cream, so come and experience the possibilities of Soka Senbei!




Soka Senbei Garden received the award in the Building Landscape Category of the 16th Soka City Landscape Award. You can spend a relaxing time at the café surrounded by the gentle tasteful warmth of flowers and wood.

title: Kochi, Creative Park ACTLAND E-ticket for fee-charging exhibition (exchange ticket)
overview:
ACTLAND stands for Art, Culture, and Technology, three elements crucial for the development of humanity. By fostering an appreciation of the arts, refining culture, and advancing science and technology, we can cultivate sensitivity, enrich minds, and create a better society.




Comprised of eight museums, ACTLAND is home to a variety of artifacts and relics that can’t be found anywhere else. These include the Ryoma History Museum, World Heroes Museum, Nee-Owoo African Gallery, Ekin-ha Art Gallery, World Model Car Museum, World Classic Car Museum, Bonnetbus Museum, and Kuma's Container Gallery.




In addition to the museums, ACTLAND offers various experience zones where visitors can freely play, appreciate, or experiment with scientific equipment, rare toys, and more.
ACTLAND is a place for visitors to awaken their senses and fearlessly explore with the motto ""Let's experience!"" We welcome you to ACTLAND to share our passion for Art, Culture, and Technology!
highlights: Explore eight unique museums including Ryoma History Museum
Earn and use Rakuten Points whenever you purchase tickets with a Rakuten account!
Walk through the life of Ryoma, recreated with more than 120 wax figures
At the Ekin-ha Art Gallery enjoy powerful theatrical paintings by Ekin, a painter at the end of the Edo period
In the World Classic Car Museum, visitors can see 22 classic cars collected from Japan and overseas
important_info:

.

.

*Please check the annual calendar on the official website for details regarding the year-end and New Year's vacations, GW, and summer vacation periods.
.
Holders of a disability certificate ("MIRAIRO ID" must be presented) will receive a 20% discount on the admission fee.
Please present your ID and purchase your ticket in person at the site.
.
Please note that once you leave the museum, you will need another ticket to enter.
.
Please note that the opening days may be subject to change. Please check before coming to the venue as it will be announced on the official website.
.
These e-tickets (exchange tickets) are valid for 90 days from the date of purchase.
description:
"Single museum E-ticket (exchange ticket)" which allows admission to one of the seven fee-charging exhibition in ACTLAND (excludes the Ryoma History Museum), "Ryoma History Museum E-ticket (exchange ticket)" which allows admission to the Ryoma History Museum, and "【3-tickets】Admission E-ticket (exchange ticket)" which allows admission to a choice of three of the eight fee-charging museums.
Rakuten Travel Sightseeing Experience offers a 10% discount (Single Museum: Adults 900 yen, Ryoma Museum: Adults 1,350 yen) from the local sales price (Single Museum: Adults 1,000 yen, Ryoma Museum: Adults 1,500 yen) for the E-ticket (exchange ticket) for the "Single Museum" and "Ryoma History Museum".




<8 fee-charging exhibition muesums>
【Ryoma History Museum】
The "Ryoma Theater" recreates the 33 years of Ryoma Sakamoto's life, from his birth to the end of his life, in 27 carefully selected historical scenes and over 120 wax figures.




【Ekin-ha Art Gallery】
The Ekin-ha Art Gallery is an exhibition hall that features works by Ekin, who was immensely popular for his novel style of theatrical paintings and folding screens, as well as many other creative ways to enjoy the art of Ekin. The museum also exhibits the works of his disciples, who inherited his "Ekin" style.




【World Classic Car Museum】
The World Classic Car Museum exhibits 22 unique and fascinating classic cars collected from Japan and overseas, including the prototype of the Ford Model T, which laid the foundation of the automobile industry.




【The World Celebrity Museum】
The World Celebrity Museum features realistic wax figures and stage sets that recreate famous scenes from modern history that have only been depicted in textbooks. Visitors can look back on scenes from conferences that changed the course of world history and the heroic deeds of national heroes.




【Nee-Owoo African Gallery】
Nee-Owoo's works have a unique texture created by the unique motifs of African art and the metallic texture of metal. This is the only museum in the world that exhibits many of Nee-Owoo's unique works.




【KUMA'S Container Gallery】
This gallery displays a collection of works created over the years by Katsuyuki Shinohara, a.k.a. Kumasan of the Gagez family, all in one place. Produced under the concept of a "mobile gallery," it is housed in four containers.




【Bonnetbus Museum】
Bonnet buses with a nostalgic flavor are on display, including a British-made bonnet bus and a bonnet fire truck. Visitors can not only look at the vehicles from the outside, but also sit in the driver's seat or passenger seats to experience the actual riding experience.




【World Model Car Museum】
More than 3,700 1/43 model cars represent the "design," "engineering," and "development and evolution" of about 200 brands. Enjoy the history of the world's famous cars made possible by model cars.

title: Amazing Tokyo Tuna Auction – Toyosu & Tsukiji Outer Market Tour with Nationally Licensed Guide
overview:
See two of the most famous fish markets in the world — Toyosu and Tsukiji — and taste the freshest sushi Tokyo has to offer!




Enjoy an exciting tuna auction at Toyosu Market, and explore the historic Tsukiji Outer Market, with key insights from your nationally-licensed tour guide Tetsuya!
highlights: Visit Namiyoke Shrine, dedicated to all the people working at Tsukiji Market
Enjoy ultra-fresh sushi at restaurants around Toyosu Market and Tsukiji Outer Market (Foods are not included)
Explore a bustling Tsukiji Outer Market
A national- licensed, English-speaking guide will take you to a tuna auction in Toyosu Market
important_info:
Except for the Tuna Auction Observation Gallery on the 2nd floor, there is a “special lower observation deck” where you can watch the Tuna Auction at a closer distance.
If you'd like to observe from the special lower observation deck, you must make an online application at the first 10 days of the previous month, sometimes it gives or takes a few days though. 100 people will be chosen by lottery. Please book well in advance.
The maximum number of guests on this tour is 10. If you are in a small group, there is a chance you'll be paired with another group.
Hotel pick-up service is not provided.
We can delay the meeting time if you wish, but please note that you will not be able to observe the tuna auction, which is the highlight of the tour.
what_to_bring: Casual clothes and comfortable shoes are highly recommended. Sandals are not allowed inside the market.
meeting_point: Meet at Shijo-Mae Station on the Yurikamome Line
schedule:
05:40 — Meet at Shijo-Mae Station on the Yurikamome Line, then watch the tuna auction with your guide
06:20 — Explore the fruit and vegetable market and intermediate wholesale outlets
06:50 — Breakfast in the Toyosu Market area
07:30 — Explore the intermediate fish wholesale market in Toyosu Market
08:00 — Move to Tsukiji Outer Market
08:20 — Explore Tsukiji Outer Market
09:40 — Tour ends
cancellation:
No-show: You pay 100% of experience price
8 days before: You pay 100% of experience price
description:
The Tokyo Metropolitan Government opened Toyosu Market in 2018 as a replacement for Tsukiji Market. On this tour, you'll explore both markets and see what delicious delights they have to offer.




Tetsuya, a nationally-licensed tour guide, will show you the Tuna Auction Observation Gallery on the 2nd floor of Toyosu Market. From there, you'll see various registered buyers, wearing caps with their company's name and certified number card.




They inspect which tuna is worth buying by carefully examining a cross-section near the tail, checking the oil content, color, texture, and taste. The tuna auction usually begins at about 5:30 am and ends at about 6:30 am.




You'll also visit Tsukiji Outer Market. The market is home to more than 400 stores lined on both sides of the streets, with sushi and ramen restaurants, seafood stores, kitchen knife stores, fruit and vegetable stores, chinaware stores, green tea stores, Japanese confectionery stores, and many others.




There are also many kinds of umami (savory taste) ingredients such as kombu kelp and flakes of dried bonito for sale here. You can enjoy the market's old charm and soak in its rich history.




There is also one special spot to visit: Namiyoke Shrine. It houses a guardian deity for the people working at Tsukiji Outer Market. In the precinct of the shrine, you can see a huge lion’s mask displayed.




Book this tour now and both of Tokyo's famous fish markets!

description: Blue Ocean is the only place in Onna Village, Okinawa Prefecture, on the west coast where you can enjoy Blue Cave Experience Diving and Parasailing in the same location. This affordable tour offers all the activities without any transportation, and departs from the same port. Enjoy both Blue Cave Experience Diving and parasailing in approximately four hours after departing from the port. What is Introductory Diving? ★ Introductory Diving is a program that allows even those without a license to try scuba diving under the guidance of an instructor. Enjoy your first dive while practicing underwater hand signals, shallow breathing, and underwater swimming. Blue Ocean handles all the hassle of preparing your dive mask, tank, diving equipment, and wetsuit. Each group is assigned a dedicated instructor, and this course is designed for first-time divers. Our experienced staff ensures you can participate with peace of mind, and we'll provide thorough, thorough support from the start. This introductory diving tour is perfect for beginners and those who can't swim. The unique feature of the Blue Cave Experience Diving by boat is the extremely high probability of reaching the Blue Cave. There's no need to go down the stairs at Cape Maeda. You also don't have to carry heavy equipment like tanks or walk. Recommended for those who aren't confident in their physical strength or for those who have boarded a boat. What is parasailing? Parasailing is a marine activity where you are pulled by a boat and rise into the sky on a parachute, enjoying the ocean and scenery from above. It's very popular in Okinawa. What is it like to enjoy it?・Safety is ensured with a life jacket and harness. ・The boat moves → you float up into the sky (rope length 50-200m). ・A 10-minute aerial stroll. Almost no controls are required, making it suitable for beginners. This marine sport is not a thrilling one, but rather a "quiet way to enjoy the scenery." Benefits of experiencing parasailing in Onna Village: ・View the crystal-clear waters of Onna Village from above. ・

description:
"Sadahama" is a secluded beach located in Nakijin, in the northern part of Okinawa Island. Because it is a natural beach that has not been commercialized for tourism, you can find a "real ocean" with overwhelming clarity and tranquility. Clear kayaks offer stability and a special experience of gliding leisurely across the water while enjoying the coral and tropical fish spreading out beneath your feet. It is a very popular activity for families as it can be safely enjoyed even with small children. Furthermore, with drone photography included, you can capture "movie-scene-worthy" memories from the sky. You can take a special photo that can only be captured here, with the clear sea and clear kayak looking stunning. The experience takes place on a private beach with few tourists. It is a beautiful location that has been used as a filming location for music videos by famous artists and dramas. We will provide a thorough lecture before the experience so that even first-timers can participate with peace of mind. We will guide you to the best point according to the sea conditions of the day, so you can enjoy it safely. - How to use ① About the meeting place Depending on the sea conditions and wind direction on the day, the meeting place may be changed to one of the following. ⸻Meeting Place ① 619 Yabu, Nago City, Okinawa Prefecture 905-0007 (75 Marine Shop) [Meeting Place ②] Sadahama Beach 631 Moroshi, Nakijin Village, Kunigami District, Okinawa Prefecture 905-0426 *Please arrive 15 minutes before your reservation time. *The final meeting place will be announced the day before or on the day of the event. *If you are unsure of the location, please feel free to call us. ⸻Registration and Lecture (approx. 10 minutes) After registration, we will carefully explain how to use the kayaks and important points to note on the beach. Even first-timers can participate with peace of mind. ⸻③Experience (approx. 50 minutes) Enjoy a clear kayak experience in the crystal-clear waters of Okinawa. Drone photography will also be conducted during this time. ⸻④Dismissal (approx. 10 minutes) After the experience, we will hand over the photographic data and dismiss you. ⸻Notes (Please be sure to check) ・The location may change depending on the weather and sea conditions on the day. ・If there is a change in the location, we will contact you around 7 PM the day before. • Please be sure to register a phone number where you can be reached when making your reservation. description:
This is a snorkeling tour that takes you to the Blue Cave and Yabiji, two of the most popular scenic spots in Miyakojima. The Blue Cave is only about 5 minutes away from the shop, so it's a recommended plan for those who want to make the most of their time. The Blue Cave was my playground as a child. *At the time, there was an urban legend that there was a giant shark deep inside the cave, so it was a bit of a creepy spot. The sunlight that shines in from the entrance illuminates the clear water surface, casting a vivid and fantastic Irabu Blue, inviting visitors into a world of emotion. At Yabiji, one of the largest coral reefs in Japan, we head to a point where sea turtles lurk in the coral reef! Wild creatures change their behavior patterns depending on the sea conditions, so we are good at choosing points that suit the weather and sea conditions on the day of the tour, rather than being limited to a certain type. (We will visit 3 to 4 points depending on the sea conditions) Yabiji is made up of 100 coral reefs, both large and small, and there is a wide variety of marine life. There are many points that the captain, a local with over 18 years of experience, knows well. We will deliver the charm of the sea, where we have played with all our might since we were children and learned a lot, to the islanders. Please look forward to it. As a local guide, we have developed many points since the time when the number of tourists was 1/20 of what it is now. Now, many local guides who have built their careers at our company are active in Miyakojima. Since our founding, we have made safety our number one priority, and have created marine tours unique to the local area, incorporating stories about local history and culture. Thanks to you, we now have many repeat customers who have become like friends. Even if you are new to snorkeling, please feel free to join our Yumutsu tour.

description:
The Blue Cave is one of Okinawa's most famous ocean landmarks. On this private tour, you'll dive to the bottom of the cave with an experienced guide and enjoy stunning undersea views.




With the dock only a 1 minute walk from the shop and the Blue Cave just 5 minutes' away by boat, you'll waste no time in the transfer! With your guide's help, you'll head beneath the waves to enjoy Okinawa's famously clear seas.




During the tour, your guide will take photos and videos that'll be sent to you immediately after the tour — perfect for social media!




The dive organization has been certified by the Okinawa Public Safety Commission and is ISO24803 compliant.

Precautions: Please use a coral-friendly sunscreen that does not contain the ingredients "oxybenzone" and "octinoxate". Due to the alcohol disinfection of snorkeling masks, we ask that customers renting masks come without makeup. *If you are wearing makeup, please remove any lipstick, gloss, lip balm, foundation, etc. that will come into direct contact with the mask. This is to protect the nature and wildlife of Miyako Island, so we ask for your understanding and cooperation. Please note that we may not be able to see wild sea turtles. Please make your own way to the meeting place for the Uni Beach tour. *Drone photography may not be possible depending on the conditions. Thank you for your understanding.
.

In such cases, the tour fee will not be refunded. - You have experienced asthma symptoms within the past year. - You have had a stroke or heart disease in the past. - You use a pacemaker, ICD (implantable cardioverter-defibrillator), or stent. - You have respiratory, ear, or circulatory problems. - You use a hearing aid. - You have a panic disorder. - You have consumed alcohol within the last 8 hours. - You are currently pregnant or may be pregnant.

title: KOURI OCEAN TOWER TICKET
overview: Kouri Island is located in the northern ocean of Okinawa's main island and is connected to the island by the Kouri Ohashi Bridge. The island is dotted with many spots where visitors can enjoy the beauty of the sea, and it is also known as the ""Island of Love"" due to the legend of Adam and Eve in Okinawa. The Kouri Ocean Tower offers visitors a panoramic view of the beautiful sky and sea, as well as information on the island's history and the beautiful shells of the world.
highlights: From the Ocean Tower, 82 meters above sea level, enjoy the blue of the sea and sky, the Kouri blue as far as the eye can see.
On the Ocean Deck, you can enjoy the liberating view while being enveloped by the sea breeze.
At the Shell Museum, you can see more than 10,000 beautiful shells collected from all over the world.
An automated cart will take you to the observation tower through a garden overflowing with Okinawan plants. (Enjoy an 8-minute cart ride)

description:
You can feel the beautiful and clear sea of Miyako Island with your whole body! Enjoy the beautiful coral reefs and swim with sea turtles at a special spot where you have a high probability of encountering sea turtles! The time required is only 2 hours, so you can enjoy other activities without ruining your day.




The clear blue ocean is characteristic of Miyako Island and is widely known as “Miyako blue. Here, you will encounter not only sea turtles, but also colorful fish and a wide variety of beautiful sea creatures.




During the experience, the guide will take pictures with a waterproof digital camera so that you can take home memories of your trip. The photo data will be shared with you after the experience, so you do not need to bring your own camera! Forget about taking pictures and enjoy the activity to the fullest.




Enjoy the precious experience of swimming with sea turtles and experiencing the sea of Miyakojima with your whole body, which attracts attention not only from Japan but from all over the world!

description:
★Our concept is "Slow, Relaxed, and Relaxed!"★FLOWFISH operates with a small group size for your peace of mind. We will guide you to three diving spots per day! This tour allows you to enjoy the Yaeyama Sea at your own pace. Whether you're a beginner or experienced, and from adults to children, we will guide you at a leisurely pace to accommodate each guest, so even first-time divers can enjoy themselves with peace of mind! Our captain, a veteran with over 15 years of guiding experience, will be flexible and will accommodate even bad conditions. We will do our best to accommodate your requests, whether you want to see manta rays, swim with sea turtles, or visit a spot with beautiful coral reefs and white sand!★Land on the coveted uninhabited island of "Panari, the Last Remote Island" or the "Mysterious Island, Hamajima"!★We will land on one of the islands depending on the sea conditions, weather, and reservation status on the day. Enjoy beautiful scenery and a relaxing time on this uninhabited island unique to the southern islands! ✳︎What is the Mysterious Island?! ✳︎A 20-minute boat ride from Ishigaki Island, this uninhabited island with white sandy beaches floats in the emerald blue sea. It appears and disappears with the tides, changing its appearance every day, and is therefore known as the "phantom island." ✳︎What is Aragusuku Island (Panari)? ✳︎A 50-minute boat ride from Ishigaki Island, this island is the site of a mermaid legend. There are no regular boats, so it is normally only accessible on tours. Also, as it is privately owned, we have received special permission from the owner to land on it. ★Photos taken during the tour will be given to you for free! ★Photos taken by staff during the tour will be sent to you by email at a later date. ★Basic Schedule★ (May vary depending on weather, sea conditions, and reservations) ●Pick-up around 8:00 | ●8:30-9:00 Departure | ●Point 1 | ●Point 2 | ●Landing on the mysterious island of Hamajima or Panari | Lunchtime | ●Point 3 | ●Around 15:30-16:00 Return to port ↓ ●Drop-off to your accommodation Safety is our number one priority on this tour! Come and enjoy the beautiful Yaeyama sea at your own pace!

title: Kyoto "Ukiyo-e Super Creator Utagawa Kuniyoshi Exhibition" Admission E-Ticket
overview:
Ukiyo-e artist Utagawa Kuniyoshi (1797–1861), who was active in the late Edo period, was a disciple of Utagawa Toyokuni, the founder of the Utagawa school. Kuniyoshi rose to prominence with his “Suikoden Series,” based on Chinese classics, earning him the nickname “Kuniyoshi of Warrior Prints.”




He went on to produce works across a wide range of genres beyond warrior prints, including portraits of beautiful women, historical paintings, and even landscape works incorporating Western techniques, fully demonstrating his extraordinary talent.




This exhibition showcases approximately 200 works spanning Kuniyoshi’s specialties, including warrior prints, caricatures, and bijin-ga, offering a deep dive into his richly imaginative world.
highlights: A comprehensive exhibition highlighting the enduring appeal of Kuniyoshi Utagawa
Featuring approximately 200 works across diverse genres, including warrior prints, bijin-ga, landscapes, and more

title: Osaka–Okayama Sanyo Shinkansen Flexible Bullet Train Ticket[E-Ticket]
overview:
Book now for flexible Sanyo Shinkansen tickets between Osaka and Okayama — at a special price only for visitors to Japan!




Exclusive to those with temporary visitor status, take the Sanyo Shinkansen for less! This one-way ticket is valid up to 90 days after booking. Ride the Sanyo Shinkansen between Osaka and Okayama Station for a super-smooth high-speed journey and a chance to ride the Hello Kitty Shinkansen!

description:
The Sanyo Shinkansen is a high-speed bullet train line in West Japan that runs between Hakata in Kyushu and Shin-Osaka in Kansai. The Shinkansen network is world-famous, offering fantastic speed and superb comfort. Plus, if you ride the Kodama service No. 842 or 849, you can expect a cute and fun Hello Kitty experience!




Ideal for long-distance journeys, the Shinkansen eats up the miles for easy inter-city travel across Japan. With Osaka-to-Okayama bullet train tickets (or Okayama-to-Osaka bullet train tickets), you’ll cruise along the Sanyo Shinkansen at incredible speed on a ride like no other.




If your itinerary covers Osaka and Okayama, book now so nothing gets in the way of your perfect Japan adventure!




Book your flexible tickets now, use the QR Code* to collect them in Japan, and make a seat reservation at the station in Japan, so nothing gets in the way of your bullet train adventure!




*QR Code is a registered trademark of Denso Wave, Inc.
how_it_works:
This ticket can be used on a variety of services. See the table below for the validity, and any other tickets needed.






Purchasing a "Discounted One-way Ticket"



*Available from select overseas travel agencies.
Purchasing Exchange Order through a Travel Agency outside Japan




Step 1: Purchase an exchange order (E-TICKET) at sales locations outside Japan
• Once issued, the E-TICKET must be exchanged within 3 months




Step 2: Exchange the E-TICKET for a "Discounted One-way Ticket" at an exchange location in Japan
• E-TICKET exchange locations: Click here for details
• Receiving an E-TICKET: Click here for details
• The E-TICKET that could not been exchanged can be refunded at the overseas travel agency where the pass was originally purchased (a separate handling fee will be applied)
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is not available for purchase within Japan



Availability and Use



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is available and usable all year around



Changes



• Changing the validity period start date is allowed only once
• The ticket cannot be changed for a different ticket type; the ticket must instead be refunded, and the desired ticket purchased separately
• Changing the departure or arrival station within a section is allowed only once for unused tickets within the validity period
• Changing trains is allowed only once before the departure of the designated train, and only if tickets for the train being changed to are available within the validity period



Refunds



• Refunds are accepted only at the "Discounted One-way Ticket" exchange location.
• Click here for information on refund locations for refunds due to non-availability of services, delays, or other circumstances
• The sales price minus a handling fee will be refunded.




Handling fee:
If no reserved seating ticket has been issued: 560 YEN
If a reserved seating ticket has been issued:
• Up to 2 days before departure: 560 YEN
• Between day before departure and time of departure: 30% of original undiscounted Express ticket (based on original ticket purchase date) + 220 YEN (minimum handling fee: 560 YEN)




• A handling fee equivalent to the Limited express ticket for each train will be applied to a reserved seating ticket after departure time.
• Refunds cannot be provided if the "Discounted One-way Ticket" has been used or the validity period has ended.
• A refund of an unexchanged E-TICKET is available from the overseas travel agency where the ticket was purchased. Please note that an additional handling fee will apply.



Related information



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is only valid for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" holder
• Presentation of a passport (of the person using the "Discounted One-way Ticket") is required to exchange an E-TICKET
• No more than one E-TICKET can be exchanged per passport
• A passport is required when using a "Discounted One-way Ticket" and must be presented to the JR staff upon request
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" will not be reissued even if lost or stolen
• The conditions of use for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" are stipulated by the JR-WEST carriage conditions and the laws of Japan
• Conditions of use for an exchange order are stipulated by regulations and contracts of issuing companies
• If any discrepancies arise between the foreign language and Japanese versions of the carriage conditions, the Japanese version has precedence
• The information displayed (including prices) is current as of August 1, 2024, and is subject to change

title: [Okinawa Miyakojima] Book a SUP Tour to the Phantom Island “Yuni no Hama“
overview:
This is a tour to visit and land on the fantastic “Yuni no Hama” by SUP!




The beach of Yuni (Yuni no Hama) appears only at low tide near the Irabu Bridge connecting Miyako Island and Irabu Island, and is commonly called “Phantom Island” because it will never be the same shape or size again.




The beautiful white beach is like a private beach where no one can disturb you!




You can enjoy a 360-degree panoramic view of the Miyako blue ocean, and also enjoy the mysterious sensation as if you are standing on the horizon.
