description:
Resin art has been gaining popularity in Japan in recent years, and ocean resin that can express the ocean is very popular in Miyakojima! It is sure to be a great gift for ocean lovers! The white waves created with a heat gun look like real waves... Certified instructors who have learned the basic techniques thoroughly at the Epoxy Art Association will help you with the experience. It can be enjoyed by families with children, couples, couples, friends, or by yourself, regardless of age or gender. Same-day reservations are OK! Don't worry about hot or rainy days! Miyakojima has a summer-like climate all year round and sudden rains are common, but you can enjoy it anytime without worrying about the weather! [Recommended for the following people!] ・I want to make some memories even though the weather is bad. ・The weather is bad and I can't do the marine sports I had booked.・I'm staying in Miyakojima for a long time, so I want to try something a little different. ・I want to try something that I can participate in with my children. ・I want to try something quick in my spare time. [Course Description] "Ocean Coaster & Mini Board" Production Course └ You can make one coaster and a mini board (approximately 15cm x 15cm). *The smaller the resin art work, the more difficult it is to make waves, so if you want to enjoy resin art to the fullest, we recommend the "B5 Board" plan. *This is a course where you can make two types of works, but please order one course per person. [Melty Art's appealing points! ] ・Epoxy Art Association certified instructors will help you make it! We will teach you in a fun and careful way so that even beginners can make it with confidence! ・Instructors who live in Miyakojima will liven it up with fun stories! title: Shibuya Monkey Kart Tour on a Customized Go-Kart!
overview:
Discover Tokyo's vibrant streets on a customized go-kart tour of Shibuya and beyond with Monkey Kart! Our specially designed karts promise thrilling exploration of iconic neighborhoods and famous landmarks, from Scramble Crossing to Harajuku, complete with themed costumes for a unique tour experience!




We also offer an Asakusa Course, and an Asakusa ATV Tour that takes 2 per vehicle!
highlights: Grab a crazy costume and turn heads riding our eye-catching customized go-karts
Cruise Shibuya and Tokyo in style at speeds up to 60 km per hour
Let the staff take your photo at iconic spots like Shibuya, Harajuku, and Shinjuku
Pro photo editing is included to ensure a suitable memento of a unique experience
Reserve with a Rakuten Account to earn or use points for every booking!
important_info:
All drivers must bring a driver's license valid under the 1949 Geneva Convention, and a hard copy of the appropriate international driving permit (or other license/permit valid in Japan such as SOFA or Japanese license)
Please ensure you have the required license/permit before booking, as no refunds will be given to drivers lacking the required documents on arrival
For more details on eligible licenses and required documents, please click here
Note that ineligible licenses include those from China, Indonesia, Mexico, Pakistan, Qatar, and Saudi Arabia
Drivers must be aged 18+ to participate in this activity
Please do not wear high heels, long skirts, or other clothing that can interfere with driving the go-kart
In case of unforeseen events or extreme weather, the operator reserves the right to cancel the activity; if this happens, you have the option to reschedule or request a full refund
Meetup is strictly 15 minutes before departure; operator is required to check your driving license before participation so please make sure to arrive early
Latecomers or no-shows can't be refunded
This experience requires good weather; if it’s cancelled due to poor weather, you’ll be offered a different date or a full refund
This activity is in no way a “Mario Kart” tour or a reflection of Nintendo
Each go-kart is for a single driver, there are no 2-seater go-karts; those wishing to ride as a passenger should instead book the Asakusa ATV Tour

"Godzilla Intercept Operation: National Godzilla Awajishima Research Center" is the world's only entertainment attraction that finally opened in 2020, featuring the world's largest life-size Godzilla that landed on Awaji Island. Participants become members of the National Godzilla Awajishima Research Center and take on a zip-line and shooting mission to monitor Godzilla, who has been suspended due to a capture operation. ⚪︎ Pre-Theater ⚪︎ Featuring a star-studded cast, Toho-produced visuals, and an original story created specifically for this attraction, participants are instantly drawn into the world of Godzilla. ⚪︎ Zipline ⚪︎ Two courses are available: the "Internal Monitoring Course," where participants enter Godzilla's body through its mouth via a zip line, and the "External Monitoring Course," where participants glide alongside Godzilla. *Please choose one of the two courses: the "Internal Monitoring Course" or the "External Monitoring Course." *Please note that selection may not be available depending on the capacity. ⚪ Godzilla Cell Shooting: This shooting attraction involves using a special gun to eliminate Godzilla cells scattered by Godzilla and stop their proliferation. Dodge attacks from the Godzilla cells and aim for a high score. ⚪ Godzilla Museum: The world's first permanent Godzilla museum, where you can enjoy the history of Godzilla through various materials. See exclusive exhibits along with numerous official materials. ⚪ Awaji Island Special Effects Studio: Edit videos and photos on the spot and purchase one-of-a-kind original videos and commemorative photos. Experience the immersive experience of a film studio. (Photo fee required.) ⚪ AR x Real Mystery Game: Track down the cells of a giant unknown creature in this puzzle-solving game you can play while strolling around the park. title: Awaji Island/Fukura Port Departure: High-Speed Thrill Cruise - Adventure RIB Ride
overview: Awaji Island Adventure RIB Ride is a thrilling 45-minute cruising activity that takes you speeding across the sea around Awaji Island on a special vessel called a "RIB." RIBs are known for their high speed and stability, offering a rollercoaster-like exhilaration combined with panoramic 360-degree views.
highlights: Book with a Rakuten account to earn or use points for each booking
important_info:
【Cold Weather Protection】
It can feel very cold on the RIB, which travels at high speed on the sea, especially until mid-May. Although you will rarely get splashed, we offer free rentals of top-brand marine sports wear, as well as goggles, gloves, and knit caps for those who wish to use them, as protection against the cold.
【Boarding Requirements】
・Must be at least 140cm tall (Children of elementary school age or younger must be accompanied by a parent or guardian).
・Guests aged 65 or older, and those with pre-existing medical conditions, will be required to submit a medical questionnaire before boarding. The captain reserves the right to refuse boarding if they determine there is a risk.
・Pregnant women, those who may be pregnant, those who are feeling unwell, and those who are intoxicated are not permitted to board.
【Important Notes Regarding Boarding】
・Safety is our top priority. We reserve the right to cancel the operation if there is a risk of making a safe voyage difficult.
・The captain and operations manager may decide to cancel the operation due to weather, sea conditions, safety concerns, or other circumstances.
・If a cancellation is decided in advance, the operating company will contact customers who have reservations.
・Wearing a life jacket is mandatory for all passengers.
・We will explain the correct way to wear a life jacket during a safety briefing before boarding. We appreciate your cooperation in ensuring safe water activities.
・Please wear comfortable shoes such as sneakers (Please be careful of your footing when boarding and disembarking).
・High heels are not permitted due to safety concerns.
・Depending on the weather and sea conditions, you may get splashed.
・Please be careful not to drop your personal belongings such as smartphones and wallets.
・We can store your luggage, but we cannot be responsible for valuables. Please store valuables in your car or use a coin locker.
・We will navigate with safety as our top priority, but the boat may rock significantly. Please do not stand up while the boat is in motion.
・Please follow the captain's instructions while on board.
【Parking】
・All parking is free of charge.
・Staff will guide you to the nearest available parking space in order, so please first come to parking area #0 (in front of Roadside Station Fukura Nanairo-kan) as shown on the map.
・Parking areas are crowded on Saturdays, Sundays, and holidays. On busy days, the parking area may be a 5-minute walk from Roadside Station Fukura. Please be sure to arrive with plenty of time to spare.

【Vessels Used】

【Route】
Tour around the Fukura Bay area
Tour from Fukura Bay towards the Naruto Strait area ~ around the Ama coast
※This course does not go to see the whirlpools.







【What is a RIB?】
We use RIBs (Rigid Inflatable Boats), small vessels that are very popular in marine activities overseas. These boats consist of a fiberglass (FRP) hull with high-tech inflatable tubes attached. The large inflatable tubes on both sides provide excellent seakeeping capabilities, ensuring stable navigation in all kinds of sea conditions and superior safety.

description:
＼If you're unsure which to choose, try this! Our No. 1 popular course for a balanced experience of Irabu Island／ This standard course takes you on a buggy and mini-jeep tour of Irabu Island's roads and photo spots. As it's a completely private tour limited to one group at a time, couples, families, and friends can enjoy it without any worries. There is an instruction and practice time before departure, so even first-timers can participate with confidence. On the tour, we will take you to 1-2 spots that are characteristic of Irabu Island, depending on the weather and road conditions of the day. We will adjust the route according to the conditions of the day, focusing on areas where you can feel the scenery and nature characteristic of Irabu Island, such as around Irabu Bridge, around Shiratori Cape, towards Ndokubiabu, and farm roads in sugarcane fields. IRABU KICKS tours are supervised by operators from Irabu Island, who are also native to the island. Based on the perspective of someone who grew up on the island, we value creating tours that allow you to feel the scenery of Irabu Island, as well as the atmosphere of local life and culture, not just the tourist spots. On the day, a staff member will lead the tour with safety in mind. During the tour, our staff will take photos and videos of you driving and at photo spots. One of the attractions is that they can capture driving scenes that are difficult to take on your own, as well as natural expressions of your trip. You can generally choose between a buggy and a mini jeep, but we may not be able to accommodate your request depending on the number of people in your reservation, vehicle availability, and the operating conditions on the day. If you have a preferred vehicle, please let us know when you make your reservation. *The tour takes approximately 2 hours. *This includes check-in, driver's license verification, operation instructions, practice run, and guidance after returning. *Please allow approximately 1.5 hours for the actual driving and experience time. *The number and content of photos and videos taken will vary depending on the weather, route, and safety conditions on the day. *Stopping spots and routes will be determined considering the weather, road conditions, congestion, and safety on the day. [Summer Limited | Sunset Slot Available] In the summer, we also offer a sunset slot that can be enjoyed in the evening. Evening tours require consultation, so please inquire in advance if you are interested. *Viewing the sunset is not guaranteed. *Depending on the time of sunset, weather, and cloud conditions, the sunset may not be visible. *Please note that the shooting content may change in the evening due to the time of day when it gets dark.

title: Nagano Ina Hanamaruki Miso Making Experience Ticket
overview: Why not try making miso at the Miso Making Experience Center in Ina City, Nagano Prefecture, while enjoying the majestic view of the Southern Japanese Alps? This experience starts off with learning about the basics of miso. After that, you will visit the neighboring Hanamaruki Foods factory, and experience making miso at the experience hall! The miso you make will be fermented and controlled at the museum, and delivered to you about 3 months later.
highlights: You can even tour the adjacent factory!

.
description:
In addition to the hands-on experience of making miso, you will also be abel to learn about miso and tour the adjacent Ina factory.




[Schedule of the day]
・Check in at the reception desk and go to the experience hall.
First of all, please come to the reception desk on the 1st floor lobby of the Ina factory, which is adjacent to the Miso Making Experience Hall, at least 15 minutes before the time of the experience.




・Learn the basics of miso
After the registration, you will move to the Miso Making Experience Hall, where you will learn the basics of miso and the process of making miso in a theater room on the first floor with a large screen showing images.




・Guided tour of the Ina factory
After learning the basics of miso, visitors will move to the adjoining Ina factory. The tour will show you how miso is produced and shipped at the factory.
In the aisle, there is a corner where you can view TV commercials from the past, from nostalgic commercials to the latest commercials.




～Experience making miso! ～
After the tour of the Ina factory, return to the Experience Hall and experience making miso in the open-air experience room with a view of the Alps!
(1) Check the ingredients and tools
Materials and tools necessary for making miso will be provided at each table. Under the instruction of our staff, we will explain the ingredients and tools to each table.
(Aprons and net hats are also provided.)
(2) Mixing Koji and salt
First, put koji and salt in a plastic bag. Shake the bag up and down to mix the koji and salt well so that there are no lumps of salt.
(3) Mash the soybeans
Put half of the steamed soybeans into the mincing machine and mash the soybeans by turning the handle by hand.
(4) Mix koji and soybeans well
Put the mashed soybeans into the bag in which you mixed the koji and salt earlier. Tie the bag tightly with a rubber band to prevent the mouth from opening, and knead the mixture well from the top of the bag.
(5) Place a weight on the bag to complete the process!
Put the seed water* into the bag, tie it with rubber bands, and knead to mix. After that, remove the rubber bands and put the whole bag into a container to release the air in the bag, and tie it again with rubber bands. Put a wooden lid and a weight on it, and it is ready to use!
*Seed water is water that contains yeast for fermenting miso.
(6) Mature in a dedicated fermentation room!
The prepared miso (about 2 kg) is matured in a special fermentation room for about 3 months to become delicious miso!
We will deliver it to you as soon as it is ready!

Among them, we'll head to the special area of ​​Pinai-sara Falls, designated as a "Specific Tourism Resource," which will require permission to enter from March 2025. We'll paddle a canoe down a mangrove river and trek through a subtropical jungle. We'll spend a full day exploring the base and top of Pinai-sara Falls, which boasts the greatest drop in Okinawa Prefecture (approximately 55m). This fulfilling 6-7 hour course is recommended for those who want to fully experience the great nature of Iriomote Island! title: Samurai Theater “The Show” at Tokyo’s Kanda Myojin Shrine
overview: Get tickets for a thrilling samurai stage show (with dinner & all-you-can-drink options) at one of Tokyo's most historic shrines! Swordplay, dance, and non-verbal theater tell the thrilling tale of an infamous 10th-century warlord enshrined right here at Kanda Myojin!
highlights: Enjoy Japanese food and free-flowing drinks before the show
Explore the beautiful grounds of Kanda Myojin Shrine
Immerse in Japanese culture, then take photos with the samurai after the show
See a Tokyo samurai show with dance, drama, and danger!
Book with a Rakuten account to earn or use points for each ticket
important_info:
Please come to the first-floor lobby of EDOCCO Edo Cultural Complex on the grounds of Kanda Myojin Shrine
The theater is in EDOCCO Studio on floor B1; if arriving after 19:45 and reception has closed, please call the theater staff (03-6811-6582)
Infants (ages 0–5) are free of charge: please choose the number of infants as an add-on option
Specific seating cannot be requested: places will be assigned by staff
All-you-can-drink options include beer, wine, highball, and soft drinks (arrive by 19:00 to enjoy to the fullest)
A-la-carte menu is subject to change, but options typically include kara-age, takoyaki, steamed or grilled dumplings, edamame, and french fries (about 800–1,000 JPY)
Food and drinks can be ordered from 19:00 until the show starts
This show is a non-verbal theater performance: be sure to read the pamphlet before the show starts to fully enjoy it
Taking photos or video during the show is not allowed except at the time specified by staff
We do not allow pets, smoking, or bringing outside food and drinks
Venue is wheelchair accessible

description:
Introducing “The Show,” an energetic and historical samurai show in Tokyo’s new Edo Culture Complex!




This action-packed show tells the story of Taira no Masakado, a legendary samurai warlord with a vengeful spirit who is deified at this very shrine, Kanda Myojin.




The non-verbal performance is accessible regardless of language, and introduces a piece of Japanese history through action, dance, and the expressive performers — all supported by modern theater technologies where old meets new.




Relax in the spacious theater space with table seating, and order a-la-cart from a menu of popular Japanese dishes like “takoyaki” and “kara-age.” All tickets include either one drink, or an all-you-can drink option, to make a real party out of it!




The venue, EDOCCO Edo Culture Complex is a four-story venue on the grounds of Kanda Myojin, built to commemorate the shrine’s 1,300-year anniversary, with a theme of “Tradition x Innovation.” When you visit for the Tokyo samurai show, be sure you’ll have time to browse the rest of EDOCCO, and look around the beautiful shrine complex that surrounds it!

title: Tokyo Tea Ceremony Experience or Wearing Kimono at Bonsai Museum
overview: Enjoy an authentic tea ceremony and kimono experience in a traditional Japanese-style house of Shunkaen Bonsai Museum in Tokyo! You can see not only beautiful Japanese architecture but also selected bonsai art displayed in the garden. It will be an unforgettable experience!
highlights: Ladies can put on the beautiful long-sleeved Furisode kimono
Enjoy an authentic tea ceremony in a traditional Japanese-style house of Shunkaen Bonsai Museum
From the tea room you can see beautiful Bonsai art displayed in the Japanese-style garden
Enjoy taking pictures both in the room and the garden while wearing Kimono
important_info: If you arrive more than 30 minutes late, you may not be allowed to take the experience.
description:
If you're interested in Japanese culture, why don't you experience a tea ceremony with me? You can enjoy the tea ceremony in a traditional Japanese-style room facing a scenic garden. Please relax and have a wonderful time!




This activity is available every day from 10:00 to 16:00. The tea room is located in East Tokyo, near Koiwa or Mizue stations, about 30 minutes by train and city bus from Akihabara Station.




You can choose between three types of experiences:





Tea ceremony only (45 minutes on average)


Kimono experience only (45 minutes on average)


Tea ceremony and kimono experience (60-90 minutes in total)





The above duration is approximate. The kimono experience will take about 15 minutes for a single visitor and more than 45 minutes for more than 4 people. Guests of all ages and gender can take part.




After the experiences, you can enjoy taking photos in both the tea room and the garden. Please take a pleasant walk in the garden as well.

overview: This is a kayaking experience tour for those who have never kayaked before. Why not try kayaking on Lake Shiromaru where there is no current? We provide personalized guidance to ensure everyone is satisfied with the tour!
highlights: An Okutama adventure for ages 9 and above — the whole family can enjoy together!
1 hour 10 minutes from Shinjuku or 1 hour 30 minutes from Shibuya by train — easy access from the center of Tokyo!
Easy access from the city — enjoy the great outdoors!
important_info:
About Participation



Participants must be at least 9 years old (3rd-grade elementary school students).
Minimum number of participants: 1
There are no restrictions on participation due to health conditions, but if you have any concerns please write a comment when booking. The tour operator will respond.



Cancellation of the tour due to weather conditions, etc.



The tour may be cancelled due to weather conditions or other reasons. We will not be able to compensate for travel expenses even if the tour is canceled after you arrive at the destination. Please understand this in advance.
Kayak tours will be held even if it rains. In the event of cancellation due to inclement weather, the morning tour will be cancelled by 20:00 the day before the tour, and the afternoon tour will be cancelled by 08:00 on the day of the tour.



Other Information

description:
Crusoe Rafting's kayak tour is a fun kayak experience tour for beginners. From the rafting base, Crusoe Rafting will take you to the beautiful emerald-colored Lake Shiromaru. The lake has no current, so you can enjoy kayaking safely and leisurely. The guides are all experienced kayakers, from Japan's national kayaking freestyle team representative to a national tournament participant, so you don't have to worry even if this is your first kayaking experience. From the basics of paddling to how to move the kayak as you wish, we promise a tour that satisfies all participants with detailed guidance tailored to each person's skill level.




Photos taken by Crusoe Rafting are uploaded to a password-protected web album immediately after the tour, and can be viewed and downloaded freely.




Insurance
Crusoe Rafting tours include insurance:



Personal Accident Insurance: Death, permanent disability: 8,000,000 JPY / hospitalization: 5,000 JPY per day / hospital visit: 3,000 JPY
Indemnity Insurance: Crusoe Rafting has liability insurance in addition to the above accident insurance, so you can rest assured that you will be covered in case of an emergency.



About Fees
Tour prices differ according to the season, and whether you book for a weekday or a weekend/national holiday. Please choose your date, and the price option for that day will be highlighted.




The "Junior" price applies to high-school or college students with a student ID. For others, please book the "Adult" option for those of high-school age and older.




About the BBQ set
BBQ set plan is available to enjoy BBQ after rafting. After the tour, we will drive you to the BBQ place. If necessary, we can make a 10-minute stop at a nearby supermarket to purchase ingredients, etc. Please apply for this option if you wish.

Contents: Meat and vegetable BBQ ingredients (270g meat and 5-6 vegetables)



You are free to bring your own food and alcohol.
You can also buy them at a nearby supermarket and bring them in.
Includes transportation to and from the BBQ site.

title: Osaka–Fukui Hokuriku Shinkansen & Limited Express Train Ticket[E-Ticket]
overview:
Book now for flexible express train tickets between Osaka and Fukui — at a special price only for visitors to Japan!




Exclusive to those with temporary visitor status, take the Hokuriku Shinkansen for less! This one-way ticket is valid up to 90 days after booking. Ride the Shinkansen between Fukui and Tsuruga Station, and connect to Osaka with the Limited Express Thunderbird!







• For other Hokuriku Shinkansen routes click here
• For other bullet train routes and prices, see our check our Japan Shinkansen ticket page
• See the How it Works section for full details of ticket usage!
highlights: Get Hokuriku One-Way Ticket tickets at exclusive rates!
Exchange for reserved-seat ticket at no extra charge!
Flexible tickets valid up to 90 days after booking!
Book with a Rakuten account to earn points for every ticket!
important_info:
How to Use:
1: Choose the option for your direction of travel
2: Confirmation email will be sent to the appointed email address once the booking is made
3: The QR Code* voucher for your ticket is valid up to 90 days later
4: Please go to green ticket-vending machines and pick up physical ticket by scanning QR Code of e-ticket and non-Japanese passport (click here to search)
4: Use the physical ticket to pass through the automated ticket gate




Reserving a Reserved Seat Ticket
• Reserved seat tickets can be reserved at a ticket vending machine or a station ticket office.
• Click here for information on reserving reserved seat tickets from a ticket vending machine.




Eligibility:
• "Discounted One-way Ticket" user must be holding a passport issued by a foreign government（外国での永住権を持っているお客様を含め、日本国籍のお客様は使用できません）
• "Discounted One-way Ticket" user must be entering in Japan as a "短期滞在" (Temporary Visitor)"
• "短期滞在" (Temporary Visitor) is a status of residence defined under Japanese immigration law
• Individual customers are not allowed to purchase or exchange more than one of the same ticket for the same use period
• You must present your passport to receive a pass
description:
The Hokuriku Shinkansen is a high-speed bullet train line that serves some of Japan's beautiful, less-visited cities like Fukui, Toyama, and Kanazawa. Change trains at Tsuruga Station for the Limited Express Thunderbird, which connects the travel hubs of Osaka and Kyoto!




With a Osaka-to-Fukui (or Fukui-to-Osaka) Hokuriku One-Way Ticket for the Shinkansen and Thunderbird Express, you’ll cruise between cities on a ride like no other!




Book your flexible tickets now, use the QR Code* to collect them in Japan, and make a seat reservation at the station in Japan, so nothing gets in the way of your bullet train adventure!




*QR Code is a registered trademark of Denso Wave, Inc.
how_it_works:
This ticket can be used on a variety of services. See the table below for the validity, and any other tickets needed.






Purchasing a "Discounted One-way Ticket"



*Available from select overseas travel agencies.
Purchasing Exchange Order through a Travel Agency outside Japan




Step 1: Purchase an exchange order (E-TICKET) at sales locations outside Japan
• Once issued, the E-TICKET must be exchanged within 3 months




Step 2: Exchange the E-TICKET for a "Discounted One-way Ticket" at an exchange location in Japan
• E-TICKET exchange locations: Click here for details
• Receiving an E-TICKET: Click here for details
• The E-TICKET that could not been exchanged can be refunded at the overseas travel agency where the pass was originally purchased (a separate handling fee will be applied)
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is not available for purchase within Japan



Availability and Use



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is available and usable all year around



Changes



• Changing the validity period start date is allowed only once
• The ticket cannot be changed for a different ticket type; the ticket must instead be refunded, and the desired ticket purchased separately
• Changing the departure or arrival station within a section is allowed only once for unused tickets within the validity period
• Changing trains is allowed only once before the departure of the designated train, and only if tickets for the train being changed to are available within the validity period



Refunds



• Refunds are accepted only at the "Discounted One-way Ticket" exchange location.
• Click here for information on refund locations for refunds due to non-availability of services, delays, or other circumstances
• The sales price minus a handling fee will be refunded.




Handling fee:
If no reserved seating ticket has been issued: 560 YEN
If a reserved seating ticket has been issued:
• Up to 2 days before departure: 560 YEN
• Between day before departure and time of departure: 30% of original undiscounted Express ticket (based on original ticket purchase date) + 220 YEN (minimum handling fee: 560 YEN)




• A handling fee equivalent to the Limited express ticket for each train will be applied to a reserved seating ticket after departure time.
• Refunds cannot be provided if the "Discounted One-way Ticket" has been used or the validity period has ended.
• A refund of an unexchanged E-TICKET is available from the overseas travel agency where the ticket was purchased. Please note that an additional handling fee will apply.



Related information



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is only valid for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" holder
• Presentation of a passport (of the person using the "Discounted One-way Ticket") is required to exchange an E-TICKET
• No more than one E-TICKET can be exchanged per passport
• A passport is required when using a "Discounted One-way Ticket" and must be presented to the JR staff upon request
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" will not be reissued even if lost or stolen

title: Combo Tickets: TOKYO BRT 1 day ticket & Tokyo Subway 24-Hour Ticket
overview: This product is a combination plan that includes the TOKYO BRT 1 day ticket and the Tokyo Subway Ticket (24-Hour Ticket). It offers unlimited rides on all Toei Subway lines and all Tokyo Metro lines, and is jointly issued by the Bureau of Transportation Tokyo Metropolitan Government and Tokyo Metro Co., Ltd.
highlights: Book with a Rakuten account to earn or use points for every ticket!
Book now, use up to 3 months later!
important_info:
.
There is no discount available for disability certificates.
.
[TOKYO BRT 1 day ticket]
When using the voucher, open the usage screen from the link sent to you at the time of purchase, press the “Use” button, and show the screen to the driver.
*Please ensure you have a stable network connection, as you will need to access the web page on your smartphone.
*Screenshots and printed copies cannot be accepted.
*If you purchase multiple tickets in one transaction, they cannot be used separately, and all tickets will be activated simultaneously. To use the tickets individually, please purchase them separately.
*For security purposes, you may be required to authenticate using a one-time password (OTP). If prompted, please follow the steps below:
1. Enter the email address to receive the OTP and press the send button.
2. Check that the OTP has been received by the email address set up in step 1. (The email containing the OTP will be sent from the domain “@linktivity.co.jp.” Please also check your spam folder just to be sure. If you can’t find it there, your email address may be incorrect. Open the URL for the usage screen from the voucher again and try again by entering your email address.)
3. Enter the OTP listed in the received email into the ticket screen and press the Confirm button. If you enter it correctly, the usage screen will be displayed. (Once OTP authentication is complete, the OTP will be sent to the email address you set. This email address cannot be changed, so please set an email address that can be confirmed locally on the day of use.)
.
[Tokyo Subway Ticket]
・At the time of collecting a paper ticket, you will need a “QR code” issued after the reservation is completed.
Please bring a device that can display the “QR code” or print out the “QR code”.
*If you are unable to present the “QR code”, you may not use the voucher.
・Please note that we do not offer partial exchanges of tickets. Each purchase will have a unique QR code, even if multiple tickets are bought. When you scan the QR code on the Tokyo Metro or Toei Subway, all the tickets you purchase will be exchanged and valid starting from the day of exchange.
・If the magnetic field on your Tokyo Subway Ticket becomes damaged or weakened, it may not be usable at automatic ticket gates. Please avoid placing the ticket near objects that emit strong magnetic fields (such as smartphones, home appliances, magnetic fasteners on wallets, etc.).
.
In case of bad weather or other emergencies, the facilities will be temporarily closed. Please check the website of each facility before visiting.
.
Carry-on luggage restrictions on TOKYO BRT trains:
You may bring personal items on board with a total weight of up to 10kg, a total volume of 0.027 cubic meters, and a maximum length of 1 meter.
.
Regarding the use of wheelchairs and strollers on TOKYO BRT:
You may use the designated free space without folding your wheelchair or stroller. However, please note that boarding may be refused if the free space is already occupied by another wheelchair, stroller, etc., or if the vehicle is too crowded to allow safe boarding.
.
The expiration date of the voucher:
[TOKYO BRT 1 day ticket]
Valid for use within 3 months from the date of purchase. All rides must be completed within this 3-month period.
[Tokyo Subway Ticket]
The paper ticket can be collected within 90 days from the date of purchase.
*The exchanged ticket will be valid for 6 months from the exchange date.
*The valid time will be printed on the ticket when you start using it to pass a ticket gate.
*If you enter the station and board the train within the valid time, you can still leave the station even if the valid time is passed.
*If the ticket is not used by the expiration date printed on it, it will become invalid.
*If the exchanged ticket cannot be used at the automated ticket gates, please inform a station attendant. After verification, a new ticket will be issued.
.
If your ticket cannot be displayed due to a device malfunction, a dead battery, or an unstable communication service, you will not be able to use the service. In such cases, please pay the fare separately before using the service. Please note that any fares paid separately during the period when your ticket cannot be displayed are non-refundable.
.
Recommended environment for using digital tickets: iOS 15 or later / Safari (latest version), Android 10.0 or later / Chrome (latest version).*Please note that even with the recommended browsing environment, service availability may vary depending on your device or usage conditions.
.
The trademark for QR Code is owned by DENSO WAVE INCORPORATED.

title: 【Ishigaki Island/Half Day】 Blue Cave Exploration & Snorkeling
overview:
Blue Cave Exploration & Snorkeling
This is a snorkeling plan for the popular spot “Blue Cave” located in the northern part of Ishigaki Island.
As the name suggests, the water inside the cave is clear blue, and if the conditions are met, it will reflect the blue sky and the sun, shining even more blue.
You can go ashore to explore the inside of the cave and observe the stalactite caves and other natural products of Ishigaki!
Furthermore, the area around the “Blue Cave” is very popular as a snorkeling spot!
There are many coral reefs and a lot of fish.

title: Japanese Sword Forge Tour & Knife Making in Kameoka, Kyoto
overview: Visit a sword forge in Kyoto Prefecture, learn how Japanese swords are crafted, and make your own knife!
highlights: Visit an authentic sword forge in Kyoto
Meet and listen to a sword smith, only two left in Kyoto Prefecture
Learn traditional technique of katana making
Create your own survival knife by heating and hammering
important_info:
All participants should be 10 years or older
The minimum number of participants is 2

The detailed address and map will be provided upon confirmation
Your clothes may get dusted when you work on

if you need translation, please book the add-on "English Translation Service"

description:
This tour lets you visit one of the two remaining katana-making studios in Kyoto Prefecture.




You can not only see and hold real katanas but also learn to appreciate them. Discover how their shapes have changed over time and understand why Japanese people have cherished them even after they became obsolete as weapons with the advent of guns. All this will be explained directly by the smith.




After watching a live demonstration by the smith, you will forge your own original survival knife, guided by the smith himself. It will be crafted using traditional knife forging techniques. Listening to the explanations provided during each step will help you realize the maturity and refinement of these techniques that have been handed down over centuries.




This experience is available to beginners, and you can take your knife with you on the same day. The studio is accessible via a 30-minute train ride and a 30-minute taxi ride from the center of Kyoto.




You will make the knife, and the smith will provide a leather cover for it.




Don't miss this wonderful sword-making opportunity when you are in Kyoto, especially if you are looking for an extraordinary experience to enjoy during your visit!

description:
This is an experience to make a one-of-a-kind bracelet (it can also be made into an anklet) that combines a braided cord bracelet and power stones. The braided cord is braided in a four-way braid on a round table, which was featured in the movie. You can choose three power stones from about 15 types. title: Dinner or Lunch with a Maiko
overview: Are you looking for special dinner in KYOTO? We offer this. You can enjoy Maiko's performance and try KYOTO cuisine. You can not only see her, but also interact with each other.
highlights: You'll get a chance to take photos with Maiko up close!

important_info:
Please arrive 5 minutes prior to the start time
A minimum of 10 participants are required for the experience to be held
The start time is subject to change depending on local circumstances
Please note that confirmed reservations may be cancelled in case of exceptional circumstance at the "hanamachi" leisure district
Depending on the date of the experience, the performer may be a maiko or geisha/geiko
In principle, no food or beverages may be brought into the venue
Ages 2+ are charged the regular fee
Ages 0–1 are free of charge but no seats are provided



About English-language support



The service is basically provided in Japanese
Guaranteed English interpreting is available for 8,800 JPY per group; if this is not booked, some interpreting will be provided but we cannot guarantee the extent
Last-minute booking of English interpreter is subject to availability



About private charter bookings



1–24 people: 237,600JPY (the 24-person rate)
25–40 people: 396,000 JPY (the 40-person rate)
For a party of more than 40 people, 9,990 JPY per person is required for each additional person
The above rates are for the use of one venue; if the number of people requires more than one venue, please contact us for details
Please let us know the actual number of participants when you inquire about private charter bookings



Additional options



Premium seating upgrade: SS Seat (front row) 3,300 JPY, S2 Seat (2nd row), 2,200 JPY, S3 Seat (3rd row) 1,100 JPY
Tea ceremony experience: 1,600 JPY
description:
Don’t miss the chance to meet and learn about Maiko during your visit to Kyoto.
What makes Maiko so special is their dedication to a traditional lifestyle that continues even today.
From their exquisite kimono and elegant hairstyles to their unique daily routines, Maiko represent a living tradition.




The food comes from one of Kyoto's long established restaurant. It is really traditional dishes. Dashi, soup stock is used a lot for KYOTO cuisine. To order delivery is also common and traditional style in Kyoto. We call it " Shidashi".
You’ll experience Kyoto’s rich culture with both your eyes and your taste buds during this program.




You’ll enjoy:
・Watching a Maiko’s tea ceremony
Observe the graceful movements and etiquette of a traditional tea ceremony.
If you’d like to make your own tea, we have an option. Please contact us to add the making tea option.




・Q&A Session
This is a highlight of the program. You won’t just see a Maiko—you’ll get to know her world.
Ask questions directly and gain insight into her daily life.
You’ll feel as if you’ve stepped into a hidden world.




・Photo Opportunity
Take a commemorative photo right beside Maiko.
As a special gift, she’ll present you with a lucky charm called a Senjyafuda.




・ Dance Performance
Enjoy two traditional dances that express the beauty of Japan’s seasons, love stories, or everyday life. The elegance of each movement will inspire you.




・Interactive Game with Maiko
Maiko often play simple but exciting games with guests as part of their hospitality.
Volunteers are welcome to join, but watching is just as fun!
Clap and cheer to support the players—you’ll feel like part of one joyful team.
Maiko are professionals… can anyone beat them?




Please note that the venue is not an "ochaya" or a traditional Japanese house. It is located in a building, but the place offers a relaxed atmosphere and friendly staff. No special preparation is required, and you are welcome to join without any concerns.
Depending on the situation, we may invite Geiko instead of Maiko.
This might be held with people who are not having dishes.




Program Schedule




00min Start of the program
↓ Tea ceremony demonstration by Maiko ※Tea ceremony experience can be added as an option.
↓ Traditional Kyoto's dinner prepared by a long established restaurant
↓ Question and Answer session
↓ Take photo with Maiko
↓ Maiko's "Senjafuda" present!
↓ Maiko's dance performance
↓ Game with Maiko
75min End of the program
↓ Maiko leaves the room
↓ Chatting time
90min Room departure

overview: If you want to get your very own kimono in Tokyo, join our renovation workshop and make a "3-minute kimono"! schedule:

Experience flow:

cancellation:

description:
If you've ever wanted to buy or wear a kimono. but it seemed too expensive or difficult, this is the workshop for you!




Visit a cosy private home in Tokyo's Koto Ward for a special workshop to remake a traditional kimono of your choosing as an innovative and easy-to-wear "3-minute kimono"!




In our mission to spread the love of kimono far and wide, we have taught over 700 people from 45 different countries how to wear kimonos without the need for traditional dressing skills that take extensive training to learn.




Located just a 5-minute walk from Kiba Station on the Tozai Line, our class is perfect for anyone who wants to try kimono dressing for the first time, and be able to wear their own kimono they'll take home afterwards.




1: Choose from our selection of around 300 different kimonos
2: Remake the kimono with our innovative process so it's easy to wear
3: Coordinate your finished kimono with a pre-prepared easy-to-wear obi, obi-age, and obi-himo, and take photos of the ensemble




You'll keep the kimono (included in the fee) so you can take your creation home and wear it easily any time.




We have four different ranks of kimono that you'll choose from




• Red Sticker: standard kimono included in this plan
• Blue Sticker: +2,000 JPY upgrade
• Green Sticker: +4,000 JPY upgrade
• Yellow Sticker: +6,000 JPY upgrade




Easy-to-wear "obi" belts are available for purchase from 5,000 JPY, and they come with an obi-age and obi-himo. We also give a free casual photo session at the end, to capture to moment in your new renovated kimono with all the accessories.




My mother was a kimono teacher, and from an early age, her students would visit our home. She was always moved by the beautiful transformation it created. But, it is said less than 5% of people in Japan can wear a kimono now, because it is quite challenging to put on.




One common issue with antique kimonos is their small size, as Japanese people were generally smaller in the past. Typically, kimonos require folding a part called "ohashiyori." However, we've redesigned the process to eliminate this fold, allowing even taller individuals to wear these beautiful antique kimonos comfortably, without a trained kimono dresser.
how_it_works:
1: Choose your favorite kimono (about 15 minutes)
2: Create your easy-to-wear kimono (about 1.5 hours)
3: Try on your kimono and obi, then take photos and change back (about 15 minutes)

.
This ticket can only be used at TeamLab Planets located in Toyosu and cannot be used at other TeamLab venues. Please pay attention to the correct venue.
.
[Notes of Purchasing TOKYO DMM Tickets]
- All prices include tax.
- Please be aware that you may have to wait 30 to 90 minutes before entering.
- Purchased tickets cannot be canceled or refunded.
- If you are entering with a "junior high/high school student" ticket, present your student ID.
- It is strictly prohibited to resell tickets, and tickets purchased through resale will not be accepted. The facility will not be held responsible for any such cases.
.
【Important Information for Visitors to teamLab Planets TOKYO DMM】
Ticket and Admission
・Please visit the venue entrance on the admission date stated on your voucher and line up in the admission queue. Please note that you cannot visit on any date other than the admission date stated on the voucher.
・You may be required to wait approximately 30 to 90 minutes for entry.
・If you leave the waiting line, please ensure that someone from your group remains in line. Guests visiting alone should speak with nearby staff.
・The waiting line is outdoors. Please prepare appropriately for weather conditions at your own discretion.
・Strollers must be placed in the designated area before entering.
・Children under the age of 13 are not permitted to enter alone. They must enter with a guardian aged 18 or older. One guardian aged 18 or older is required for every three children under the age of 13, including children with free admission.
・Some artworks may not be experienced by guests who are pregnant due to safety considerations.
・Re-entry after admission is not permitted. Refunds cannot be issued after entry.
Inside the Facility
・In Water, guests will experience the artwork barefoot. Please remove your shoes before entering the locker area.
・Some artworks involve water and may cause you to get wet. Please remove socks or stockings in the locker area.
・Some areas may involve water reaching up to knee height even for adults.
・For artworks using water, chlorine is used and adjusted to maintain appropriate safety standards.
・Guests with small children are recommended to bring clothing that can get wet or a change of clothes.
・If a guest’s clothing becomes wet, staff will guide you to change clothes. For safety reasons, guests wearing wet clothing cannot proceed to the next artwork. If you do not have a change of clothes, towels are available so you can dry your clothes before proceeding to the next artwork.
※Please note that this is not a swimming area.
・Some floors have mirrored surfaces. Guests concerned about reflections may use rental half pants or waist cloths.
・Free half pants are available upon request. Sizes range from XS to 6L. Please return them when leaving.
・Some artworks cannot be experienced while wearing sandals without heel support or high heels. Free rental shoes are available.
・Some areas contain strong light stimulation or dark environments.
・Guests visiting with children must not let go of their children’s hands as it may be dangerous.
・Detour routes are available for guests who wish to skip certain artworks, including those with disabilities or those accompanied by small children. Please speak with nearby staff if needed.
・Guests with babies may enter while using a baby carrier.
・Diaper changing tables are available in the restrooms. If you require nursing or rest, please use the first aid room and speak with nearby staff.
・During busy periods, staff may ask guests who have stayed in one area for a long time to move on.
【Drawing on the Water Surface Created by the Dance of Koi and People – Infinity】
・For safety and hygiene reasons, guests with an inseam shorter than 40 cm cannot experience this artwork. Children may participate if carried by a guardian aged 18 or older using a baby carrier or by being held.
【Moss Garden of Resonating Microcosms – Solidified Light Color, Dusk to Dawn】
・This artwork can be viewed in light rain. However, it may be suspended during heavy rain accompanied by strong winds such as typhoons.
Regarding Personal Belongings
・Inside the facility there are dark corridors, unstable floors, and artworks involving water. For safety and hygiene reasons, bringing personal belongings into the artwork areas is prohibited. If you must carry necessary items due to special circumstances, please consult staff.
・Free lockers are available. After passing the entrance gate, please place your belongings in the locker area.
Locker size: Width 23 cm × Depth 34 cm × Height 37 cm
・Belongings exceeding the above size cannot be stored as there is no cloakroom service.
・Large items such as suitcases exceeding the above size will be secured with a wire lock at the stroller area.
Photography
・Photography and video recording are allowed, but please ensure that other guests are not disturbed.
・Flash photography is prohibited.
・Bringing or using photography equipment longer than 30 cm, including monopods, tripods, or selfie sticks, is prohibited.
For Guests with Disabilities
・Guests arriving in wheelchairs must transfer to the wheelchair provided inside teamLab Planets when entering the facility. These are standard wheelchairs without fixation devices.
※The number of in-house wheelchairs is limited.
※For safety reasons, guests using senior carts with handles or electric wheelchairs that cannot be controlled below 2 km/h must transfer to the facility’s wheelchair.
・Guests entering with their own wheelchair must attach tire covers.
・For artworks with mirrored floors, guests with a total weight exceeding 250 kg may be asked to refrain from viewing. When entering the artwork, staff will guide you and determine whether viewing is possible depending on the floor conditions.
・For safety reasons, wheelchair users may need to use detour routes for certain artworks or may be guided to view only from designated viewing areas. Please refer to the “Wheelchair FAQ” for details.
・Entry with a cane or prosthetic leg is permitted. However, depending on the guest’s condition and the nature of the artwork, some artworks may not be accessible. Before entry, staff will explain viewing routes and usage rules according to the situation.
・For safety reasons, guests who require assistance for mobility should visit with a companion.
・If you bring an assistance dog, it will be kept in a cage before entry.
Please Note if You Fall Under Any of the Following
・Those who have experienced seizures or loss of consciousness due to light stimulation
・Those with extreme fear of confined spaces, darkness, or heights
・Those experiencing palpitations or shortness of breath
・Those who are pregnant or may be pregnant
・Those carrying children
・Anyone with concerns about physical fitness or health condition
Prohibited Activities
・Entry by intoxicated persons or those with poor manners
・Eating, drinking, or smoking inside the facility
・Bringing dangerous items or items deemed inappropriate by management
・Re-entry after exiting through the exit gate
・Commercial photography or interviews without prior consent
・Accompanying any animals, including assistance dogs
・Accompanying animals other than assistance dogs
Other Information
・The venue assumes no responsibility for injuries, losses, damages, or other troubles that occur during use of the facility.
※For details of the venue and additional notices, please check the official website https://planets.teamlab.art/tokyo/#notice
・The venue assumes no responsibility for loss or theft of valuables.
・Media coverage or photography may take place without prior notice. If you do not consent to being photographed, please inform staff. This applies regardless of the purpose of the footage.
・Some artworks may not be available due to unforeseen circumstances.
※Please note that refunds will not be issued to guests who are asked to leave the facility at the discretion of management, including for the reasons stated above.
.
Please note sudden closure and event cancellation may happen due to unexpected circumstances such as bad weather, etc. Please check the homepage of each facility before visiting.
.
Reservations can be made up to 30 days before the specified participation date.
.

description:
We will go down the downstream course of Koboke on the Yoshino River, the largest in Japan. Speaking of the Yoshino River, which flows through Tokushima and Kochi Prefectures, it is the most rapid in Japan! This is a one-day course where you can enjoy Koboke, which is said to be particularly violent even in the Yoshino River, with a professional. If you want to tackle more rapids! This is perfect for active people♪ For lunch, you will be served a voluminous stamina bento from a popular local restaurant. This outdoor course is a little different from calm rivers, and is full of the fun of nature. The tour is about 6 hours long, so you can enjoy the experience to the fullest. There are many repeat visitors who are used to rafting, but even those who are new to rafting can try it. This is a course filled with the greatness and beauty of nature. Rafting is also a water sport where you get on a boat and work together to overcome rapids and other things. It is said to have originated in the United States, and is now a popular activity that is known to everyone overseas, including in Nepal and New Zealand. Even if you are worried about sharing a boat with other people, you will often see them cooperate with each other in some way and before you know it, they will have become friends with each other. Refresh yourself with a tropical resort and overseas feel!

description:
Yabu Beach is located in Nago City, in the northern part of Okinawa Island. It's a tranquil location that hasn't been overly commercialized, offering a relaxed atmosphere. The ocean stretches out before you, and lush green mountains surround you. In this place where the sea and nature are in perfect harmony, you can enjoy a truly liberating experience. Clear kayaks are stable and safe, making them ideal for families with young children. Children can even participate while sitting on their parents' laps, allowing the whole family to share the same experience. The relatively calm waves make it easy for first-timers and those who aren't strong swimmers to participate, allowing you to relax and feel close to the Okinawan sea. Furthermore, drone photography is included, allowing you to capture special family moments in this location surrounded by the sea and mountains as lasting memories. To ensure that even first-timers can participate with confidence, a thorough lecture is provided before the experience. The tour will be conducted at a safe point depending on the sea conditions of the day. - How to Use ① Meeting Place Depending on the sea conditions and wind direction on the day, the meeting place may be changed to one of the following locations. ⸻② Registration and Lecture (approx. 10 minutes) After registration, we will explain how to use the kayaks and provide instructions. We will also provide instructions that are easy to understand for those with children. ⸻③ Experience (approx. 50 minutes) Enjoy a clear kayak experience in calm waters. Drone photography will also be conducted during this time. ⸻④ Dismissal (approx. 10 minutes) After the experience is over, we will provide you with the photos and videos and dismiss you. ⸻Notes: ・The content and area of ​​the event may change depending on the weather and sea conditions on the day. ・To prioritize safety, the date and time may be changed or the event may be canceled depending on the situation. • If there are any changes to the event details, we will contact you by the day before. • Young children must be accompanied by a guardian. • Please be sure to register a phone number where you can be reached when making your reservation. • For customers from overseas, we will contact you via the messaging function on the reservation site. title: Introductory Sake Tasting Class with a Sommelier in Asakusa Tokyo
overview:
Deepen your sake knowledge in a Tokyo tasting workshop led by a sake sommelier, followed by a tasting of up to 10 premium sake varieties in Asakusa!




Learn about sake’s history, brewing process, and regional styles before sampling a curated selection. Ideal for those who want both education and experience.
highlights: Easy to access location near Asakusa Station
Learn about Japanese sake from a sake sommelier with ample international experience
Discover how it’s made, its historical and cultural significance, and the varieties during an informational workshop
Enjoy tasting up to 10 kinds of sake from more than 50 brands from across Japan
important_info: Participants must be aged 20+ (the legal drinking age in Japan); please bring a valid ID for proof of age

description:
Your host Hide is an English-speaking sake sommelier who uses his extensive experience living abroad to share his passion for the sake of the world. Learn to differentiate between varieties, and how to find the best variety to suit your tastebuds. With contagious enthusiasm, he will show you what makes sake so special!




You'll start with a 30-minute workshop led by Hide on all things sake. Learn about how it's made, its many varieties, and its impact on Japanese culture. What are the unique processes of making sake? What kind of rituals and ceremonies feature sake? Find the answers to all your pressing questions from Hide.




Next, enjoy tasting up to 10 varieties of sake from across Japan. Guided by Hide, you can choose from over 50 varieties, from dry to sweet, cloudy to sharp, sparkling or still. Your host will support you in finding your new favorite and even purchase a bottle to take home if you'd like.




Discover the drink that captivates a nation in this approachable, beginner-friendly sake workshop and tasting led by an expert in the field!

title: Mt. Fuji Zen Temple Experience (Vegan Friendly)
overview:
Experience zazen (seated meditation) and sutra copying under the guidance of a Buddhist monk. Reflect on your inner self through waterfall meditation and forest bathing, and feel a sense of “tranquility.”




The “Zen Retreat” offers an opportunity to harmonize your mind, body, and values through the experience of Zen — a tradition of Eastern thought that has engaged with life and its cycles for over a thousand years.
highlights: Experience Authentic Zen at a Historic Temple at the Foot of Mt. Fuji
Zen is More Than Just Sitting—Walk, Breathe, and Awaken Your Senses
Shojin Cuisine: Mindful Eating Rooted in Zen Teachings
description:
About our “Zen Experience”
Experience the transformative power of Zen, not only as a mindfulness practice that deepens your understanding of Japanese culture and nature but also as a journey into your own heart. Why not explore the world of Zen at Ko-un-in temple with over 600 years of history at the base of Mount Fuji? It's bound to become an unforgettable memory.



Experience Program


Zen Tea Ceremony – 30 minutes
Zen Wellness Yoga – 30 minutes
Zazen Meditation – 30 minutes
Sutra Copying – 60 minutes
Shojin Ramen Lunch – 30 minutes
Zen Nature Walk – 60 minutes



Please note: Activities may vary depending on the season.




Program Duration
This is a one-day retreat, approximately 3 hours in length.



Experience Details



Zazen Experience
Immerse yourself in the world of Zen with a genuine monk who has undergone extensive training. Learn the fundamentals of zazen, where Zen transcends words and concepts, guiding you to find tranquility through intuitive experiences. Experience the simplicity of life, cherishing nature, and aligning your mind to live in the present moment.




Matcha Experience
Engage in the traditional Japanese tea ceremony, sipping on matcha to envelop your mind and body in a moment of tranquility. Paired with delightful confections, the tea ceremony is an opportunity to appreciate the harmony between tea and sweets. Don't miss the chance to savor these delicious Japanese treats.




Sutra Copying (Shakyo)
Shakyo is the meditative practice of hand-copying Buddhist scriptures, one character at a time. It is a time to quietly reflect and connect with the Buddha’s teachings. The goal is not perfect writing, but mindful presence in each stroke—fully immersing yourself in the “now.”




Shojin Ryori Culinary
Indulge in Shojin Ryori, a culinary journey that meticulously selects seasonal, local ingredients, completely avoiding animal products. A monk will elucidate the Zen spirit and philosophy behind each dish, allowing you to understand the deeper meaning. Shojin Ryori emphasizes a gentle approach to the body, prioritizing harmony, and relentlessly pursuing deliciousness while being mindful of health.




Shojin Ramen (Zen-style Vegan Ramen)
Savor an original ramen dish created according to the principles of shojin ryori. Made entirely without animal ingredients, this plant-based ramen embodies the Zen philosophy of simplicity and balance. A monk will share the meaning behind the meal, transforming it into a meditative experience.




Explore the Springs of Mount Fuji
Connect with Japan's nature and culture through a guided exploration of the Mount Fuji’s spring waters. Wander along the creeks and clear streams, surrounded by the breathtaking natural environment of Mount Fuji. Enjoy the changing landscapes with each season, creating a delightful and serene experience.

Total
16,000 JPY

+145

description:
The Macro World! Yanbaru Night Safari Tour: Explore the ancient subtropical forests of the Yanbaru World Natural Heritage Site. Many creatures awaken at night. This thrilling night safari tour takes you into the jungle where these creatures live. There are several nesting areas for the main Okinawa rail. If you find yourself sleeping in one of these trees, you might even encounter your long-awaited encounter! (Please note that the bird may not be in its nesting area.) Your guide will provide you with an enjoyable tour of Yanbaru, including information about the flora and fauna. They will also capture the macro world with a single-lens reflex camera, giving you dynamic photos that allow you to appreciate the beauty of nature and its creatures even more deeply. Explore the Yanbaru forest at night, which offers a completely different experience than during the day. You'll encounter vipers, giant eels, wild boars, and freshwater prawns, as well as numerous frogs and endemic species. If you're lucky, you might even spot a Okinawa rail sleeping in a tree, along with many other precious endemic species!!! If it's clear, we'll also give a free starry sky photo to all participants♪ (If you want to enjoy the starry sky♪ and take starry sky photos with your family or friends, please apply for the starry sky tour) *Tours are only available for two people or more. overview:

highlights: We have changing rooms and outfits available for you.

important_info:
< For pregnant women>
It is possible to receive a foot massage even if you are pregnant.
There are many reflex points on the soles of the feet. Some of these points tend to cause the uterus to contract when pressed by pregnant women. Our therapists are well versed in contraindications, etc., so they remove pressure points that should not be pressed on pregnant women and focus mainly on the calves, which tend to swell more easily than the soles of the feet. We accept pregnant women as well, but if you are unsure, we recommend that you refrain from the experience.




If you wish to cancel a reservation, please contact us as soon as possible.
If you cancel on the day of your appointment or cancel without notice, 100% of the reservation fee will be charged.
If you are more than 15 minutes late, your reservation will be cancelled.

description:

Graffiti Nature - Beating High Mountains and Deep Valleys, Red List
See the living things eat and are eaten by other living things in one shared ecosystem




A Table where Little People Live

Light Ball Orchestra

Waterfall Droplets, Little Drops Cause Large Movement

SKETCH FACTORY

[Koji Kinutani Tenku Museum] At the Koji Kinutani Tenku Museum, you can enjoy a variety of colorful works by Koji Kinutani, a painter who received the Order of Culture in 2021. In addition, there are plenty of hands-on content such as "a powerful 3D image that jumps into the picture", which is the world's first attempt, and "VR image that connects with the painter" that the painter himself explains in the hall and the production atelier. You can enjoy the world of art more closely from adults to children. Located on the 27th floor of Osaka's landmark Umeda Sky Building, there is also a "Tenku Cafe" in the building where you can enjoy a beautiful view overlooking Osaka Bay. After appreciating the art, enjoy a coffee time at the sky cafe with a nice view. [Aerial Garden Observatory] The Umeda Sky Building, a connected skyscraper with 40 floors above ground, 2 floors below ground, and a height of 173 meters, towering over Umeda, Osaka, is a world-famous building due to its unique shape of connecting two buildings. is known. A popular facility where you can enjoy a spectacular view from the rooftop floor of the Sky Building is the Kuchu Teien Observatory. You can see the city of Osaka from the rooftop corridor "Sky Walk", which offers a 360-degree view. Absolutely recommended on sunny days! One of the highlights is the beautiful sunset and the beautiful night view of the Kita area.

title: Tokyo to Chiba! Japanese School Day Experience at Kimino High School
overview:
Would you like to be a Japanese student for a day in Kimitsu City, Chiba Prefecture?
Discover the charm of traditional countryside school life in Japan!




Come and experience a unique one-day school program set in a former school building. Dress up in school uniforms like "Gakuran" and "Sailor Fuku" and immerse yourself in various cultural activities such as calligraphy, sports festivals, and school lunches.




This program is designed for visitors from overseas and is conducted in English, so you can explore the principles of Japanese education that foster autonomy and cooperation.




Of course, Japanese participants are also more than welcome!
highlights: Experience the full spectrum of popular Japanese cultural activities
Calligraphy and ninja adventures to complete cosplay immersion
Become the student you've seen in Japanese anime, manga, and dramas
You can immerse Japanese school experience

Please note that media coverage and filming may occur during your experience. If you prefer not to be included, please let us know in advance.
Please note that the schedule times are approximate and may change depending on the number of participants
In the event of natural disasters such as typhoons, earthquakes, and heavy snowfalls, we will not charge a cancellation fee if we decide to close our business. In such a case, we will contact each customer who has made a reservation individually.

description:
Change into a school uniform and enroll in school for a day to experience Japanese culture. "A Day at a Japanese Countryside School" is a service that allows foreign tourists to experience one of the hidden charms of Japanese culture: the good old days of a "Showa-era school" at our abandoned school campsite, Campiece Kimitsu” (located in Kimitsu City, Chiba).




In a certain Japanese countryside town, set in the showa period... Surrounded by nature and pastoral landscapes, you'll enroll for a day at a school standing in the midst of it all. An actual "homeroom teacher" will conduct classes. Participants will wear school uniforms, including gakuran and sailor suits. The experience starts with an entrance ceremony, followed by morning homeroom, day duty, lessons in calligraphy and history, school lunch, and a sports festival... culminating in a graduation ceremony where diplomas are awarded. You will have a simulated experience of the "school life" that Japanese students have gone through.

title: Oiran Makeover & Pro Photoshoot in Chiba near Tokyo
overview:
Enjoy a head-to-toe oiran transformation and professional photoshoot in Chiba with no hidden fees!




This is a women-only studio, ensuring a comfortable and secure experience (except for hair stylists and photographers). You can wear a real kimono made of pure silk, and a dedicated photographer will take your photos with an authentic backdrop. Pose guidance is included, so you can feel at ease. The studio is completely reserved for one group per time slot, allowing you to relax without worrying about other guests, and you can select up to 12 retouched photos. We provide a professional full-face makeover from scratch!




Just a one-minute walk from the JR station makes it easily accessible. Approximately 15 minutes by car from Makuhari Messe, about 40 minutes by train from Tokyo Disney Resort, and about five minutes by car from Kemigawa Shrine.
highlights: Immerse yourself in the fashion culture of Japanese courtesans
Try on a real silk kimono for amazing pictures
Includes 12 professional digital photos to commemorate your trip
Book with a Rakuten Account to earn points for each reservation!
Experience a quality-oriented oiran photoshoot
important_info:
• Reservations are required
• Please be sure to arrive at the reserved date and time
• Target age: 15 years and older
• Photos will be delivered as digital data
• Since we take time to prepare for a photogenic and authentic transformation, please allow plenty of time for your visit
• If you have allergies to cosmetics or hairspray, please consult us in advance and bring your own makeup products if necessary
• This is a women-only studio; men are not allowed to enter except for the photographer
• If someone is accompanying you, please have them wait at a nearby café, as the surrounding area is a residential neighborhood
• As a souvenir, we will present you with "Asari Tsukudani," a traditional clam dish from Chiba (please note the ingredients contain wheat and soy, may include shrimp and crab)
• For the hairstyling, a ponytail is required: if your hair is not long enough, we will attach floral accessories instead of setting a traditional oiran bun (please contact us in advance if needed)
• We provide kimono sizes up to approximately a Japanese 3L size
• If the participant is a minor, one female guardian may accompany them; for adult participants, companions are not allowed
• Photography is not permitted during makeup, hairstyling, or kimono dressing
• There is no smoking area available; please refrain from smoking in the surrounding area as it may disturb local residents
• The studio is located in a residential area; please avoid sitting, eating, drinking, or making loud noises outside the studio
• Inside the studio, only water is allowed; other food and drinks are prohibited
• Many items in the studio are handcrafted and one-of-a-kind; please be careful not to damage them

title: Deep Sumo Experience near Tokyo: Enter the Sanctuary (Kawasaki)
overview:
Immerse fully in the world of sumo, with an authentic sumo stable visit in Kawasaki, just outside Tokyo. Follow your guide to a hidden sumo stable to experience a live training session, learn sumo secrets, and even participate in a match. Then, enjoy making and eating traditional chanko-nabe with the wrestlers, and eat in the stable with them!




10 minutes from Yokohama Station, 20 from Tokyo Station, 30 from Shinjuku Station!
highlights: See and experience the actual training in a genuine sumo stable for yourself
Your guide explains the history and traditions of sumo with many tips for enjoying sumo matches
Try a practice match (optional) with a wrestler — men women, and kids can all challenge them!
Make dumplings for chanko-nabe, a dish that builds the sumo body, and eat with the wrestlers!
Reserve with a Rakuten account to earn or use points for each booking
important_info:
Please arrive early at the meeting point; the tour will depart on time and latecomers will not be refunded due to missing the tour
Vegan/vegetarian options are available, please tell us your preference during checkout
Please note that we are unable to guarantee allergy-free or cater to all dietary restrictions as the food is not prepared by the tour operator
This tour is not wheelchair/stroller accessible
The shirts are available in sizes 100 cm, 150 cm, S, M, L, 2L, and 3L
Ages 0–3 can join for free, but meals are not provided
