description:
Step into the heart of Japan's traditional sumo culture with the authentic "Enter the Sanctuary" experience on the outskirts of Tokyo.




Your journey begins at Shin-Kawasaki Station, where you'll meet your guide and travel to a real sumo stable that was active until 2022. As you explore the stable, your guide will unveil the intricate lifestyle and customs of sumo wrestlers, giving you a deeper understanding of this ancient sport.




At the stable, you'll have the unique opportunity to observe a live sumo training session. Watch as the rikishi (wrestlers) go through their rigorous morning routines, and discover the detailed techniques and movements that have been passed down since the days of the samurai. For those feeling adventurous, there's an option to step into the ring and try out some of these traditional moves firsthand.




The excitement peaks when you're given the chance to participate in a real sumo match. Feel the intensity and power of the wrestlers as you face off in the ring, a truly unforgettable experience. After the match, join the wrestlers in preparing and savoring chanko-nabe, a beloved dish known for nourishing sumo wrestlers, building their incredible bodies. This communal meal not only lets you taste the flavors of sumo but also share stories and ask questions directly to the rikishi.




This tour isn't just a passive experience; it's an interactive and deep dive into the world of sumo.




Conveniently located just 20 minutes from Tokyo Station and 30 minutes from Shinjuku Station, it offers an active, engaging, and educational sightseeing option for Tokyo visitors.




Whether you're a sumo novice or enthusiast, this experience promises rich cultural insights and fun-filled memories. Plus, you’ll take home an exclusive “Enter the Sanctuary” t-shirt as a souvenir of your unique adventure. Join us for an authentic glimpse into the life of a sumo wrestler and the rich heritage of this fascinating sport.

title: Write Japanese Calligraphy! Delve into the Essence of Japanese Beauty in Hoyu Sakuma’s Calligraphy Classroom in Tokyo
overview:
Japanese calligraphy is an art which embodies the elegance of Japan. You can learn how to create art works of calligraphy in English.
Make your own calligraphy works in my classroom and take them with you as your own precious souvenirs!
highlights: Learn how to beautifully write your own name and other words of your choice with Kanji
Enjoy an immersive experience in learning about Japanese beauty and culture
Create your own art works of calligraphy that you can take home, frame, or give as a gift
Learn the basics of calligraphy like Japanese students do
Attune your sense of harmony and balance through creating works of calligraphy
important_info:
Group discounts are available! Must reserve by number of people (e.g. the "3-person Calligraphy Lesson" rate is only available for groups of 3).
Please note that time taken to pick up visitors from Musashi-Koganei station may be included within the overall 1.5 hours of the lesson. Please let us know if you require guidance back to the station after the lesson is over.
what_to_bring: Clothes that you don't mind getting ink on during the calligraphy lesson.
meeting_point: Centrall Gate of Musashi-Koganei Station on JR Chuo Line, Tokyo
schedule: n/a
cancellation:
No-show: You pay 100% of experience price
3 days before: You pay 100% of experience price
description:
Enjoy an immersive experience of Japanese art and culture through studying shodo – the art of Japanese calligraphy using ink, brush, and paper! Through an hour and a half long lesson with native Japanese teacher Hoyu, you will learn the basics of Japanese calligraphy and will even be able to create your own works to take home as a souvenir, or give as a gift. Let this be a way for you to have an unforgettable time in Japan!




Beginning with the basics of brush technique and posture, teacher Hoyu will guide you gradually until you are able to compose works on your own comfortably. In doing a variety of exercises to hone your skills, you can learn to create beautiful works of calligraphy. You can also choose what you want to write, and the instructor will teach you how to compose it.




This is a rare opportunity to create your own souvenir of Japan, while enjoying the serenity that comes from dedicating yourself to the delicate nature of this art. Enjoy a glimpse of the Japanese beauty, and bring home the art work as something to remember from your trip. Enjoy Japan not as a spectator, but as an active participant!




Nearby the venue is the Edo-Tokyo Open Air Architectural Museum, located in the beautiful Koganei Park. There you can have a unique opportunity to go through Tokyo's history by entering replicas of historical buildings (not included in the activity price). So why not learn traditional calligraphy in the morning with me, and then take a trip back in time in the afternoon?




*We have bags that you can write your calligraphy on for an extra charge. Please let me know in advance if you would like to receive this service. We are able to print the image of your calligraphy onto bags as well but please note that shipping and handling charges and 8 days time will be required to prepare and deliver to you.




**Other non-teaching calligraphy requests, as well as sudden requests (within 2 days) will require an additional fee.
how_it_works:
On the reservation day, we will meet prior to the lesson at the Central Gate of Musashi-Koganei Station on JR Chuo Line, Tokyo. From there, we will lead you on foot to the classroom (7-10 min). The calligraphy lesson will last 1.5 hours, during which the instructor will provide you with calligraphy tools and teach you the basics of how to use them. After that, you will choose what you want to write, and the instructor will guide you in English as well as provide corrections until you are satisfied with your work. You may keep your work for display at your home or as a gift, etc. (frames are available for a separate 1000 yen price).




If you wish, you may take pictures wearing yukata, kimono (Taisho Roman hakama), jinbaori, or samurai armor (including an imitation sword). After calligraphy, you can enjoy matcha and sweets, so please feel free to contact us if you are interested in any of these additional options.




*Please note that time taken to pick up visitors from Musashi-Koganei station may be included within the overall 1.5 hours of the lesson. Please let us know if you require guidance back to the station after the lesson is over.

title: Learn how to Play Shamisen and Koto in Tokyo
overview: Discover the world of Japanese traditional music when you learn how to play shamisen and koto — classical Japanese musical instruments! This is sure to be a wonderful experience during your trip to Japan.
highlights: Learn how to play shamisen, a 3-string Japanese instrument
Learn to play koto, a refined classical musical instrument
important_info:
In case of 7 to 14 people, the experience will be divided into two sessions of 7 people each.
Example: First session 10:00, second session 11:00 *If your preferred start time is 10:00.
The price is 5,000 yen per person.
In case of 15 to 50 people, the session will be held all together at one time.
description:
The shamisen is a traditional 3-string Japanese instrument similar to a banjo and traditionally used to accompany Japanese Minyou, or folk music. There are, however, varying styles of playing the shamisen, including the up-tempo and percussive Tsugaru style of the Tsugaru region in Northern Japan.




In recent years, Tsugaru Shamisen has become popularized by the soulful and bombastic style of the Yoshida Brothers, whose playing has turned the folk instrument into a stage performance. Although it may seem daunting, anyone can quickly learn how to play shamisen through simple songs like the popular Japanese folksong "Sakura Sakura" or even up-tempo pieces.

title: Top-Rated Tokyo Taiko Drumming Workshop in Shibuya
overview:
Feel the heartbeat of Japan with a world-class Taiko drummer in her top-rated workshop!




Step into the rhythm of Tokyo with an unforgettable taiko drumming workshop led by Eva Kestner. A renowned international taiko artist, educator, and performer, Eva's taiko drumming workshop is rated 5 stars* on multiple review sites!




In a 90 minute workshop, you'll learn the basic stance and grip, the taiko rhythm and movements, then practice a traditional and a modern taiko drumming piece — all with fluent English instruction. Eva also gives a short performance and brief taiko history lesson.




Whether you’re a total beginner or a seasoned musician, this experience welcomes solo travelers, couples, families, and groups of all ages. Book with a Rakuten account to earn points for each reservation!




*As of July 1, 2025

description:
Tokyo's top-rated taiko workshop is taught by highly accomplished and award winning taiko drummer and instructor, Eva Kestner.




Enjoy a 90-minute taiko drumming workshop for people of all levels in English. We will learn the basic stance, grip, rhythm training, body movement training, and practice a traditional taiko drumming piece.



What to Expect



Expert Instruction
Learn from Eva Kestner, a Tokyo-born taiko maestro whose music is featured in global educational curriculums and major publishers.




All Levels Welcome
No experience necessary! Eva will guide you through the basics — stance, grip, rhythm, and body movement — before you practice a traditional taiko piece.




Personalized Video
Take home a recorded video of your drumming session as a unique souvenir to remember your Tokyo adventure.




Flexible Scheduling
Can’t find a time that works? Include a message when booking, and we'll strive to arrange a private session that fits your itinerary.



Instructor's Profile



Eva Kestner, taiko drum artist
Born in Tokyo, Japan, Eva is an award winning Japanese Taiko drummer, folk dancer, singer, songwriter, and educator. She started drumming at the age of 8.




Eva co-founded and directed the Taiko drumming group at Cornell University. She received the Excellency Award at Ichikawa New Artists Competition.




In 2023, her original music was published by Harper Collins Publisher and she was also the cover image of their music textbook. In 2024, her original music was used by Cambridge International Curriculum, used in over 160 countries and 10,000 schools, which sets a global standard for education, and is recognized by universities and employers worldwide.




She performs and teaches taiko internationally. She is based in Tokyo, Japan.

title: Okinawa Depart from Kadena Port Mangrove Kayaking & Fishing
overview: Immerse yourself in nature, and enjoy two of Okinawa's popular water activities in a day! Explore a dense mangrove forest on kayak, and try your luck catching tropical fish at Kadena Port. Both activities are a great way to destress and unwind.
highlights: A beginner-friendly activity that even children and first-timers can try
Enjoy two fun, leisurely water activities — mangrove kayaking and fishing — in a day
Flexible options — you can go kayaking before or after the fishing experience
Accessible and convenient — Kadena Port in central Okinawa is strategically located
important_info:
This tour may be canceled at the host's discretion depending on the weather and other conditions. If we will have to cancel this tour, we will contact you to refund or reschedule
This tour is only conducted in Japanese
Please understand that we cannot guarantee 100% that you will catch fish on this activity
The floor of the boat may be slippery, and you might get wet during this experience, so please be sure to wear waterproof clothing and footwear (or clothing and footwear that you don't mind getting wet)
As a safety measure, pregnant guests will not be allowed to participate in this activity. Please do not book this activity if you are pregnant, or you think you may be pregnant
We will call you 1 day before your tour date (by early evening, at the latest) to confirm your attendance, so please be on standby for a call from us
description:
Looking for an escape from the hustle and bustle of daily life? Let Okinawa's natural wonders heal you! Book this tour to try two of Okinawa's popular water activities in a day: kayaking through a mangrove forest and boat fishing. Both activities are beginner-friendly — children as young as three years old, as well as first-timers, are more than welcome to join in!




On the boat fishing tour, try your luck catching colorful tropical fish at Kadena Port, where the tranquil, shallow, emerald-green waters are teeming with coral reefs and fish. If you're lucky enough to catch any fish, you can take them home or have them cooked at a nearby restaurant for a separate fee.




For the kayaking tour, you'll explore a mangrove forest. Because mangroves only grow in tropical and subtropical regions, they're a rarity in mainland Japan, but they're abundant in Okinawa. These trees grow in places where seawater fills up at high tide, such as estuaries. Mangrove forests tend to have unique ecosystems, so they're home to unique species of crabs and other fishes, as well as colorful plants and waterfowl. Try spotting unique flora and fauna as you kayak through the mangrove forest!




For both activities, all equipment will be provided, so all you have to do is show up! What's more, Kadena Port is strategically located near a main road (National Route 58), and is less than an hour's drive from Naha Airport or Onna Village, so it's easily accessible.




Book now for a day of fun, out on the waters of central Okinawa!

title: Book Universal Studios Japan Express Pass 7 Osaka [E-Ticket]
overview:
Get a Universal Studios Japan Express Pass 7 online: your ticket to Nintendo World, The Wizarding World of Harry Potter™, and shorter waits at some of the Osaka park’s many amazing rides! Choose a Universal fastpass, and let the fun commence. Don’t forget to book your Studio Pass for entry!




If there's no Express Pass 7 on your visit date, book an Express Pass 4 instead!
highlights: Try Nintendo-themed rides like Yoshi’s Adventure and Mario Kart: Koopa's Challenge™
Get Area Timed Entry Tickets to the Harry Potter or Nintendo themed areas depending on your pass!
Book your Universal Express Pass Osaka from Rakuten to earn or use Rakuten Points at the same time!
Skip the line at 7 amazing rides with a Universal Express Pass 7
important_info:
• Express Passes do not grant park admission; all visitors require a Studio Pass for entry, get yours here
• Universal Express Passes are limited for each day and session
• Valid only on your chosen date, which cannot be changed; please ensure selected date(s) match your Studio Pass
• One pass per person is required
• If your pass includes a temporarily closed attraction, you will be offered an alternative, excluding the following:
— Harry Potter and the Forbidden Journey™
— Flight of the Hippogriff
— Hollywood Dream: The Ride — Backdrop
• Some attractions have designated timed entry: entry times are assigned according to availability and cannot be selected, you will be informed of your time slot when receiving your pass
• If you arrive late to an attraction with timed access, you will not be refunded and cannot change to a different time slot
• Passes cannot be used by those who enter the park with a Twilight Pass (for entry after 15:00)
• You don’t have to register your ticket on the official app, but if you wish to, please wait 24 hours after receiving the pass (it takes 24 hours for the app to recognize new passes)
• Children must be accompanied by an adult
• Outside food and beverages, as well as dangerous items, are prohibited
• Depending on your age, height, or condition, you may be unable to use some attractions; please check in advance
• Re-entry is not allowed




How to Book
1: Choose an option, then click "select date" to see availability of that pass
2: The price is updated depending on the date
3: If your chosen pass is not available, choose another option and check calendar again
4: Choose a "session" for the timing of area entry and certain rides
5: Choose number of participants; if ticket stock is not enough, try another option or session
6: After booking, receive your Universal Express Pass and QR Code by email; take care to confirm validity date and times
7: On the day of your visit, present your QR Code at the entrance of each ride; for timed entry rides, you must go at the time specified on your pass
Note that a separate Studio Pass is needed for entry to Universal Studios Japan




Ride Closures
Please note the following scheduled ride closures in 2025
• Hollywood Dream: The Ride — Backdrop: January 26 to March 23
• Jaws™: April 6 to April 28
• The Flying Dinosaur: May 7 to May 17
If your Express Pass includes any of these rides, please visit one of the alternative attractions indicated on your ticket instead. No need for further reservations. Please check with the staff for the latest information of the operation time of each attraction.

• To ensure the best experience, guests should either purchase a Universal Express Pass or an Area Timed Entry Ticket in advance.

description:
◆ Overview: The 30-Minute Tour allows you to fully experience the charms of Asakusa. This standard tour is perfect for both first-timers and repeat visitors. Starting at Kaminarimon Gate, you'll explore Nakamise Street, the area around Sensoji Temple's main hall and five-story pagoda, and backstreets brimming with traditional Japanese charm. This tour efficiently covers areas that are difficult to explore on foot, offering both sightseeing and educational experiences. ◆ Recommended for: Those who want to fully experience Asakusa without tiring out on foot, those looking for special memories on a family trip or as a couple, and those wanting to discover a side of Asakusa not found in guidebooks. This popular tour also allows international visitors to experience the authentic Japanese atmosphere. With more time than the shorter tours, you can enjoy photography and relaxed conversation. ◆ The Allure of Rickshaws: Rickshaws are more than just a means of transportation. Through the driver's lively guidance and humorous conversation, you'll learn about Asakusa's history and trivia. Another great attraction is the elevated perspective, allowing you to see the cityscape and scenery from a fresh perspective, often hidden by foot. Your rickshaw driver, an expert on Asakusa, will take your commemorative photo at the best spot. Feeling the pleasant breeze and experiencing an extraordinary time is a unique experience only aboard a rickshaw can offer. ◆The Charm of Asakusa: Asakusa is one of Tokyo's most popular tourist destinations, where Edo culture and the warm atmosphere of the downtown area still thrive. Historical landmarks like Kaminarimon and Sensoji Temple, as well as the souvenir shops lining Nakamise Street and gourmet food, thrill visitors. With many long-established restaurants and renowned shops showcasing artisanal craftsmanship, Asakusa's charm lies in the ability to immerse yourself in its history and culture just by strolling around. Seasonal festivals and events also thrive, making the area always bustling and vibrant. Even a short tour can fully experience the "Asakusa-esque" atmosphere, and a rickshaw ride makes it even more special. By being guided through the city's history and hidden gems, you'll gain a deeper understanding of Asakusa's charm and discover photogenic spots. Asakusa, where tradition and modernity blend together, offers new discoveries and inspiration with every visit. About Kurumaya Our shop, Kurumaya, is a team of professionals who have been providing rickshaw service in Asakusa for many years. Thanks to our support, we have the highest number of reviews on Google, with 1,700 reviews. Our experienced rickshaw drivers will introduce you to courses and recommended spots tailored to your needs, ensuring a memorable experience even in a short trip. We will also help you with photography and will do our utmost to help you create memories of your trip. We value a safe and comfortable ride and provide you with a time filled with smiles. We will do our best to guide you and help you create memories of your visit to Asakusa, Tokyo.

description:
This experience is a one-day package tour. Half day is for sightseeing in Yubu Island, and the other half day is for exploring the mangrove forest by canoe or SUP (stand-up paddle boat).




Reference schedule (subject to change depending on the weather)
In the morning, we will visit a mangrove river in Iriomote Island. In the morning, we will go to the mangrove river of Iriomote Island. On the way, we will trek around Iriomote Island to experience the great nature.




In the afternoon, we will visit a small island called Yubu Island located about 15 minutes away from Iriomote Island. We will take a buffalo cart to Yubu Island. Yubu Island is visited by many people as a sightseeing spot, and you can see the subtropical ecosystem.




Yubu Island is also a great place to relax on the sandy beach. It is also fun to observe rare plants and creatures. Ride on a buffalo and take pictures. Since you have free time, you can enjoy sightseeing no matter what you do.




Capture the memories of your trip in photos to take home as a souvenir! During the tour, your guide will take pictures with a special waterproof camera. The photos will be shared as data at a later date. You don't have to worry about getting your precious camera wet.

title: Kansai Railway Pass – 2 or 3 Days Unlimited Travel Around Osaka, Kyoto & Kobe [E-Ticket]
overview:
Buy a Kansai Railway Pass for 2 or 3 days of attraction discounts and unlimited travel on subway and private railways all over the Kansai region!




This ticket, an update on the Kansai Thru Pass, is all you need to enjoy all metro and private railways across Kansai. You don't need to purchase a ticket every time, and transit is also easy. The pass comes with special offers at major facilities and stores along the railways (see below for more detail).
highlights: This pass can be used on 2 or 3 non-consecutive days within its period of validity!
Unlimited rides on subways and railways throughout Kansai
Enjoy special benefits at over 260 tourist facilities along the included routes
Get perks at 100s of facilities to save money while exploring the beauty of Kansai
Book with a Rakuten account to earn or use Rakuten Points!
important_info:
Validity Period
Exchange period: April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2026
Utilization period: April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2026



Usage days do not have to be consecutive
Each day is counted as the time from the first train to the last train, not a 24-hour period



Conditions of Use
To purchase the pass, you must be one of the following:
1. A non-Japanese tourist visiting Japan with a short-term stay status (you need to present your passport to purchase and receive the pass)
2. A Japanese national living outside of Japan (you need to be an overseas resident with a permanent resident status, or be a holder of a permanent residence card, and need to present a personal ID document)
3. Accompanying a person satisfying (1) or (2) above as a guide (you need to purchase the pass together with the person satisfying (1) or (2) above)
Note: those staying in Japan short-term for language lessons or work, and those with a long-term visa (for working holiday, work, marriage, etc.) cannot purchase or use this pass



Each pass is for one person only
You can ride certain subways and private railways in Kansai region within the validity period of the pass
You cannot use this pass on JR trains, or buses
You will need to buy a separate express ticket, special coach ticket, and/or reserved seat ticket to ride a reserved seat express train of the Nankai Electric Railway, Kinki Nippon Railway, or Semboku Rapid
Passes cannot be refunded even in the case of suspended services once they have been used
The pass can be reissued at major stations if the magnetic strip fails to function during the validity
Tickets will not be reissued if lost
If any ticket is used illegally, it will be invalidated and collected, and an additional fare will be charged, according to the regulations of each transportation company
A separate fare will be required for journeys outside the valid zone; please inquire with station staff



How to Redeem:
1. Book now to receive an e-voucher with QR Code*
2. Present the QR Code and your passport at one of these locations:
• Kansai International Airport: Kansai Tourist Information Center open 21:00–19:00 (details here)
• Osaka: EDION Namba Main Store 8F open 10:00–21:00 (details here)
• Kyoto: Kansai Tourist Information Center open 10:00–17:30 (details here)
3. Use on any 2 or 3 days, depending on the pass, from April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2026
4. Depending on the venue, you can use the included paper coupons or the Kansai Railway Pass app to avail of special benefits at attractions (only on days you use the pass)
description:
The Kansai Railway Pass is a special ticket offered only to travelers visiting Japan for sightseeing. It allows unlimited ride on all metro and private railways in Kansai, in Osaka, Kyoto, Kobe/Himeji (Hyogo), Nara, Wakayama, and Shiga Prefectures!




JR lines, Randen (Keifuku Railway) lines, and some other trains are not available. Buses are not available.




Travel efficiently around the Kansai region with this wonderful pass!




The Kansai Railway Pass (formerly Kansai Thru Pass) enables you to enjoy unlimited rides on subways and private railways (trains on the JR Lines are excluded) throughout the Kansai region! Thus, you can also explore not only Osaka, but also Kobe, Kyoto, Nara, Wakayama, and Koyasan!




Choose a 2-day or 3-day pass, which can be used on non-consecutive days within its period of validity (e.g., Monday, and Friday). In addition to the above features, you'll also enjoy the following advantages and benefits:



Travel efficiently with no hassle, as you don't have to confirm the rate for each route to buy the tickets
Special benefits at more than 260 tourist facilities along the railway lines
You can use this pass from Kansai Airport Station on the Nankai Train to reach the center of Osaka (the Limited Express Rapi:t requires an additional fee)



Book your Kansai Railway Pass for unlimited travel around the Kansai region now!




*QR Code is a registered trademark of Denso Wave, Inc.
how_it_works:
Cautions



The Kansai Railway Pass is a special ticket offered only to those traveling to Japan for sightseeing
As long as the pass has not expired, it can be used on non-consecutive days (for instance, you can use the 3-day pass for three days on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday)
In Japan, each person can purchase up to three passes
Purchasing a combination of different tickets is ok; purchase is limited to 3 tickets of all types together



How to use the pass for special offer at facilities



At select facilities, you can enjoy special offers by presenting the pass
The service detail is subject to change without notice
The special offers from facilities are valid only on the day the pass is used
Special offers are valid for only one person per pass
For more details, such as business hours, contact the facilities of the special offer
For a full list of facilities offering benefits, click here



How to Use the Pass



Insert the pass in the ticket gate with the arrow on the front pointing forward.
The day of travel and the name of the station where you begin your travel will be printed on the back of the pass. One day is counted as the duration from the first train to the last train in a day (not for 24 hours from the first use).
Insert the ticket in the ticket gate when exiting. Be sure to take your pass with you once you have passed.
Some ticket gates are not automated. In such a case, please note the date of use on the back of the pass with a permanent ink pen and show it to an attendant.
If the pass becomes unusable due to magnetic strip failure during the journey, the following stations will reissue a new pass: click here.

description:
This authentic hot air balloon free flight experience was featured in episode 6 of TV Tokyo's Drama 25, "Solo Active Girls' Recommendation 1." Enjoy an extraordinary aerial stroll while taking in the breathtaking views from a maximum altitude of 1,000 meters (depending on weather conditions). Perfect for anniversaries with loved ones, special moments with family and friends, or a luxurious morning spent alone. On the day of your experience, you'll have access to the VIP lounge. Enjoy free drinks and a relaxing atmosphere in the lounge, where you can enjoy a wide variety of balloon merchandise and materials. Arrive early to catch your breath before the early morning meeting, and savor the extraordinary experience long afterward. Your flight will be piloted by a competitive pilot, ensuring safety and security. You'll also participate in setup and cleanup, and learn about the mechanics of a hot air balloon. English support is available. A light snack set of waffles and non-alcoholic beverages will be provided, adding a special touch to your time in the sky. Following your flight, you'll receive a "boarding certificate" and a traditional champagne flight ceremony. Take this opportunity to experience a memorable, high-quality flight. Each hot air balloon can accommodate up to five adults from October to April, and up to four adults from May to September. *If you wish to board with five people between May and September, please let us know the total weight of all passengers before making your reservation and consult with us. *March 8th, 14th, and 15th are reserve days for burning reeds at the Watarase Reservoir, so reservations are accepted by request only.

title: Kyoto–Fukui Hokuriku Shinkansen & Limited Express Train Ticket[E-Ticket]
overview:
Book now for flexible express train tickets between Kyoto and Fukui — at a special price only for visitors to Japan!




Exclusive to those with temporary visitor status, take the Hokuriku Shinkansen for less! This one-way ticket is valid up to 90 days after booking. Ride the Shinkansen between Fukui and Tsuruga Station, and connect to Kyoto with the Limited Express Thunderbird!







• See all discounted Hokuriku routes here
• Check other Shinkansen routes and prices here
• See the How it Works section for full details of ticket usage!
highlights: Get Hokuriku One-Way Ticket tickets at exclusive rates!
Book with a Rakuten account to earn points for every ticket!
Exchange for reserved-seat ticket at no extra charge!
Flexible tickets valid up to 90 days after booking!
important_info:
How to Use:
1: Choose the option for your direction of travel
2: Confirmation email will be sent to the appointed email address once the booking is made
3: The QR Code* voucher for your ticket is valid up to 90 days later
4: Please go to green ticket-vending machines and pick up physical ticket by scanning QR Code of e-ticket and non-Japanese passport (click here to search)
4: Use the physical ticket to pass through the automated ticket gate




Reserving a Reserved Seat Ticket
• Reserved seat tickets can be reserved at a ticket vending machine or a station ticket office.
• Click here for information on reserving reserved seat tickets from a ticket vending machine.




Eligibility:
• "Discounted One-way Ticket" user must be holding a passport issued by a foreign government（外国での永住権を持っているお客様を含め、日本国籍のお客様は使用できません）
• "Discounted One-way Ticket" user must be entering in Japan as a "短期滞在" (Temporary Visitor)"
• "短期滞在" (Temporary Visitor) is a status of residence defined under Japanese immigration law
• Individual customers are not allowed to purchase or exchange more than one of the same ticket for the same use period
• You must present your passport to receive a pass
description:
The Hokuriku Shinkansen is a high-speed bullet train line that serves some of Japan's beautiful, less-visited cities like Fukui, Toyama, and Kanazawa. Change trains at Tsuruga Station for the Limited Express Thunderbird, which connects the travel hubs of Osaka and Kyoto!




With a Kyoto-to-Fukui (or Fukui-to-Kyoto) Hokuriku One-Way Ticket for the Shinkansen and Thunderbird Express, you’ll cruise between cities on a ride like no other!




Book your flexible tickets now, use the QR Code* to collect them in Japan, and make a seat reservation at the station in Japan, so nothing gets in the way of your bullet train adventure!




*QR Code is a registered trademark of Denso Wave, Inc.
how_it_works:
This ticket can be used on a variety of services. See the table below for the validity, and any other tickets needed.






Purchasing a "Discounted One-way Ticket"



*Available from select overseas travel agencies.
Purchasing Exchange Order through a Travel Agency outside Japan




Step 1: Purchase an exchange order (E-TICKET) at sales locations outside Japan
• Once issued, the E-TICKET must be exchanged within 3 months




Step 2: Exchange the E-TICKET for a "Discounted One-way Ticket" at an exchange location in Japan
• E-TICKET exchange locations: Click here for details
• Receiving an E-TICKET: Click here for details
• The E-TICKET that could not been exchanged can be refunded at the overseas travel agency where the pass was originally purchased (a separate handling fee will be applied)
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is not available for purchase within Japan



Availability and Use



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is available and usable all year around



Changes



• Changing the validity period start date is allowed only once
• The ticket cannot be changed for a different ticket type; the ticket must instead be refunded, and the desired ticket purchased separately
• Changing the departure or arrival station within a section is allowed only once for unused tickets within the validity period
• Changing trains is allowed only once before the departure of the designated train, and only if tickets for the train being changed to are available within the validity period



Refunds



• Refunds are accepted only at the "Discounted One-way Ticket" exchange location.
• Click here for information on refund locations for refunds due to non-availability of services, delays, or other circumstances
• The sales price minus a handling fee will be refunded.




Handling fee:
If no reserved seating ticket has been issued: 560 YEN
If a reserved seating ticket has been issued:
• Up to 2 days before departure: 560 YEN
• Between day before departure and time of departure: 30% of original undiscounted Express ticket (based on original ticket purchase date) + 220 YEN (minimum handling fee: 560 YEN)




• A handling fee equivalent to the Limited express ticket for each train will be applied to a reserved seating ticket after departure time.
• Refunds cannot be provided if the "Discounted One-way Ticket" has been used or the validity period has ended.
• A refund of an unexchanged E-TICKET is available from the overseas travel agency where the ticket was purchased. Please note that an additional handling fee will apply.



Related information



• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" is only valid for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" holder
• Presentation of a passport (of the person using the "Discounted One-way Ticket") is required to exchange an E-TICKET
• No more than one E-TICKET can be exchanged per passport
• A passport is required when using a "Discounted One-way Ticket" and must be presented to the JR staff upon request
• The "Discounted One-way Ticket" will not be reissued even if lost or stolen
• The conditions of use for the "Discounted One-way Ticket" are stipulated by the JR-WEST carriage conditions and the laws of Japan
• Conditions of use for an exchange order are stipulated by regulations and contracts of issuing companies
• If any discrepancies arise between the foreign language and Japanese versions of the carriage conditions, the Japanese version has precedence
• The information displayed (including prices) is current as of August 1, 2024, and is subject to change

title: Book a Nara Half-Day Walking Tour: Todai-ji, Nara Park & More!
overview:
Book a Nara walking tour to see Todai-ji Temple, Kofuku-ji Temple, the Nara Big Buddha, and Nara Park with an English-speaking guide. Take a fascinating journey through charming streets, delve into the heart of the old town, and learn about centuries-old landmarks.




Nara Park is also a top spot to see cherry blossoms. Book for late March for a chance to enjoy sakura 2024 on your tour!
highlights: Enjoy a half-day walking around Japan’s tranquil and historic former capital
Visit in spring for a chance to see Nara Park under a canopy of cherry blossoms
Visit two of Nara’s most important temples and get historical insights from your guide
See the incredible Great Buddha statue & discover the mysteries of Nara’s hidden pyramid
Book with a Rakuten account to earn or use points!
important_info:
This tour requires booking 4 days in advance
Please arrive at the meeting point 15 minutes early, as the tour will depart on time

If wishing to see cherry blossoms on this tour, please check the sakura forecast before booking, and book in the understanding that sakura-viewing is subject to local conditions on the day

description:
This adventure takes you to iconic sites like awe-inspiring Kofuku-ji Temple, famed for its majestic five-story pagoda, and the magnificent Todai-ji Temple, home to the imposing Great Buddha statue. Along the way, be enchanted by tales of Japanese Buddhism and the profound significance of these revered temples.




Ascend to the Nigatsu-do Hall in the grounds of Todai-ji for panoramic views of Nara's picturesque landscape, before taking a leisurely stroll through traditional souvenir shops en route to the enchanting Kasuga Shrine and Nara Park, where friendly deer roam freely.




Walk through the tranquil Nara Park, where adorable wild deer eagerly await your arrival. In spring the park boasts the blossoms of about 1,700 sakura trees, and in autumn it becomes a hot spot for viewing red autumn foliage. Marvel at the beauty of the vermilion Kasuga Shrine adorned with hundreds of lanterns, a testament to Japan's spiritual heritage.




Conclude your unforgettable journey with a delightful lunch at a local eatery, followed by a glimpse into Nara's bygone era as you step inside a historic merchant's house to discover the timeless traditions of this captivating town.




Join us for an immersive Nara historical walking tour that promises enchanting sights, cultural delights, and cherished memories that will last a lifetime.

title: Nikko Futarasan Jinja Shrine Shinkyo Bridge Admission Instant E-Ticket[E-Ticket]
overview: Book your Nikko Futarasan Jinja Shrine Shinkyo Bridge Admission Ticket and marvel at this sacred, vermilion lacquered bridge! Experience the historical and cultural significance of Shinkyo, a World Heritage site and one of Japan's finest bridges.
highlights: Get convenient e-tickets in advance
Book with a Rakuten account to earn or use points for each ticket!
Discover one of Japan's finest three bridges, renowned for its architectural beauty
Explore a World Heritage site with rich historical significance dating back to the Nara period
Admire the vermilion lacquered Shinkyo Bridge, a designated important cultural property
important_info:
Please note that the student ID must be presented when using a child ticket on site (elementary school, junior high school, or high school). If you cannot show it, you will be required to buy an adult ticket.
Free for preschool children (under 6 years old).
what_to_bring: Present the e-ticket at Nikko Futarasan Jinja Shrine Shinkyo Bridge
meeting_point: Nikko Futarasan Jinja Shrine Shinkyo Bridge
schedule:
April - October: 8:00 - 16:00
November - March: 9:30 - 15:00
cancellation: Non-refundable after you booked
description:
Vermilion lacquered sacred bridge; the beautiful Shinkyo is worth enough to decorate the entrance of the sanctuary Nikko. The Shinkyo used to be called Yamasugeno-jabashi, which means snake bridge with sedge, or Mihashi, which just means a respectable bridge. This bridge belongs to Futarasan Shrine and is designated as an important cultural property. In addition, Shinkyo was inscribed as a World Heritage site in December 1999.




The bridge is 28m in length, 7.4m in width, 10.6m in height (from the surface of the water), and includes 10 beams on both sides of the railings, each decorated with round orbs (ginkgo), painted with black paint inside the bridge deck and red paint in the other parts.




The bridge was regarded as a sacred bridge after it was built for mysterious reasons at the end of the Nara period. After it was rebuilt in Kanei's 13th year to its current appearance, it was only used for tribute coins such as generals, imperial envoys, and generally used when crossing the river. In addition, a temporary bridge (Nikko Bridge) is constructed downstream for use.




In Showa 48, in order to prepare for the 1200 year sacrificial house of Mentishantop Town in Showa 57, it was recruited to the outside world and has been extensively renovated since Heisei 9 to the present.




Shinkyo is known as one of the finest three bridges (Kintaikyo Bridge in Yamaguchi prefecture, Saruhashi Bridge in Yamanashi pref.), which is the oldest bridge in Japan in terms of drawbridge style built over the gorge.

title: Matsumoto City Museum of Art Permanent Exhibition Ticket[E-Ticket]
overview: Book your Matsumoto City Museum of Art permanent exhibition ticket and explore over 100 years of history and culture! Discover Matsumoto's rich heritage through eight themed exhibits and over 110,000 valuable items.
highlights: Get convenient e-tickets in advance
Book with a Rakuten account to earn or use points for each ticket!

important_info:

description:
This ticket allows you to visit the permanent exhibition at Matsumoto City Museum, which showcases various aspects of Matsumoto's history, culture, and nature through eight main themes. Matsumoto City Museum opened on September 21, 1906 (Meiji 39) as the "Meiji 37-38 Battle Commemoration Museum." Initially located within the Matsumoto Elementary School for Boys, it has a history of over 100 years. In 1952 (Showa 27), it became the first museum in Nagano Prefecture to be registered under the Museum Act. After several changes in name and location, in October 2023 (Reiwa 5), it reopened at its current location as a core facility of the "Comprehensive Matsumoto Museum." The museum's collection includes over 110,000 valuable items, including national important tangible folk cultural properties such as the "Peacock-shaped Bronze Chime," "Tanabata Dolls," and "Folk Beliefs Materials," as well as agricultural tools.

description:
Yabu Beach is located in Nago City, in the northern part of Okinawa Island. It's a tranquil location that hasn't been overly commercialized, where time seems to slow down. The sea stretches out before you, and lush green mountains rise behind you. You can enjoy the liberating scenery unique to this place where the sea and nature are in perfect harmony. With Clear SUP, you can feel the crystal-clear sea spreading out beneath your feet and spend a relaxing time in the calm air. Unlike flashy tourist spots, the charm of the Nago area is that you can experience "just the right Okinawa". Furthermore, drone photography is included, so you can capture a special photo that can only be seen in this location surrounded by the sea and mountains. We will give you the data on the spot so that you can create memories that can only be captured here. We will provide a thorough lecture before the experience so that even first-timers can participate with peace of mind. Depending on the sea conditions on the day, we will conduct the tour at a point where you can enjoy it safely. - How to use ① About the meeting place Depending on the sea conditions and wind direction on the day, the meeting place may be changed to one of the following. ⸻② Reception and Lecture (approx. 10 minutes) After reception, we will explain how to use the SUP and kayak and give you some instructions. Even first-timers can participate with peace of mind. ⸻③ Experience (approx. 50 minutes) Enjoy cruising in the crystal-clear sea. Drone photography will also be done during this time. ⸻④ Dismissal (approx. 10 minutes) After the experience is over, we will give you the photos and videos and dismiss you. ⸻Notes ・The content and area of ​​the event may change depending on the weather and sea conditions on the day. ・If there are any changes, we will contact you by the day before. ・Please be sure to register a phone number where you can be reached when making your reservation. • For customers from overseas, we will contact you via the messaging function on the reservation website. Please be sure to check it.

title: Make Sushi & Dashimaki (Japanese Omelet) at Sasebo, Nagasaki
overview: At a long-running seafood restaurant in Sasebo, learn to make bite-sized nigiri sushi, rolled maki sushi, and 'dashimaki' (Japanese rolled omelet)! You'll wear a sushi chef's uniform and use fresh ingredients from Sasebo Fish Market.
highlights: Make dashimaki omelet and two different types of sushi with fresh, locally sourced ingredients
Enjoy the fruits of your labor for lunch, and take home English-language recipes afterward
Flexible options — for the sushi toppings, choose from either raw fish or cooked ingredients
Appreciate Sasebo's specialties: not only seafood, but also Mikawachi porcelain
An immersive experience — wear a sushi chef's uniform as you make food in a traditional Japanese room
important_info:
• Participants must be aged 5 or older
• Guests aged 18 and under must be accompanied by a guardian
• A minimum of 2 participants is required for us to accept your booking
• We cannot accommodate food allergies; if you are allergic to seafood, soy, and/or eggs, please do not book this activity
• If you want to make 2 additional pieces of maguro (tuna) nigiri sushi, please book the relevant add-on before checkout. Please note that this type of sushi uses raw fish
description:
At a renowned, long-running seafood restaurant in Sasebo, Nagasaki, wear a sushi chef's uniform and make two different types of sushi, plus 'dashimaki' (Japanese rolled omelet)! Being a port town, Sasebo is known for its fresh seafood, so you will use fresh ingredients from Sasebo Fish Market for this experience.




For the sushi toppings, choose from either raw fish or cooked ingredients. After making dashimaki, you will make bite-sized nigiri sushi (also spelled 'nigiri-zushi') and rolled maki sushi (also spelled 'maki-zushi), as well as dashimaki.




Then, sit down to feast on the fruits of your labor for lunch! Take in the ambiance of the venue — a traditional Japanese room — and appreciate Sasebo's specialties: not only seafood, but also its homegrown, high-quality porcelain, Mikawachi ware. Your sushi and dashimaki will be served with soy sauce in an elegant Mikawachi porcelain dish.




Finally, after the activity, take home some English-language recipes so that you can make sushi yourself at home!

This is a plan to cruise the ocean of Miyako Island in the early morning by SUP/canoe. description:
Sunrise SUP or Canoeing (Stand Up Paddle Boarding) experience at the most beautiful beach in Miyako Island!




★Sunrise Plan
Early morning SUP or canoeing experience on a spectacular beach in Miyako Island. Refresh yourself with the fresh morning air and the beautiful scenery that will clear your mind! Since it is held early in the morning, you can join it on the last day of your trip before you go back home.




★What is SUP?
SUP is another name for stand-up paddle boarding. It is a popular water sport in Hawaii and has been gaining popularity around the world in recent years. Even if you have not experienced it yet, you may have seen it on social media or TV. Standing on a board and paddling through the ocean, SUP is the latest way to enjoy the ocean and is recommended for people of all ages!




★What is *Canoeing?
A classic marine activity! Canoes are very stable and simple to operate as a vehicle on the water. It is very stable as a vehicle on the water and very simple to operate, so everyone can experience it safely.




During the experience, the guide will take pictures with a waterproof digital camera so that you can take home memories of your trip. The photos will be shared with you after the experience, so you do not need to bring your own camera to enjoy the activity to the fullest.




Most of the participants are beginners, so please feel free to join the tour without any hesitation. Our guides will give you a detailed lecture so that you can enjoy SUP or canoeing without any worries.

title: Treasuring Honor at Osaka Castle: Deep Samurai Life Bushido Experience
overview:
Experience a rare deep dive into the world of the samurai, complete with armor and a visit to a castle that has witnessed both prosperity and upheaval.




Delve into the history of the Tenkyojin and the Tokugawa, and learn about the famous castle that stands as a testament to their glory. Become a samurai of the Toyotomi Clan, learn bushido, and enter the battle of Osaka Castle! Close-up professional photography is included, so you won't miss a moment of this extraordinary and precious experience.




This immersive experience includes donning a traditional kimono, practicing the art of calligraphy, participating in a formal tea ceremony, learning swordsmanship and trying trial cutting, and finally, wearing samurai armor for a memorable photo opportunity on the grounds of Osaka Castle.
highlights: Don samurai armor and take photos on the grounds of Osaka Castle
Express your whole body dynamically and put your soul into your samurai character
Learn how to use a sword and try your hand at trial cutting ("tameshigiri")
Wear a kimono, a symbol of Japan, and experience traditional Zen culture
Practice calligraphy and learn the art of samurai calligraphy
important_info:
Suitable for ages 6+
Please bring undershirts and underpants (t-shirts are acceptable) to wear as innerwear for hakama and kimono; we have a few innerwear available for loan, but sizes are limited
Japanese swords are handled by a master with 10 years of swordsmanship experience, who will instruct you before you try out the sword
Staff who can speak two or three languages (English, Spanish, and Japanese) will provide interpretation
A welcome drink is provided: taste matcha, a traditional Japanese popular beverage (quench your thirst with iced green matcha during the summer months)



About Calligraphy
The following information is required by the calligraphy teachers in preparation for the activity; please inform us of the following information before the day of the experience:



Your full name
Email address to send the photo shooting data to



Optional Add-On



Hanging Scrolls: For an additional 2,200 JPY, add a hanging scroll featuring traditional Japanese art with 1300 years of history; challenge yourself to write the kanji you have practiced on the hanging scroll or ask a master to write your mastery skills on it as a souvenir
description:
The experience lasts 180 minutes (210 minutes for "Harakiri Plan") and offers a full Zen and Bushido learning experience. Calligraphy and tea ceremony are closely related to Zen, while Bushido represents an awareness of the mindset (swordsmanship) and way of life (fighting in armor) of the samurai in a samurai society.




Activity 1: Wearing a Kimono
Time required: 15 minutes
Wearing a kimono, a symbol of Japan, you will experience traditional culture. "Zen" is alive and well in our traditional culture of calligraphy and tea ceremony and is imbued with the aesthetic sense of Japanese culture. By learning these arts, one can acquire the spirit of courtesy, manners, beautiful gestures, and hospitality. Before experiencing this, you will be asked to change into a kimono. Men are invited to wear "hakama" (traditional Japanese trousers) and "tabi" (split-toe socks) and become a samurai. Women may wear a gorgeous kimono and become a "Yamato Nadeshiko" (an idealized Japanese woman). Kimonos for children over 6 years old are also available.




Activity 2: Learning and Practicing Calligraphy
Time required: 30 minutes
Participants practice how to hold a brush and how to use tools. Then, students practice how to hold the brush and use the tools.




Activity 3: Samurai Calligraphy
Time required: 15 minutes
When a samurai foresees his death, he looks back on his life so far and writes his last words on earth with a brush. The participants will write the kanji they have just chosen, paying attention to the technique.




Activity 4: Samurai Tea Ceremony
Time required: 30 minutes
The master of the tea ceremony prepares and serves powdered green tea to the guests. You will learn various things such as etiquette, manners, beautiful gestures, hospitality, and traditional Japanese culture.




Activity 5: Samurai Sword Trial Cutting
Time required: 30 minutes
You will learn how to use a sword and become aware of your self-respect and the responsibility of carrying a deadly weapon. At that time, you will be allowed to wear a serious sword. Participants will try their hand at sword cutting with a samurai sword. Experience the best cutting edge in the world.




Activity 6: Wearing Armor to Battle!
Time required: 60 minutes
It is April 1615, and the Osaka Summer Battle has begun. You, a member of Toyotomi's side, wear armor and go into battle. Pretend to be the best soldier in Japan and take pictures on the grounds of Osaka Castle!

Total

description:
Located about 15 minutes by car from Naha Airport, Clear SUP takes place at "Kamiji," a location with excellent accessibility yet boasts crystal-clear waters. Despite its proximity to urban areas, stepping out to sea offers a truly extraordinary experience. The tour features routes that pass under bridges and cruising in calm waters, allowing you to easily enjoy the Okinawan sea. Clear SUP lets you relax and feel the ocean stretching out beneath your feet. Furthermore, drone photography is included, capturing a special moment from the sky. You'll receive a video on the spot, creating a immersive memory of your experience with the Okinawan sea. Thorough instruction is provided before the tour, ensuring even first-timers can participate with confidence. The tour is conducted at a safe and enjoyable location, depending on the day's sea conditions. Its appeal lies in the fact that you can easily participate even with limited time, such as on your arrival or departure day. ⸻How to Use ① Meeting Point: Kameji (Urasoe City) - Approximately 15 minutes by car from Naha Airport - Please arrive 15 minutes before your reservation time - If you are unsure of the location, please feel free to call us. ⸻② Registration & Lecture (approximately 10 minutes) After registration, we will explain how to use the SUP and important points on the beach. Even first-timers can participate with peace of mind. ⸻③ Experience (approximately 50 minutes) Enjoy cruising in calm waters. Drone photography will also be conducted during this time. ⸻④ Dismissal (approximately 10 minutes) After the experience is over, we will hand over the photographic data and dismiss you. ⸻Notes - The content and area of ​​the event may change depending on the weather and sea conditions on the day. - If there are any changes, we will contact you by the day before. - There are no changing rooms or shower facilities. - Please use public restrooms, etc. to change clothes. - There are simple facilities for washing your feet. - Please be sure to register a phone number where you can be reached when making a reservation. - For customers from overseas, we will contact you via the message function on the reservation site. description:
Wavering Boundaries




Fascinated by goldfish and unwavering in his commitment to creative endeavors, artist Riusuke Fukahori has been crafting three-dimensional goldfish using an innovative technique known as 2.5D painting, in which acrylic paint is layered on transparent resin. His works offer an experience of realism, as if beautiful, lifelike goldfish are swimming right before your eyes.




Is it the virtual or the real, or illusion or reality, that lies within the ripples of the water’s surface? While Fukahori presents his works as if they are alive, he simultaneously confronts the fact that they are merely layers of lifeless paint. Lying within his creations is a fundamental proposition in realism: the coexistence of illusion and material substance.




This exhibition showcases approximately 300 works, including his early three-dimensional pieces, paintings, videos, and large-scale installations, including new creations. In addition, it shines a renewed light on his artistry in portraying goldfish, exploring his thoughts on the act of painting and the essence of reality.




Enjoy the lively world of goldfish, which invites us to drift between the virtual and the real.



About Riusuke Fukahori



Born in Aichi Prefecture in 1973, Riusuke Fukahori graduated from the Department of Crafts and Media Design of Aichi Prefectural University of Fine Arts and Music in 1995. Facing a creative impasse and on the brink of abandoning his artistic career, he became fascinated for the first time by a pet goldfish that he had neglected for seven years and began painting goldfish. His unique transcendent skill has earned him tremendous acclaim in Japan and abroad.



Event Details



Title:
Riusuke Fukahori Exhibition: Wavering Boundaries

• Closed: July 7 (Mon)

description:
Lake Kussharo, the world's largest caldera lake, is a huge depression created by volcanic eruptions that has become a beautiful lake and is a rich field where many wild animals live. Even now, the hot springs bubbling along the lake make you feel the power of the earth. On a day with stable winds, we head to Oyakotsu Jigoku, the tip of the Wakoto Peninsula, to make hot spring eggs! Experience the feeling of being one with nature, rocked by the water, blown by the wind, and warmed by the hot springs. Why not explore the watery world of Lake Kussharo, a caldera lake created by volcanic eruption, on a SUP? The appeal of SUP is that you are close to the water and it is easy to feel one with nature. We hope you will feel the transparency of the water of Lake Kussharo, the strength of the volcano, and the comfort of the breeze! ~Schedule for the day~ 9:00 Meet at Lake Kussharo Wakoto Peninsula After filling out the participation consent form, we will introduce the field. We will give a lecture on how to operate the SUP and how to use the equipment safely. 9:20 Let's go! First, you will experience a menu to get used to SUP near the shore. Once you get used to it, you will explore around Wakoto Peninsula with a guide! 11:30 We will disband at the Wakoto Peninsula parking lot. Thank you for your hard work! I recommend the soft ice cream and potato mochi from the shop "Miyazaki" on Wakoto Peninsula! I can't get enough of something sweet after exercising!

overview:
Why not try SUP at Minatohama Beach in Miyagi Prefecture?
Anyone with a desire to have fun is welcome to join us!
　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　
After a lecture on basic SUP knowledge and paddling techniques on the beach, practice begins on the water!
Start from a sitting position and when you get used to it, you can finally try paddling standing up!
Enjoy the new view from the sea!
highlights: Beginners are welcome! Instructors will guide you carefully and thoroughly.
Beach cleaning experience is also available! (only for those who wish).
Instructors and staff will take photos of you and give you the data!
Free pick-up from Tagajo Station on the JR Senseki Line!
important_info:
・Age for participation　Elementary school children - 65 years old.
・Please arrive at the meeting point 10 minutes before the reserved time with your clothes changed.
・Free pick-up and drop-off from Tagajo Station on the JR Senseki Line.
Please let us know at the time of booking if you wish to use this service.
*We plan to meet at Tagajo Station 40 minutes before the start time.
(The journey to Minatohama Beach is scheduled to take approximately 15-20 minutes, with a change of clothes for the experience taking approximately 10 minutes).
・Please inform us in advance if you have any pre-existing medical conditions or are taking any medication.
・Pregnant women are not permitted.
・Please do not participate if you are not feeling well or have been drinking or have had a drink.
・Wetsuits are available to rent (at an additional fee). Please let us know if you wish to use a wetsuit.
・If we judge it to be dangerous due to weather and sea conditions, we may change or cancel it regardless of whether it is experienced beforehand or during the experience.
・Please note that the activity may be held on the Omotehama beach (Foreigners' beach) depending on the weather and sea conditions.
・Please participate on the premise that you will fall into the water because it is an activity on the sea.
・The wearing of life jackets is mandatory, and staff members are constantly watching over you from the sea and on land.
description:
SIJ (SUP Instructors of Japan) certified instructors and SUPA (Stand Up Paddleboard Association) certified instructors provide thorough and detailed instruction on the basics, such as how to stand on the board and hold the paddle.




[POINT 1] Beginners are welcome! Experience is available from just one person!
Even complete beginners can have fun.
The board may wobble at first, but you will be able to keep your balance while paddling!




For beginners, instructors will carefully and thoroughly guide you through the basics, such as where to stand on the board and how to hold the paddle!




[POINT 2] SDGs Beach clean-up experience! (For those who wish to participate)
After experiencing SUP, experience a beach clean-up!
While cleaning the beach, think about SDG Goal 14: "Protect the richness of the oceans"!
We will prepare the beach clean-up bags and tongs.




[POINT 3] We will provide photo data of the experience.
We will provide you with photographic data of your experience.
'I don't feel comfortable taking photos on the board' or 'I can't take my camera with me as it would get wet'
You don't need to worry about this! Our instructors and staff will take good photos of you!




[POINT 4] Free pick-up from Tagajo Station on the JR Senseki Line!
If you wish, we offer free pick-up from Tagajo Station on the JR Senseki Line.
Please let us know at the time of booking if you wish to use this service.




[POINT 5] "Minatohama Beach Seaside Rest Area" available!
Changing rooms, toilets and water showers are available.
*Water showers will be available from 1 July until 30 September.
*Only if the event is held at Minatohama Beach.

title: Make "Mizuhiki" Knotwork Accessories in Kyoto
overview: Get creative in Kyoto and learn to turn colorful mizuhiki strings into unique accessories — a perfectly match to your personal style or even a kimono. These delicate, hand-tied accessories are not only adorable but also make thoughtful souvenirs or gifts for loved ones. Book now to discover the art of mizuhiki!
highlights: Reserve with a Rakuten account to earn or use points for each booking!
Learn to make those charming accessories you see in Japanese souvenir shops
Finish early? Make a second accessory for no extra charge!
Enjoy a complimentary mizuhiki gift to take home
important_info: • Children under elementary school age are not permitted to participate
what_to_bring: Nothing! We provide everything you need.

cancellation:

Same day: You pay 100% of experience price

description:
With a rainbow of vibrant mizuhiki strings to choose from, creating your own accessories is fun and easy. Except there are so many options you might have trouble choosing!




Whether you're making straps, earrings, charms, or brooches, you’ll enjoy the hands-on experience of crafting something beautiful.




Mizuhiki is a traditional Japanese craft that involves tying colorful, thin cords into intricate knots, often used for decorations and gift-wrapping in Japan. These cords symbolize connection, and play an important part in Japanese culture.




By learning the art of mizuhiki, you’re not only creating beautiful, personalized accessories but also experiencing a piece of Japan’s rich cultural heritage. It's a fun, hands-on activity that allows you to express your creativity while making meaningful souvenirs or gifts to take home. Whether you’re a craft enthusiast or just looking for a unique cultural experience, mizuhiki is a must-try!
how_it_works: Pick your favorite accessory design from the samples, then practice the art of tying traditional knots. With dozens of mizuhiki varieties available, you'll select your favorite and create a one-of-a-kind accessory to cherish.

overview:
This is a charter plan to enjoy a snorkeling and fishing trolling experience on Minnajima Island!




Explore the fascinating underwater world of clear water and white sand at Minnajima Island and experience the beauty of colorful fish and corals.




In addition, you can experience trolling from the boat to target tuna, bonito, Cirrus, Spanish mackerel, and more!
If you are lucky, you may even have a chance to catch a powerful marlin!




Experience an unforgettable day combining the natural beauty of Minnajima Island with the adventure of the sea!
highlights: Charter plan to enjoy snorkeling & trolling experience on Minnajima Island!

We'll support you behind the scenes as you create lasting memories.☆・Don't worry about making mistakes on the electric potter's wheel! Use as much clay as you like.♪♪ Firing costs are included in the basic fee, so the total cost is surprisingly reasonable.♪ Choose two pieces from your creations and fire them. You can also add more as an option!・Additional firings are also available! If you would like to fire more than two pieces, please let us know at the end of the session.・Additional bases, flowerpot drainage, and ashtrays are included in the basic fee. Enjoy the basic plan to your heart's content.♪・If you make a mistake, we'll offer advice on shaping, but don't dwell on it too much; it's okay to fail again. Failure is a step toward improvement, so enjoy it, too.♪・In addition to the unlimited clay, you can also select other options.・The owner is a perennial beginner surfer who loves hot springs, fishing, and traveling. Please feel free to come! ☆■□ Pottery Experience □■ ① Check-in. Free parking available. We'll greet you with a smile as soon as we see you. Please state your name. ② Confirm your plan. After donning a complimentary rental apron, confirm the contents and price of the experience, and then begin. ③ Begin the experience. Even first-timers are supported. The lecture is tailored to your experience level. If you have any requests, such as "I want it to be like this," please feel free to ask. ④ An announcement will be made 10 minutes before the end. You may even create a great piece just before the end. Good luck. After your piece is complete, it will be semi-dried, then carved, dried, bisque-fired, glazed (colored), fired, sanded, and finished. ⑤ Delivery. It will take approximately 1-2 months to complete. You can pick it up in person or choose delivery with an additional option. ☆ * Completion time varies depending on the shape and thickness of the piece. ■□ What You Can Make □■ [Potwheel] Create a beautifully shaped piece. Sake cups, tea bowls, teacups, mugs (handles are an option for 400 yen), vases, and more. The difficulty of making each piece varies. *The larger the piece, the more difficult it becomes. Feel free to start with something small! *Cancellation policy applies

description:
★100% chance of spotting a sea turtle★ [Tour Details] The Blue Cave is a popular snorkeling spot. The light that penetrates the cave is transformed into a pale blue light due to its wavelength, creating a mystical glow throughout the cave. This tour features a beach entry! After a photo stop in front of the Blue Cave, you'll be guided by snorkel to the sea turtle's nest. (Note: This tour prioritizes time spent searching for and swimming with sea turtles, so photos will be taken in front of the cave rather than inside.) This fascinating tour offers fantastical views of beautiful coral and tropical fish, as well as the chance to encounter sea turtles! ★RUANOA Ishigaki Island Tour Highlights: 1. Reservations accepted the day before or on the day. 2. Free photos and 4K video (approximately 10-20 photos per group). 3. Free transfers (city area only). 4. Free snorkel equipment rental. 5. Free wetsuit rental. 6. Beginners and children welcome. 7. Easy beach entry. ◆ The Blue Cave is a weather-sensitive spot. description: ◆The best swimming season has arrived. The water is clear and perfect for the season. [Departing Chiba] "Nearest Station Near Your Hotel" [Important] Same-day reservations are only accepted if our staff is on-site, so please contact us by email or phone to confirm before applying. ◆Safety First◆ There have been a recent increase in snorkeling accidents. This plan is not an assembly line, but rather a small group of up to four people (to ensure safety, four people will be split into groups and enter the water in pairs). For a relaxed and comfortable experience, we will provide a slightly longer lecture to ensure you have fully mastered the skills, and then we will adjust the program to your level. Due to the small group size and lengthy lecture, the participation fee is slightly higher. Please consider this plan if you agree with the purpose. Thank you. ★Overview★ In the summer, the beach opens, and the snorkeling area is limited and crowded. Changing rooms and showers are also available. (Charged) (Information will be provided after application). Let's enjoy the waters of Moriya Beach, Chiba Prefecture's most popular beach. (Schedule) Meet at the designated parking lot (parking is at your own expense). A lecture will be given for first-time snorkelers. 1. Sizing of rental equipment (mask, snorkel). *Fins are prohibited during the swimming season due to Katsuura City regulations. 2. Instructions on snorkeling will be given on land. 3. Put on your equipment and practice in shallow waters where you can stand upright. 4. Watch for fish in shallow waters where you can stand upright. Once you can swim, move on to another area. *Wear a wetsuit (additional 2,000 yen) to protect yourself from sunburn and ensure buoyancy. Even if you can't swim, it's okay! ★Even if you can't swim, you can breathe with your face in the water using a snorkel. ★We limit groups to a maximum of four people per session, so you can rest assured. ★Our staff will be happy to assist you with the following: ★・Want to try real snorkeling? ・Want to learn the correct skills due to the high incidence of snorkeling accidents. ・Want to snorkel to your heart's content. ・Want to try it at a popular summer beach.・I want to practice before doing serious snorkeling at a resort in the Maldives. ・I have the equipment, but it's difficult to carry around. ・Underwater commemorative photos included (data) ★Recommended points of this plan★ ・Small group size to accommodate customer concerns and ensure thorough skill practice. ★

overview:
Mangrove SUP/Canoe (Kayak) + Splash Canyoning
This course allows you to enjoy nature to the maximum level by SUP/canoe cruising in mangroves including the Urauchi River and canyoning (river rafting) in the Omija River.
Let's enjoy the great nature as much as you want by cruising in the mangrove forest by SUP/canoe (kayak) and jumping into the river by canyoning!
SUP/canoe field will be guided to the best location depending on the wind, tide level, and crowd on the day of the tour.
highlights: Recommended activity for beginners!
Free photo data, equipment rental, and hot shower♪
Includes special offers for participants (coupons for restaurants, etc., and information on good spots)!
More than 300,000 people have participated in this tour! Safe and secure tour!
9:00 start tour is available for one day trip from Ishigaki Island!
important_info:
-Meeting time may change depending on the season and field conditions on the day of the tour. The store will contact you the day before the tour for the meeting place and time.
-The tour will be held rain or shine. However, we may cancel the tour if we deem that the field is unsafe.
-Please change into clothes that you don't mind getting wet before the tour.
-Please use the restroom before coming to the tour. (There are no restrooms near the tour site.)
-Age range is from 6 years old to 59 years old.
-If you have children between 6 and 11 years old, please choose canoeing.
-We will guide you to the best field depending on the wind direction, tide level, and crowds on the day of the tour.
There is a possibility that you may be guided in the ocean instead of the mangroves.
-Tours are available in Japanese or English.
description:
Enjoy the nature of Iriomote Island with the popular perfect river experience! You can fully enjoy two river activities, canoeing or SUP and splash canyoning, in the clear waters of the mangrove forests of Iriomote Island, rich in nature.




Splash Canyoning
Splash canyoning is a super-exhilarating activity that takes you down a canyon in a single bound! Safety measures are taken by wearing life jackets and helmets! You can enjoy the exhilarating thrill of splashing down the canyon and jumping into a waterfall.




⭑World Heritage Mangrove SUP/Canoe
Mangrove cruising allows you to enjoy the natural mangrove forests of Iriomote Island by SUP or canoe.




SUP (Stand Up Paddleboard) is a new activity that has been attracting attention in recent years, in which you stand on the board and paddle. Of course, you can also enjoy it sitting down.




Canoeing is recommended for those who want to relax while experiencing the rich nature of Iriomote Island. It can be enjoyed by people of all ages, from small children to the elderly.




The perfect river experience where you can enjoy Iriomote Island to the fullest is an experience you should not miss when you come to Okinawa!

description:
Embark on a journey to Miyajima, known as the island of the gods. ride the ferry from Hiroshima to behold the iconic Otorii Gate and visit the ancient Itsukushima Shrine, dating back to the Heian period. Explore Miyajima's culture at a museum before relishing a Japanese lunch featuring Hiroshima Prefecture's finest ingredients.




Experience the island's history at Daisho-in Temple, followed by a tranquil tea or coffee break amidst nature. Learn about the rich history and culture. Gain insight into the differences between the Japanese Shinto religion and Buddhism from a local guide.




Delight in local delicacies like fresh oysters and momiji dumplings. The tour culminates at a souvenir shop street, offering mementos of your unforgettable Miyajima adventure.

title: Noboribetsu Marine Park NIXE Instant Tickets [E-Ticket]
overview: Book Noboribetsu Marine Park NIXE instant e-tickets and use up to 180 days later! Enjoy Hokkaido's largest aquarium and a surprising castle setting. Experience dolphin shows, sea lion performances, and the charming penguin parade year-round!
highlights: Get flexible e-tickets valid up to 180 days after booking!
Explore Hokkaido's largest aquarium, housed in a picturesque castle
Enjoy year-round animal shows, including dolphins, sea lions, and penguins
Book with a Rakuten account to earn or use points for each ticket!
important_info:
• Ages 0–3 enter for free
• Penguin parades and other activities may be suspended due to the weather or their health condition
Using Tickets
• Receive your QR Code* e-ticket/voucher by email shortly after booking
• Your e-ticket/voucher is valid up to 180 days after booking
• Please present the QR Code to exchange for your ticket at the entrance of Noboribetsu Marine Park NIXE
*QR Code is a registered trademark of Denso Wave, inc.
description:
Dive into a World of Wonder at Noboribetsu Marine Park Nixe




Looking for unique things to do in Noboribetsu beyond its famous hot springs? Step into the enchanting world of Noboribetsu Marine Park Nixe, a one-of-a-kind destination that’s home to over 20,000 aquatic creatures from 400 different species. With its charming Danish castle-inspired exterior, this marine park is more than just an aquarium — it’s a magical experience for visitors of all ages.




Unforgettable Animal Encounters

Get ready to be wowed by the park’s lively animal shows! Watch playful sea lions, fur seals, and dolphins showcase their talents, or catch the adorable penguin parade as these charming birds waddle their way into your heart. Don’t miss the feeding sessions held several times a day, where you can see these fascinating creatures up close during their mealtime.




Explore the “Castle Under the Sea”

Noboribetsu Marine Park Nixe isn’t your typical aquarium — it’s a true underwater wonderland. Stroll through the Aqua Tunnel, a mesmerizing glass-domed walkway that makes you feel like you’re walking through a magical underwater castle.




Marvel at the Kaleidoscope of Goldfish, where vibrant goldfish swim in illuminated domes, their movements reflected by mirrors to create a dazzling display. At the Milky Way Pool, watch 10,000 shimmering sardines swirl in unison, sparkling like stars in the night sky. The park also boasts Hokkaido’s largest jellyfish tank, a hypnotic sight that’s sure to leave you in awe.




For those who love reptiles and amphibians, the park’s dedicated house is home to fascinating creatures like anacondas, tortoises, and frogs — perfect for fans of the scaly and slimy!




More Fun Awaits

The excitement doesn’t stop with the marine life! Noboribetsu Marine Park Nixe also features an on-site amusement park. For just 100 JPY per ride, you can enjoy a variety of attractions that add even more fun to your visit.




Book Your Tickets Today

Skip the hassle of buying tickets on-site and book with us for a seamless experience. Plus, take advantage of our special package that includes coupons worth 1,000 JPY, which you can use at the park’s shops and restaurants (some restrictions may apply).




Whether you’re traveling with family, friends, or on your own, Noboribetsu Marine Park Nixe promises a day filled with wonder, fun, and unforgettable memories. Don’t miss out — reserve your tickets now!

overview: Book online for Nikkosan Chuzenji Temple (Tochigi Rinnoji Temple) tickets, and earn points with each order for Rakuten members! This admission ticket grants entry to the Nikkosan Chuzenji Temple, home of the incredible Tachiki Kannon statue carved out of a still-standing tree!
highlights: Flexible tickets — book now, use up to 180 days later!
See the incredible "Tachiki-Kannon" a huge Buddhist statue carved from a still-standing tree

important_info:
