description:
Chuzenji is a temple built by Shodo Shonin in 784, a World Heritage Site of Nikkozan Rinnoji. It is said that Shodo Shonin saw Senju Kannon on Lake Chuzenji and carved it on a Katsura tree in the Gohonzon "Eleven-faced Senju Kanzeon Bosatsu" (National Important Cultural Property). This Kannon is still rooted in the ground, and welcomes visitors with a gentle expression. In addition, many pilgrims will also worship at the 18th temple of Bando Sanjusankannon Sacred Ground.




Gohonzon "Tachiki-Kannon" is the principal image of Chuzen-ji Temple, Nikko-san. The eleven-faced thousand-armed Kannon Bodhisattva (National Important Cultural Property). In Buddhism, there is a "guardian deity" for the year of birth, who protects the child for the rest of his/her life. The blessings of Senju Kannon are said to be "fulfillment of various wishes," and she is the Buddha who fulfills various wishes. Therefore, please stand in front of Kannon and tell her what you wish for. Since they are wishes, they can be about anything. If you are ill, please pray for a speedy recovery. If you are taking an entrance examination, please pray that you will pass the exam. Please come and tell Kannon-sama what you are wishing for at that moment.




The temple is a prayer hall where the Five Great Myoo's (Fudo Myoo, Kusansei Myoo, Gundari Myoo, Daimeitoku Myoo, and Kongoyasha Myoo) are enshrined. On the ceiling, a large cloud dragon painted by painter Kenzan Nanpu, a member of the Japan Art Academy, boasts a majestic appearance. The view of Lake Chuzenji from Godaido is so spectacular that it will shake the heart of anyone who sees it.

description:
Kashiwaya is a kaiseki restaurant located in Suita/Settsu, Osaka Prefecture, with a total seating capacity of 40 seats. Mr. Matsuo, a creative Japanese cuisine producer in Osaka, not only maintains the traditional cooking style but also constantly tries new ideas. In Osaka, known as the Japanese food utopia, Kashiwaya selects seasonal fresh ingredients to serve you exquisite dishes.







In the traditional Japanese garden, you can taste not only the food but also the traditional style. Orthodox kaiseki has a tranquil atmosphere. The architectural design is by the famous Japanese designer Toshinori Nakamura. One word painting, one flower vessel, and one courtyard immediately bring people into the Zen mood.







Away from the hustle and bustle of the city, enjoy the delicious gifts of the four seasons in a quiet and elegant Japanese-style wooden shop.

Children 0–3 years old will not be provided seats on the Shinkansen and at the Grand Sumo Tournament spectator seating area. They must sit on the lap of their parent or guardian. Children 4–5 years old will not be provided seats on the Shinkansen. They must sit on the lap of their parent or guardian. If required, please make a booking for them at the child rate or pay directly on site.

From the venue's wheelchair storage area, please go to the spectator seating area on your own or with the help of an accompanying group member. Please note that the National Government Licensed English Guide Interpreter cannot provide assistance or help.
Wheelchairs cannot be used in the spectator seating area arranged for this tour.



Important Notes/Supplements: Regarding Luggage



Suitcases and other large pieces of luggage cannot be brought into the seating area. In addition, baggage cannot be stored at the meeting location. Please make sure to store luggage at another location on your own in advance (coin lockers, etc.) or at your accommodation.

The Shinkansen schedule may vary slightly due to unforeseen conditions.

Changes may not be made to the scheduled Shinkansen. Customers who miss the designated Shinkansen may board a later departing Shinkansen in the non-reserved car section only (same day only).

title: Visit of Shogun’s Shrine Toshogu, Tea farm visit and Sushi Lunch
overview: On this 7-hour tour, we can visit a tea farm to taste Shizuoka's authentic green tea and the historical Shogun's Shrine, Kunozan Toshogu to learn Japanese history and culture. From the Nihondaira hill, a view of majestic Mt. Fuji can be expected if the weather allows. Later, let's enjoy sushi at Shimizu Port, which boasts one of the largest tuna catches in Japan.
highlights: Tea Farm
Nihondaira (a view of Mt. Fuji)
Kunozan Toshogu Shrine (which is dedicated Shogun Ieyasu Tokugawa)
Sushi Lunch
Sushi Museum (Shimizu Port is Japan's No.1 catch of tuna)
important_info:
Please wear comfortable shoes.
There are many steps to climb to visit the shrine.
description:
Shizuoka City has a lot of places to visit and enjoyed. Not only a great view of Mt. Fuji but you can see the beautiful nature and learn Japanese history and culture. In this tour, we’ll visit one of the historical sites, Kunozan Toshogu and if the weather allows you will see a magnificent view of Mt. Fuji and enjoy the taste of green tea at a tea farm and Sushi at Shimizu port. Shizuoka is the No.1 grean tea producer and boasts the No.1 catch of tuna in Japan.




● Tea Farm
Shizoka is the No.1 tea producer in Japan. On the way to Nihondaira, we'll visit a tea farm to have a look at their tea field and taste Shizuoka's green tea. If the weather is fine, you can enjoy a view of Mt. Fuji from thier tea field.




● Kunozan Toshogu Shrine
The shrine is one of the most popular sightseeing spots in Shizuoka City. The main building was designated as a national treasure in 2010. It is worth seeing. The shrine is dedicated to the first Shogun, Tokugawa Ieyasu, who was the founder of the Edo government. You may find out Ieyasu Tokugawa’s message of peace if you visit there. We can visit the shrine by 5-minute ride of an aerial gondola.




● Nihondaira Hill
A panoramic view of the port, Miho pine grove and Mt. Fuji can be enjoyed from Nihondaira, if the weather allows.




● Sushi museum is located in the shopping mall facility, S-Pulse Dream Plaza at Shimizu Port attracting many people from home and abroad. You can enjoy the authentic sushi there, because there are many sushi restaurants gathering in the facility. If you like, you can drop by a Sushi museum there.
how_it_works:
7-hour tour is my recommendation including a tea farm visit, Toshogu shrine and sushi lunch. Other than that, I'll arrange a plan depending on your interest and request. Please feel free to let me know.

title: Ride Along Drifting Experience & Replica Police Car Chase Tokyo Drift
overview:
This is the ultimate adrenaline-pumping JDM ride along drifting experience and replica police car chase tour TOKYO DRIFT!! by Matenro Drift Racing




We will take you on a ride on one of our JDM drift cars. You will experience donuts, figure 8s, and high speed turns on the passenger seat of an expert OG drift driver.




The drift cars that will be used are Silvia S14, S15, Nissan 180SX, 350Z, Miata MX5, Toyota JZX100, Mazda RX-8, and more. We also have a one of a kind replica undercover police car! Cars will vary on the day, but we will have at least 3 cars from the above on site. Our motto is to provide a safe yet exhilarating experience. We take all necessary safety measures to ensure a secure environment for our guests.




We also assist with filming, let’s create the most unforgettable highlight of your trip together!
highlights: One of a kind ride along drifting experience and police car chase in Japan!
Get chased by a replica police car or chase down a drifting JDM drift car

meeting_point: Tokyo Station Marunouchi North Exit
cancellation: Free cancellation
description:
Experience a movie-style drift car chase in Tokyo: choose to ride in a Japanese drift machine being pursued by an undercover police car, or ride the replica police car and chase down a JDM drift car. This high-octane “Tokyo Drift” experience is led by OG Japanese drift drivers, uses real drift-built cars, and delivers an adrenaline-filled private tour just outside Tokyo/Chiba. Ideal for car and drift culture fans looking for more than a standard sightseeing ride.




With this experience, first we will take you on a ride on one of our JDM drift cars. You will experience donuts, figure 8s, and high speed e-braking turns on the passenger seat of an expert OG drift driver.




Later on, you can choose to either ride in a drift car being chased by a replica undercover police car, or get on the passenger seat of a police car and chase a drift car.




Drifting is a driving technique that originated in Japan, and the country’s drift culture has captivated fans all over the world. We offer not only advanced drifting experiences, but also the thrilling excitement — just like in the movie “Tokyo Drift”.




The drift cars that will be used are Silvia S14, S15, Nissan 180SX, 350Z, Miata MX5, Toyota JZX100, Mazda RX-8, and more. Cars will vary on the day, but we will have at least 2 cars from the above on site. Also the replica undercover police vehicle is a Nissan Skyline G35 in a detail police spec mode. All modifications on the car are exactly like the real undercover police car, with sirens, microphone, police lamps. You will be amazed by the quality of the replica.




Our staff consists of top-level drift team drivers from Tokyo and Chiba, and our drivers are experienced OG street racers. We also operate our own maintenance garage, ensuring all vehicles are kept in perfect condition by automotive experts.




Our motto is to provide a safe yet exhilarating experience. We take all necessary safety measures to ensure a secure environment for our guests.




We also assist with filming, so let’s create the most unforgettable highlight of your trip to Japan—together!

title: Go On a Walking Tour to Discover Historical Tokyo!
overview: In this tour, you will visit historical and traditional sites of Tokyo with a local guide who speaks English.
highlights: Asakusa Sensouji temple is unique place that both Buddhist temple and Shinto shrine coexist in the same precincts and good to learn the difference of them. You can try various kind of Japanese food and sweets at Nakamise street, as well as other area in Asakusa.

The Meiji shrine, more than 3 million people visit for new year’s greeting. You will feel a special sacred Japanese atmosphere at the shrine. If you are lucky, you may come across a Japanese traditional bridal procession at the precincts.

Hamarikyuu Garden built in Edo era. They say there used be more than 600~700 of Japanese gardens like this, but most of them were disappeared during the Meiji Era. Fortunately, this garden could remain with almost original landscape. So, many vestiges of old days can be seen throughout the garden.

Yanaka has a feeling of old downtown atmosphere with nostalgia. Wherever we walk, we come across temples. Why? A guide would like to answer it during the tour. Yanaka Cemetery is also interesting place to visit to know how Japanese people visit to worship their ancestors. Yanaka Ginza is small but charming shopping street for locals. It’s a fun to walk and eat delicious local foods.
important_info:
Meet at Asakusa, (in front of the Asakusa Culture Tourist Information Center Building) Meeting spot is changeable according to your hotel location. In this case, a meeting splot will be informed.
Hotel pick-up service is available (option).

9:00 ~ 17:00 8 hours
( The tour start time is changeable according to your request.)
cancellation:

description:
-This is a private tour only for you and your group.
-We use public transport, such as JR line, subway line during the tour.
-Meet at Asakusa, the first spot of the tour.
-Hotel pick-up service is available (option)




The tour itinerary is as follows.



Asakusa area:
The most popular and traditional spot in Tokyo.
-Kaminarimon-gate (Gate of thunder)
-Nakamise shopping street, selling many interesting items for souvenirs.
-Sensouji-temple.


Asakusa shrine
Hoppy-street loved by locals.





Hamarikyuu trraditional Japanese style Garden:
Typical garden of the feudal lord's residence during the Edo Era.
Japanese maccha-tea service is available at the tea-house on the pond.






Yanaka area:
Yanaka area is a downtown, a quite different from Tokyo city center.





Yanaka public cemetery
Yanaka Ginza


Meiji shrine:
Built in 1920 for the Emperor Meiji and his consort.
The biggest and important shrine in Tokyo.



Finish the tour at the last spot or the nearest station.
The necessary information how to return to your hotel will be given by the guide.




If you'd like to visit other spots, please feel free to contact us.

then move to the next destinations by public transportation.

description:
A seasonal feature of winter in Hokkaido! Enjoy ice fishing on a frozen lake (ice smelt fishing). Located 10 minutes from Memanbetsu Airport, the gateway to the skies of Okhotsk, Lake Memanbetsu is a place where you can enjoy smelt fishing every year by drilling a hole in the frozen lake surface. Our professional outdoor guides will take you to the fishing spot while taking you to and from the hotel or airport. This is the most popular winter activity, as you can eat the smelt you catch as tempura on the spot. We will provide you with the equipment, so you can participate empty-handed! ♪ A guide will accompany you, so even first-time fishers can feel at ease. 1. Smelt fishing experience: Experience the thrilling and fun ice smelt fishing that is unique to Hokkaido in winter. You can drill a hole in the ice and feel a sense of accomplishment in catching the fish yourself. Of course, since you are dealing with nature, there may be few hits. 2. Support for beginners: The guide will provide friendly support so that even beginners can participate with confidence. They will carefully teach you how to use the equipment and how to attach bait. 3. Drilling holes using an ice drill: This experience includes drilling holes in the ice yourself using an ice drill, so you can actively participate even in the cold. 4. Lake Abashiri is one of the most famous places in Japan as a sacred place for smelt fishing, and anglers come from all over the country. We will guide you to Lake Abashiri, one of Hokkaido's leading smelt fishing spots. You can enjoy fishing on the lake, catch as many as you want to eat, and enjoy the fresh smelt as freshly fried tempura. 5. Peace of mind in a warm tent: A tent is set up to protect you from the cold, and the freshly caught smelt is fried on the spot and made into tempura. We offer a special time in a warm tent with fresh flavors.

title: LEGOLAND® Japan Resort in Nagoya Advance Tickets [E-Ticket]
overview:
Spark your imagination by booking LEGOLAND® Japan tickets in Nagoya, Aichi, where inspiring education meets exciting play. There are over 40 fun rides and shows to experience in seven themed zones, plus engaging, interactive attractions where creativity runs wild.




Your e-ticket gives you full-day access to all the rides and shows — book online now and skip the long ticket lines on the day of your visit!




Lego lovers in Japan are not limited to Nagoya — there are Discovery Centers in Tokyo and Osaka too!




Or double the fun with a SEA LIFE Nagoya combo ticket — book here!
highlights: Duel dragons and search for buried treasure in seven exciting themed lands
Enjoy over 40 rides, shows, and interactive attractions
Explore the life-like mini cities of Miniland
important_info:
• Must be booked at least 1 day in advance
• This ticket is valid for non-Japanese passport holders only
• Japanese passport holders who purchase the tickets will not be able to enter the park and will not be issued any refunds
• Free entry for infants aged 0–2
• Children aged 0–12 must be accompanied by a parent or guardian at least 16 years of age
• Height and age restrictions apply to some rides
• Wheelchairs are available for rent at 500 JPY per day
• Outside food and drink are not permitted inside the park
• Excessive exposure of tattoos is prohibited
description:
Experience a colorful world of wacky fun and excitement, guaranteed to inspire and engage kids of all ages. LEGOLAND® Japan in Nagoya is one of the most immersive interactive experiences around, for abundant creative learning and magical rides. A visual treat for the whole family, featuring 40 rides, shows, and attractions, constructed from an amazing 17 million LEGO bricks. There are no less than 10,000 mini models which have been meticulously recreated!




Kids will adore exploring the different themed zones – the Factory, Bricktopia, Adventure, Knights Kingdom, LEGO City, Pirate Shores, and Miniland.




Factory
Begin a day of fun and discovery at the Factory, which is the beating heart of the park, producing all the bricks that go into creating the amazing LEGO models. Watch and learn on the LEGO Factory Tour and see exactly how the LEGO bricks are produced, and take home one fresh off the production line as a unique memento. Before heading off to experience all the other LEGO attractions, check out the colorful wares at the aptly named BIG Shop – one of the largest LEGO shops in Asia.




Bricktopia
Now the real excitement begins! Inspiration meets exhilaration at Bricktopia. Build your own LEGO vehicles and monsters, or learn about more advanced brick-building techniques at the LEGO Creative Workshop. There is an array of fun-filled rides too, including the spinning Imagination Celebration and the crazy Cat Cloud Busters aerial ride. Not to be missed is LEGO Ninjago Live, an action-packed show of puppetry, interactive video, and 4D special effects.




Adventure
Adventure is a land where young explorers will have a blast venturing through ancient Egyptian ruins and underneath the deep blue sea. Join the LEGOLAND® divers on an enchanting Submarine Adventure or seek out ancient treasure at Lost Kingdom Adventure. The wet n’ wild S.Q.U.I.D. Surfer jet-ski pod ride is the perfect fun attraction to keep cool in the heat.




Knights Kingdom
Next up is a medieval magical land of swords and sorcery. Kids can do battle with dragons on a high-speed, twisting rollercoaster, while younger knights can ride the more sedate Dragon's Apprentice mini coaster. Merlin's Flying Machines, a pedal-powered spinning contraption, is great fun too and not to be missed!




Pirates Shores
Perfect for young buccaneers with a sense of adventure, Pirates Shores brings mayhem on the high seas with exciting kid-friendly rides Anchors Away and Splash Battle. It also boasts the hugely entertaining adventure play area Castaway Camp - perfect for younger pirates.




Miniland
LEGO’s famous world in miniature attraction is one of the most adored attractions at LEGO parks around the world. At LEGOLAND® Japan, you get to tour the skylines of Tokyo, Osaka, Kyoto, and Nagoya in a single day. Each of these mini-cities is intricately designed in amazing detail. The 10 different locations featured at Miniland have been created painstakingly using a mind-boggling 10 million LEGO bricks.




LEGO City
Kids can get to work in LEGO City, a fully functioning mini-metropolis. Become a police officer, a firefighter, or pilot a plane. With the principles of teamwork and creativity, Miniland is the perfect place to meet new friends and acquire new skills in a fun and inspiring atmosphere. Feeling a little fatigued? Drop by the Palace Cinema. This plush movie theatre is the ideal place to kick back, relax and watch exciting 4D films, which are shown throughout the day.




In each of the zones, there are options to slow down to grab a bite to eat, from a quick burger to full-service themed dining. You will also find numerous opportunities to stock up on LEGO toys and souvenirs as you make your way around the park. Free Wi-Fi is also available throughout LEGOLAND® Japan.




The park will spark kids’ imagination like never before, and although aimed at 2-to-12-year-olds, the whole family will adore the experience.

overview:
This is a round-the-bay course (about 50 minutes) departing from and arriving at Matsushima Island. In addition to the typical Nioh-jima and Futago-jima, the boat sails through shallow waters, bringing you the gorgeous scenery of islands and voyaging through waters that only Marubun Matsushima steamship can. Please enjoy the view as you listen to the onboard information broadcast.




And after an amazing cruise, you can head to nearby Sendai to end the day with a back-alley bar-hopping tour!

description:
"Private tour for group charter" A boat charter experience tropical fishing for one group only. Recommended for those who want to make their stay in Okinawa a special time or for those who want to spend a private space just for themselves. Unlike general shared boat tours, you don't have to worry about other people. We will guide you through the ultimate Okinawa vacation that can only be experienced on a private course. "The boat is a new boat that was launched in 2024, the best size for one group charter" The boat used for the course is a new boat delivered in 2024, "YAMAHA YFR-27HMEX". It comes with a toilet, shower, air conditioning, cabin, and sunshade awning, so women and children can feel safe. The capacity is 10 people, and up to 8 customers can board excluding the two captain staff. It is just the right size for one group charter. The engine is YAMAHA's latest model "V-type 6-cylinder model F250N". The fuel is gasoline instead of diesel, so the clean exhaust gas does not cause any smell or noise. The port is a 5-minute walk from Chatan Town American Village. The port is in the very popular Chatan Town, where there are many resort hotels, shopping centers and restaurants. Chatan Fisherina is the closest to the tourist spot American Village, so it has excellent access and atmosphere. Choose from early morning, morning, afternoon or evening, and there are 4 120-minute courses per day. Recommended for those who want to make the most of their time, such as in the evening after arriving in Okinawa or early in the morning on the last day, and have a lot of plans during their stay. Even with the 120-minute course, you can spend 60 minutes leisurely after arriving at the point.

title: Tokyo Haneda Airport Shuttle Transfer for Maihama or Central Tokyo
overview: Enjoy an efficient, hassle-free Tokyo Haneda Airport shuttle transfer service between Haneda Airport and central Tokyo (or Maihama). Get HND airport pick-up and drop-off service for 15 out of Tokyo's 23 wards, plus the Maihama area in Chiba.
highlights: A variety of hotels and ryokan covered by our shuttle service
For transfer from Haneda Airport to your hotel, your driver will wait for you for up to 90 minutes
Convenient, reliable shared shuttle service between central Tokyo (or Maihama) and Haneda Airport
No extra charges for nighttime or morning services
From your hotel to Haneda Airport, your driver will drop you off with enough time for pre-departure clearance
important_info:
• This is only available for flights scheduled to land 5:00–20:30
• For other times, please book this private transfer service
• Please note that this is a shared transfer service, not a private transfer. You cannot choose your pick-up time, vehicle type, or other details. Additionally, you may have to wait for other guests who have also booked this service
• Our system sets the start time at 00:00 by default, but your actual pick-up time will be based on your flight arrival/departure time. For departures from Haneda Airport, your driver will wait for you at the arrival hall after customs clearance for 30–90 minutes after your flight’s actual arrival time. For departures from your accommodation, your pick-up time is set at about 3–4 hours prior to your flight departure time. Your exact pick-up time will be emailed to you 3 days before the service date
• We only offer transfer to and from hotels (including ryokan) in 15 of Tokyo's 23 wards (Arakawa, Bunkyo, Chiyoda, Chuo, Edogawa, Koto, Meguro, Minato, Ota, Shibuya, Shinagawa, Shinjuku, Sumida, Taito, and Toshima) and the Maihama area. We do not offer transfer to or from residences, apartments, guesthouses, capsule hotels, hostels, and vacation homes (‘minpaku’). Please note that your hotel must be within our service areas — for hotels in central Tokyo, please check our list for confirmation: https://rakuten-travelexp.net/airport-transfer-hotel-list
• Children are charged the same price as adults. Infants aged 0–1 may ride for free provided that they sit on a parent or guardian's lap; child seats are not available
• If the information that you provided at the time of booking is incomplete, we will contact you to ask further questions. Please check your dashboard
• We cannot accommodate guests with pets; however, if you have a service animal, please inquire with us in advance
• Please note that we cannot pick you up and drop you off at hotels other than the one you have booked (e.g. train station, other hotels, etc.).
• Please note that you cannot choose the type of car or the language of the driver.



About Luggage


Each passenger may bring up to 2 pieces of luggage plus hand-carried items. We can also accommodate collapsible strollers and wheelchairs for free, but please contact us in advance to ensure space.
Additional luggage, cardboard boxes (total dimensions within 110 cm), and golf sets require extra fees; please book the add-ons.
Ski boards, snowboards, and surfboards cannot be accommodated.
We may not be able to accommodate some other special luggage (e.g., bulky or expensive items that need to be insured, like instruments and paintings); we will let you know if this is the case.
For luggage we cannot accommodate, we recommend arranging for delivery from the airport to your accommodation (or vice-versa) yourself.
Porter service is not available; please carry your luggage yourself. Guests need to take care of their own luggage.
description:
Save time and money with this fast and hassle-free shared shuttle transfer service, which takes you from Haneda Airport to central Tokyo (or the Maihama area), and vice-versa!




After a long flight to Haneda Airport, the last thing you'll want to do is figure out what train route to take and carry all your luggage with you as you commute. And, on the day of your return flight, you surely won't want to spend your time stressing out on transport and luggage.




Save yourself the trouble with our handy service — we cover 15 out of central Tokyo's 23 wards, as well as the Maihama area in Chiba, near Tokyo Disney Resort!




If you're transferring from Haneda Airport to central Tokyo or Maihama, after customs clearance, your driver (or a staff member) will wait for you with a sign saying 'Green Tomato.' Within two hours of your flight's landing, you'll be transported to your hotel in a spacious, comfortable shuttle — just sit back and relax.




If you're transferring from your hotel in central Tokyo or Maihama to Haneda Airport, your driver will pick you up at the bell desk. You'll be dropped off at Haneda Airport with more than enough time for all the necessary pre-departure measures.




Book now for hassle-free transport between your hotel in central Tokyo (or Maihama) and Haneda Airport! (Please read the 'How It Works' and 'Important Information' sections for more details.)
how_it_works:
For transfer from Haneda Airport to your hotel:
1: Once your booking is confirmed, it is non-refundable, and the details are non-changeable. For any changes (e.g. flight number), please cancel your current booking and make a new one. We will only issue refunds if your flight details are delayed/changed and/or if we have to cancel your booking due to the weather
※Please note that connecting flights are non-refundable, even in the case of natural disasters.

2: Your driver, who will be holding a signboard saying ‘Green Tomato,’ will wait for you at the arrival hall after customs clearance for 30–90 minutes after your flight’s actual arrival time. If you do not show up within 90 minutes, you will be considered a no-show; no refunds will be issued
3: After meeting your driver, you may go to the restroom, change money, or buy a SIM card at a vending machine. However, due to lack of time, exchanging JR passes or renting mobile phones are not allowed
4: You will leave the airport within 2 hours of your flight’s arrival. Since this is a shared service, you may have to wait for other passengers




Note:
If your flight is delayed but arriving on the same day as your requested date, we will provide an alternate shuttle for free. However, if it is arriving the next day or later, regardless of the reason, your booking will be cancelled without refund, and you will have to make a new booking




For transfer from your hotel to Haneda Airport:
1: Once your booking is confirmed, it is non-refundable, and the details are non-changeable. For changes to your pick-up point and pick-up time for whatever reason, please cancel your current booking and make a new one. We will only issue refunds if your flight details are delayed/changed due to the weather and/or if we have to cancel your booking for the same reason.
2: We will inform you of your pick-up time 3 days before your requested date, so please check your email or guest dashboard. Generally, you will be picked up 3–4 hours before your flight, and you will not be able to choose the time
3: On your requested date, please be at your hotel’s bell desk 15 minutes before your pick-up time. If you do not show up by your pick-up time, you will be treated as a no-show; no refunds will be issued

description:
Step back in time to a world of old-school gaming in Akihabara
Embark on a nostalgic journey through an iconic retro video game store in Akihabara: a haven for enthusiasts of classic gaming. Led by your knowledgeable guide, immerse in the distinctive 8-bit melodies and unearth hidden gems of gaming history. From vintage consoles to cherished game disks, rediscover the magic of bygone eras in gaming that evoke cherished childhood memories.




Get spellbound by Japanese maid culture at a special maid café
No visit to Tokyo's Akihabara district is complete without the unique experience of a maid cafe. Join us as we venture to a beloved local haunt where adorable maids await to enchant you with their spells and charm. Treat yourself to whimsically adorned omelets or lattes personalized with delightful ketchup or chocolate illustrations, and capture the moment with memorable photos alongside your newfound friends.




Enjoy an immersive journey into Akihabara's vibrant anime scene from a local's perspective
Unleash your inner “otaku” geek, as you explore Akihabara's expansive anime stores, brimming with an extensive array of figurines and cosplay delights. Accompanied by your expert guide, traverse these colorful havens that might otherwise be daunting for solo travelers, and delve into the heart of Akihabara's vibrant subculture with confidence and excitement.

title: Nature Experience Rokko-Shidare Observatory
overview:
Rokko-Shidare is a mountain-top observatory that combines views, art and nature. Standing on the top of Mt Rokko, it looks like a large tree and shows different faces from the outside and the inside. It is one of the sightseeing spots you should visit at least once if you come to Mt Rokko.




The panoramic view from an altitude of approximately 880 m extends from Awaji Island to the Osaka Plain and Kansai International Airport, and at night it transforms into a "$10 million night view".

title: Electric Bicycle Tour Around Gujo Hachiman in Gifu
overview: Discover the hidden routes of Gifu’s “Water City,” Gujo Hachiman. With a guide, cycle around town and admire its pristine rivers and canals. You’ll also visit a Japanese garden in a temple, and climb up Gujo Hachiman Castle for a wonderful view.
highlights: Explore the nostalgic Gujo Hachiman, dubbed “Water City” for its many waterways
Discover hidden, less-traveled roads and back alleys with a guide
Admire well-preserved old houses and streets, as well as the tranquil Nagara River and Yoshida River
See a wonderful Japanese garden situated in a Zen temple
Climb up Gujo Hachiman Castle and see the town from above
important_info:

Participants must be aged 15 or above, at least 150 cm tall, and fit for cycling
Intoxicated guests may not participate
If we have to cancel due to inclement weather, we will inform you by 17:00 the day before to refund or reschedule

schedule:
This tour is available from March 1–November 30, daily, and has two available start times: 09:30 and 13:30
This tour lasts about 3 hours, and roughly follows this itinerary:



09:30 — Meet at Gujo Hachiman Station
09:45 — Sightseeing around town; admire the Nagara River

description:
At Gifu Prefecture — known as the “heartland of Japan” for its central location — lies the quaint town of Gujo Hachiman. Dubbed the “Water City,” Gujo Hachiman is defined not only by its old-school townscape, but also by its many life-preserving waterways.




On this cycling tour, you’ll explore Gujo Hachiman with a guide who can lead you through roads and back alleys less traveled by tourists, and often missed by guidebooks and sightseeing maps. These local routes will give you a peek into a day in the life of the townspeople.




Meet your guide at Gujo Hachiman Station, then hop on your electric bicycle and set off! Your e-bike allows for a much smoother cycling experience, and is easier to use for novice cyclists. Since you won’t easily feel worn down from pedaling, you can explore at a more leisurely pace.




As you cycle around Gujo Hachiman, admire the nostalgia-tinged townscape. Well-preserved old houses stand on Gujo Hachiman’s streets to this day, and the town boasts pristine, flowing waters — not to mention friendly locals. You’ll warm up to this charming town right away and feel an enchanting sense of nostalgia, as if you’ve traveled back to simpler times.




Admire the town’s prized waters: the beautiful Nagara River — a beloved symbol of Gifu — and the pristine Yoshida River that flows through the town. Along the way, stop for photos with Gujo Hachiman Castle and Yoshida River in the background. Exploring town, you’ll see more of its canals, which are so vital to the lives of the locals. Watch fish and children swimming freely, and touch the water. It’s so clean that you can even drink it!




Besides getting a glimpse of the countryside lifestyle, you’ll also get a look into Japanese culture by visiting a temple and castle. Make your way to Jionzenji Temple, where you can view the breathtaking Tetsuso-en Garden. An oasis tucked away in the grounds of a Zen temple, this Japanese garden is centered around a calm pond that’s surrounded by verdant maple trees. As you gaze at the scenery, you’ll hear the ambient sounds of the garden’s waterfall.




Afterward, you’ll cycle to Gujo Hachiman Castle. A beautiful fortress atop a mountain, this castle stands out among the surrounding maple trees, which turn a stunning auburn in autumn. Climb up the castle, where you can enjoy a superb view of the town below, before heading back toward the direction of Gujo Hachiman Station. Pass through Shokunin Machi (Craftsmen’s Street), which was once home to the town’s craftspeople; you’ll still see a few workshops that remain open for business.




Make a stop at a renovated teahouse for a 30-minute break, where you’ll enjoy mitarashi dango (skewered rice dumplings coated in a sweet-savory glaze) and green tea, before ending your tour back at Gujo Hachiman Station.




As a small town, Gujo Hachiman offers the same Japanese scenery as major cities like Kyoto, but without the excesses of urban life. Relatively peaceful and less crowded, this town is the perfect location for a scenic and relaxing cycling tour, so book now!

title: 7-Day JR Sanyo-San’in Area Pass [E-Ticket]
overview: The JR Sanyo-San'in Area Pass is a convenient rail pass for exploring the scenic Sanyo and San'in regions of Japan. It offers unlimited travel on JR trains, including the famous bullet trains, allowing you to visit popular destinations like Hiroshima, Kyoto, and Osaka to Fukuoka. With a validity of 7 days, it's an excellent choice for travelers looking to experience the rich history, culture, and natural beauty of western Japan.
highlights: Convenient e-ticket for easy pickup — buy before or after arrival in Japan
7 days of unlimited travel around the Sanyo-San'in area of Japan
Great flexibility: voucher is valid up to 90 days after booking
Roam Japan freely from Kansai to Fukuoka
Includes the Miyajima Ferry and Sanyo Shinkansen
important_info:
Only valid for foreign nationals visiting Japan with the status of Temporary Visitor, and the corresponding stamp in their passport
Please be aware that luggage space is sometimes limited on busy trains
For full conditions of use, visit https://rakuten-travelexp.net/sanyo-sanin-rules
How to Receive Your JR Pass
1: Choose the date you intend to redeem the JR Pass and book; a lead guest may book on behalf of a group, but note that all guests must hold non-Japanese passports and have Temporary Visitor status.
2: Receive your QR code e-voucher with your confirmation email. If your requested date is more than 90 days in the future, we will wait to issue the e-voucher closer to your requested date.
3: Present your QR code e-voucher, printed or displayed on your smartphone, at any valid exchange office to redeem your physical pass. All guests must also present their passport for verification during redemption. If you have a passport with an IC chip you can also exchange your e-voucher at a ticket vending machine marked with the passport symbol. You can redeem it up to 90 days from the issue date of your e-voucher. Main exchange offices are at Kansai International Airport and Kyoto, Osaka, Shin-Osaka, Hakata, and Hiroshima stations. For a full list, visit https://rakuten-travelexp.net/jr-west-pickup-list
How to Use Your JR Pass
1: Your JR pass can be used for 7 consecutive days and must be redeemed within 90 days from when the e-voucher is issued
2: The usage period must be within one month of exchanging your e-voucher for the physical pass
3: You need to specify the date that you want to start using the pass at the exchange office
• When you first use the Pass, enter a ticket gate where there is an attendant available and receive a stamp in the designated column
• Use your pass on:
— The Sanyo Shinkansen between Shin-Osaka and Hakata
— JR limited express, special rapid, rapid, and local train services in the coverage area (reserved or non-reserved ordinary seats)
— The Chizu Express, JR-West Miyajima Ferry, West JR Bus, and Chugoku JR Bus within the coverage area
• Not valid on:
— Tokaido Shinkansen
— Kyushu Shinkansen
— The special rapid "A Seat" paid seating service
— Conventional lines within Kyushu
— Expressway buses
— Carriages or trains requiring numbered or 'liner' tickets (additional fees apply)
• For a map of the pass coverage area, visit https://rakuten-travelexp.net/sanyo-sanin-map
what_to_bring:
• Your electronic voucher with QR code
• A non-Japanese passport with a temporary visitor stamp
meeting_point: Exchange at a valid ticket machine (click here to search)
cancellation:
Non-refundable after you booked
Terms and Conditions
Tour terms and conditions
Standard terms and conditions of travel contracts
description:
Grab an e-voucher for the JR Sanyo-San'in Area Pass, and you can exchange it easily at any participating exchange office. Although the pass is only valid for overseas visitors, with a QR code voucher you can receive it even after landing in Japan!




Book now and look forward to seven days unlimited travel from Fukuoka to Hiroshima, Himeji to Kobe, Osaka, Kyoto, Nara and more — all for a great fixed price.




This pass from JR West also grants holders discounts at various attractions across the region, such as Himeji Castle, Kyoto Sightseeing Bus, Arashiyama Monkey Park, Osaka Castle Museum, Naniwa Exploration Cruise, Kuchu-teien Observatory, Harukas 300 Observatory, Sakamoto Cable Railway, Nara Kotsu Bus Lines, Kobe Port Tower, and others.

description:

Located just a 20-minute drive from the popular new theme park, JUNGLIA, and 10 minutes from Churaumi Aquarium, COSMIC OCEAN offers a luxurious tour where you can enjoy "sea activities" in a private space exclusive to one group. ⚪︎Reasons to choose a COSMIC OCEAN tour: 1. [Freedom of Style] Swimmers and non-swimmers alike will be delighted! Since it's a private tour for one group, you can freely choose the way to enjoy the experience based on your skill level. For those who are not confident in their swimming skills: "Safe Snorkeling" where you float with a life jacket. For those who are strong swimmers: "Skin Diving" where you remove your life jacket and dive at the same eye level as the fish. *For those who want to learn more, skin diving lessons (fee required) are also available! 2. [Beach Entry] Beginners and those worried about seasickness can rest assured. Our professionals carefully select the most serene and beautiful beaches of the day. This is a beach entry tour without a boat, so there's no need to worry about seasickness. You can gradually get used to the shallow waters, so even small children and first-timers can start with peace of mind. ③ [Comfortable Facilities] Just bring your swimsuit and towel! All equipment is free to rent. Swimsuit and towel are all you need to participate. After the tour, you can use the hot showers, changing rooms, hair dryers, and other amenities at the store for free. Freshen up and head straight to your next sightseeing excursion. ⚪︎ Convenient location for fitting into northern sightseeing itineraries. Approximately 20 minutes by car from JUNGLIA and 10 minutes by car from Churaumi Aquarium. This location is perfect for the golden route of Okinawa sightseeing: "park in the morning, sea in the afternoon." ⚪︎ Benefits & Options: Free photos and video taken by the guide! Free snorkeling lesson: Your guide will provide a thorough lesson. Full rental included: mask, fins, snorkel, and life jacket. [Limited Bonus]: Free rental of wetsuits and weight belts in winter or when the water temperature is low! [Paid Option]: Skin diving lesson (for those who want to learn ear equalization and jackknife). [Items to bring]: Swimsuit, towel, sandals, change of clothes. [Parking]: Available ⚪︎Schedule (approximately 120 minutes) Meeting point/reception at the store: Ample parking available. Preparation/lecture: Move to the beach and practice safety rules and how to use the equipment. Ocean fun: Feed tropical fish and try the popular free diving! Shower/change at the store: Get ready with the full range of amenities. Disbanding: Head to your next destination! Why not dive freely into the blue world of Okinawa? We look forward to seeing you!

description:

title: Kyoto Luxury Nightlife: Sake & Whisky Regional Food Pairing Tour
overview: Book an exclusive Kyoto nightlife tour to visit the hidden gems of the city’s “flower districts” — traditional entertainment areas with a rich history of geisha, sake, and Japanese cuisine. Indulge your senses with must-try cocktails, sake, whisky and other drinks that reflect Kyoto’s deep traditions. Visit secret spots where expert bartenders craft innovative, world-class drinks for discerning locals, and sample local snacks paired with a delicious cocktail and hear fascinating stories.
highlights: Visit an award-winning bar renowned for creative cocktails and seasonal dishes
Experience a classic whisky tasting at an esteemed establishment, with chocolate pairing or bar snacks
Reserve with a Rakuten account to earn and use points for each booking
Explore hidden paths and alleys, revealing Kyoto's hidden treasures
Engage in captivating stories that transcend history and culture
important_info:
This tour includes an English-language guided walking tour, sake, cocktail, whisky, and other beverage tastings, local dishes at 4 food stops, local snacks paired with famous Kyoto whiskey
This tour requires booking 4 days in advance, and has a minimum of 2 participants
Please arrive at the meeting point 15 mins before departure as the tour will depart on time
This is a walking tour, so please wear comfortable shoes
On rainy days, please bring an umbrella with you
Participants must be aged 20+ (the legal drinking age in Japan); please bring a valid ID for proof of age
what_to_bring:
Please present your passport and e-voucher to the guide at the meeting point
Please wear light, travel-friendly clothing and shoes to participate in this tour
meeting_point: In front of Kyoto Marui Department store World Clock, at the crossing of Shijo dori - Kawaramachi Subway Station: 68 Shincho, Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto-shi, Kyoto-fu 600-8001 (map)
schedule:
16:45 — Meet your guide in front of Kyoto Marui Department store World Clock near Kawaramachi Subway Station
17:00 — Depart for 3 hour guided tour with 4 food stops
20:00 — End the tour and disband
cancellation: Non-refundable after you booked
description:
Indulge in an unforgettable evening of Kyoto's renowned spirits and culinary delights on our exclusive sake, whisky, and cocktails luxury tour. Immerse yourself in the charm of Kyoto's historic "Flower Districts" around Gion and Pontocho, where traditional culture meets vibrant nightlife.




As dusk descends, embark on a journey through hidden alleys and backstreets, where secrets await. Our first stop is a clandestine cocktail bar, lauded for its exceptional bartenders and innovative drink creations. Savor local delicacies paired with your choice of cocktail, or let our skilled bartenders craft a bespoke concoction tailored to your mood.




Continuing our exploration, we'll meander to a century-old family home-turned-restaurant nestled along the riverside. Delight in the flavors of Kyoto as you sample exquisite sake and savor the chef's imaginative dishes, featuring the region's famed mochi in unexpected ways. Be captivated by the host's tales and relish in the playful twist on kamo (duck) tasting — a true culinary delight.




Cap off your evening with a whisky tasting experience at a sophisticated bar in the bustling Pontocho district. Choose from a selection of Japanese whiskies renowned worldwide, expertly paired with decadent sweets or savory bar snacks (other drinks are offered to non-whisky drinkers). As the night unfolds, take in the vibrant atmosphere of Kyoto's nightlife while strolling through the illuminated streets of Pontocho.

description:
Experience the nationwide sensation of resin art! ✔️ Wide variety of activities available! ✔️ Professional artists teach original techniques. ✔️ Professional photography and videography available. ✔️ Located just a one-minute walk from Euglena Mall, you can paint your own imaginative ocean scene with acrylic paint and then pour in a special resin called "resin liquid" to create sparkling, jewel-like ocean art. The white waves created with a heat gun look just like the real thing. Enjoy this experience with children, the whole family, couples, friends, or even solo artists, regardless of age or gender. This experience allows you to capture your favorite ocean views from Ishigaki Island in a tangible form. Let's spend some relaxing time together in the tropics! ♡ [Course Description] This course allows you to create two 10cm x 10cm coasters per person. You'll end up with a piece that can be used as a coaster or small item holder. Why not create matching original coasters for your family or partner? ^^*The smaller the resin art piece, the more difficult it is to create waves. Therefore, if you want to fully enjoy resin art, we recommend the "Ocean Art Board" course. [SEALLY. Unique Points] ◼️ Professional instruction ensures high-quality finished products! SEALLY. is run by a resin artist with solo exhibition experience. While you are the one creating the art, we will support even beginners in creating beautiful art using our original techniques. *Depending on the day, staff may be in charge. (Our staff are certified instructors.) ◼️ Abundant photo opportunities! Free gifts!♡ Since customers are restricted from using their phones during the experience, our staff will take photos and videos of the experience and give them away for free. Check out our Instagram for a glimpse of the experience! ◼️ Experience a stylish ocean-themed interior! The interior is decorated with artwork created by resin artists.♡ Since it's an indoor experience, there's no need to worry about hot, cold, or rainy days, or even if you have children! You can enjoy the experience in a relaxed atmosphere. [Recommended for: 】・People staying on Ishigaki Island for an extended period and wanting to experience something a little different・People visiting Ishigaki Island for the first time and not sure what to do・People who have already tried all the crafting experiences and want to try something new・People who want an experience they can participate in with their children・People who want to try it quickly in their spare time [Note] Since it takes a day for the resin liquid to harden, you will not be able to take it home immediately. description:
Blessed with an abundance of underground water veins that spring up from the Momoyama Hills, Fushimi was once known as "Fushimizu" (literally "fushimizu" in Japanese), and it is said that full-scale brewing began here about 400 years ago because the water was suitable for sake brewing. It flourished as a castle town and a port on the Yodo River on the Tokaido, and sake breweries with white walls stand out along the Uji River and canals. The streets of Fushimi, where you can enjoy the atmosphere of a sake brewing town, with ten-koku boats sailing among swaying willows, have been designated as the "Fushimi Minamihama Neighborhood Landscape Development District" by Kyoto City. ~Time Schedule~We will give you a handmade guidebook at the meeting point, so please take it home as a reference during the tour or as a souvenir of your participation. 13:00 [Meet at the north ticket gate of Keihan Electric Railway Nakashojima Station] Meet in front of the Sakamoto Ryoma panel in front of the station! This is where the tour begins! It is a flat road, but you will walk about 2 kilometers, so please wear comfortable shoes on the day. 13:15 [Gekkeikan Okura Sake Museum] ☆ Includes tasting of three types of sake, including Daiginjo, and a special Gekkeikan sake cup☆ This building is a wooden storehouse built in the Meiji era that is still in use. You can drink the underground water used in sake brewing from the well in the courtyard. Just like Fushimi sake, it is very soft and delicious, so please try it. You will be surprised at how soft it is. In the exhibition room, you can see traditional sake brewing tools, which are designated as tangible folk cultural properties of Kyoto City. 13:50 [Fushimi Yume Hyakushu] ☆ Includes summer-only sake ice cream ☆ Shop & cafe "Fushimi Yume Hyakushu". In fact, this building is the former main store of Gekkeikan, which was built in the Taisho era. In addition to Gekkeikan, various Kyoto sakes and souvenirs are sold in the store. We have prepared a 3-type sake tasting set including appetizers here. 14:30 [Kizakura Kappa Country] ☆Kyoto's oldest local beer included☆ Kiizakura is famous for its sake commercials using kappa characters, but it is also the first company to make local beer in Kyoto. Therefore, in addition to the sake manufacturing process, Fushimizugura is characterized by the fact that you can tour the beer factory. After the tour, try tasting sake and beer in the lovely courtyard. 15:15 [Aburacho] ☆Authentic sake tasting set of three types☆ We have more than 80 types of sake from all 18 breweries in Fushimi, mainly ginjo sake and daiginjo sake, and more than 100 types including seasonal limited edition sake. In addition, at the sake tasting counter attached to the store, you can compare three types in small cup size as well as a glass. Just looking at the bottles will make you feel happy.

description:
Meet at Chofu Station or Shindai Fureai no Sato and cycle to six spots along the Nogawa River on an electric bicycle (with front and rear child seats, and children's bicycles available in various sizes). This two-hour tour is fully enjoyable. In the first half, you'll fish for carp and catfish using bread. Even children can easily catch fish, but the average size is around 60cm, so you may not be able to reel them in. The pull is strong, so please try your best to reel them in. In the second half, if the fishing conditions aren't ideal, you'll experience scouring the river in waders (free rubber boots) or marine shoes. The main creatures you can catch are Japanese mitten crab, Japanese carp, Japanese minnow, Japanese rhubarb, Yoshinobori, loach, Ukigoi, black killifish, crayfish, and turtles. You can also take some home. What to bring: Marine shoes (from the end of March to October), swimsuit, spats, rash guard, change of clothes, hat, drink, sneakers, plastic case for taking things home + bubble wrap (can be sold on-site) What to bring: Rain boots (from November to mid-March), fishing equipment, bait, rain boots, net, storage bucket, explanation panel for the creatures We look forward to your participation. Nogawa Nature School Sonoda (Tosshi) PS: Generally, there is a minimum number of people of 5 or more to hold the event.

description:
The Kanmon Straits, also called the Kanmon Kaikyo or the Straits of Shimonoseki, is the waterway that separates Japan’s islands of Honshu and Kyushu. Since ancient times, this strait has been a major historical stage where the past and future intersect.




The Kanmon Straits Museum reopened in September 2019, after major renovations. The main building is an experiential museum, full of creativity and playfulness where you can encounter dramatic memories and lively, realistic figures. Here, you can enjoy spectacular image projections on one of the largest screens in the country, and experience a range of performances related to the nature and history of the Kanmon Straits. There is also a cafe which offers a sweeping view of the Straits.

description:
The Tokyo National Museum, situated in Ueno, Tokyo, houses around 120,000 artifacts gathered and passed down over 150 years since its establishment.
Out of these, 89 are classified as national treasures, and 649 are considered critical cultural properties (as of April 2023), making the collection a true reflection of Japan's quality and quantity of historical and cultural heritage.




The Okyokan, a valuable Japanese house, is typically closed to the public.
Constructed in 1742 as a study area for the Meigan-in Temple, situated on the outskirts of Nagoya City, the house is located in the residence of Mr. Takashi Masuda (Don'o), the first president of Mitsui & Co., Ltd. and a renowned tea master in Shinagawa, Tokyo.
In 1933, it was donated to the museum.




We have arranged several activities for you to experience Japanese culture while appreciating the fusuma paintings (which are currently reproductions) created by Maruyama Okyo, a painter from the Edo period, such as trying on a kimono, enjoying matcha tea, practicing ZEN breathing, etc.
Furthermore, we serve dishes with carefully selected Japanese ingredients and organic beverages.




We invite you to take advantage of this rare opportunity and visit us while it lasts.

Please select the desired date and time for admission to Tokyo Skytree when booking the tickets.
Kimono rental (Hanaka) reservations should be made at least 2 hours before admission at Tokyo Skytree, and returns must be made on the same day.
how_it_works:
Once your purchase is confirmed, you will receive an email with a 2D barcode as an E-ticket.


Your ticket is fixed-date; once your purchase is confirmed, you cannot change the date, cancel, or get a refund.


On the day of your visit, present the confirmation email with the 2D barcode ( Displayed on your smartphone) and your passport or identification card.
[Schedule]
Meet up at HANAKA (2 hours before the time specified at the time of booking)
Present 2D barcode, choose a kimono, and start dressing up
↓
Go to Tokyo Skytree (Please give yourself plenty of time so that you can enter the observatory at the designated time.)
↓
Collect the Tokyo Skytree admission ticket by presenting the 2D barcode at the ticket counter on the 4th floor of Tokyo Skytree.
↓
Visit the Tembo Deck
↓
Go back to HANAKA (Please return during HANAKA 's opening hours 09:30~18:00.)
↓
Return the kimono at HANAKA. (Approx 15 minutes)

important_info:

description:
Maiko is female performing artists working in Gion area whose skills include performing various Japanese arts such as classical music, dance and games. A maiko is a geiko minor apprentice from the age of 15 to 20 before her adulthood.




Our staff who used to be a maiko, will make you up and dress you up like a real maiko. Using her skill who was the maiko since years ago, she will teach you how to pose and dance like a maiko.




This first option, Maiko Makeover Experience (Studio) includes the studio photography, and the second option includes the photo session in our new studio in Oike with a traditional Japanese style house. Why don't you take the pictures and give it to your family or friends as souvenirs?

Open for business: Dec 2, 2023 to March 3, 2024




Business hours:
The snow park may open earlier or extend business hours on busy days. Please check the official website for details.




• Weekdays: 09:00–17:00
• Saturdays, Sundays, and national holidays: 09:00–20:00
If a weekday falls on a public holiday, business hours will be the same as Saturday/Sunday/public holiday
• Early opening days (08:00–20:00): 1/7, 1/8, 2/11, 2/12, 2/23, 2/24, 2/25
• Early closing days (9:00–17:00) 12/2, 12/31, 1/1
• Last admission at 16:30 (15:30 on weekends and holidays)
• Regular holidays: none, open every day during the season




Official website: https://www.rokkosan.com/ski/en/
Please refer to the business calendar for complete and accurate business hours. You can find the business calendar at
https://www.rokkosan.com/ski/information/?yr=2023&month=12&dy=&cid=my-calendar




Ticket validity: Dec 2, 2023 to March 3, 2024

1: After booking on Rakuten Travel Experiences, an e-ticket will be sent to your registered e-mail address.




2: On arrival at the facility, visit the entrance counter and click the "Voucher" link in the e-mail sent to you at the time of purchase. Then, click the "Use" button and show the screen to the staff (please ensure that your phone can connect to the internet before your visit).

description:
"Coral Reef Snorkeling Tour ~Take photos with Yoron Island's top-class coral!~ Recommended for families and groups! Yoron Island is surrounded by coral reefs! The boundary between the coral reef and the open ocean has active currents, attracting many creatures. You can enjoy an underwater world you don't usually see, with crystal-clear blue-green waters, colorful tropical fish, and the dynamic topography of the coral reef. The experience point is only about a 5-minute boat ride from the beach, so even those prone to seasickness can relax! Point 1 [Encounter with approximately 400 types of coral and colorful creatures] Clean seawater circulates at the boundary between the coral reef and the open ocean, allowing the coral to grow healthily, and you can see large fish and schools of small fish around it. This is an experience point where you can feel the rich ecosystem created by the coral reef up close! Point 2 [The boundary between the coral reef and the open ocean is the entrance to the wide ocean] Facing the open ocean, the outer edge of the coral reef, where the swells of the open sea crash, slopes steeply down to the deep seabed, and the blue of the sea changes from a pale emerald green to a deep blue. You can experience the large-scale topography of the coral reef and the mysterious and beautiful underwater world created by the creatures that live there! Point ③ [We operate with the motto of providing the safest and most enjoyable experience!] We believe that safety is the most important thing in sea activities. In addition to the skills of our guides, we also have one lifeguard on the beach/coast to ensure thorough safety management. We have created an environment where families and groups can play with peace of mind! Point ④ [Convenient facilities such as lockers, showers, and rest areas!] This is the only place on Yoron Island with such convenient facilities! In particular, this may be the only place where you can take a warm shower in a private room after playing in the sea! "Coral Reef Snorkel Tour" Overview Start Time : 9:00-16:00 (every 60 minutes) Price: 5,500 yen (tax included) 4,500 yen (tax included) for elementary school students and younger Includes: Lecture, snorkeling experience Target: Ages 4 and up *Ages 4-8 can participate with only a mask and accompanied by a guardian. Please inquire. Capacity: Up to 4 people per session Reservation: Reservations accepted by phone (0997-97-5033) until the day before Note: May be canceled due to weather or sea conditions.

description:
This plan is conducted in Japanese by a Japanese taiko instructor and includes an English translation guide. Wadaiko is a traditional Japanese culture that has been around for over 5,000 years, with close ties to samurai, kabuki, festivals, sumo, and more. It can be enjoyed by people of all ages, genders, nationalities, and even those with no prior experience playing an instrument. It combines elements of sport and brain training, and is used at shrines and temples as an instrument that is inhabited by gods, to ward off evil and pray for happiness, making it a perfect gift or celebration. (*Venue fees may be charged separately depending on the date, time, and number of people, so please check at the time of booking.)

description:
It is an experience diving tour by boat around Manzamo in Onna Village. The boat is limited to a small number of people, so it is recommended for those who want to enjoy it slowly and leisurely. It is located in the central part of Onna Village, so it is also recommended for those staying in Onna Village. ! The sea is highly transparent, the corals are beautiful, the topography is dynamic, and you can sometimes meet sea turtles. You might be lucky to find this plan! ? Since it will be a boat tour, you can enjoy a small cruise from the port to the point ♪ You can look up at the popular sightseeing spot Manzamo from the sea ♪ Also, since it is a 2-dive plan, the first dive is slowly in the sea. You can get used to it and enjoy it firmly on the second dive, so it is also recommended for those who are a little worried about diving for the first time. Of course, even those who are not good at the sea or swimming can make a plan according to their own pace so that they can enjoy the experience with peace of mind. Since it is a chartered tour limited to one group, it is possible to make a completely custom-made tour while listening to your requests at each pace ♪ I will do my best to guide you so that your trip to Okinawa will be the best trip! ! ~ Flow of the day ~ (about 4 hours from gathering to dissolution) ① Reception / application form ② Change of clothes / briefing (explanation of experience diving) ③ Boarding / departure ④ First diving experience (experience includes practice on the surface of the water) Approximately 40 minutes ⑤ Break on board Approximately 20 minutes ⑥ Second diving experience Approximately 40 minutes ⑦ Return to port, change clothes, hand over photos * Additional dives are available as an option only for morning flights. For details, please refer to "See the price list" for details. [Notes] Please be sure to check ① This plan is for 2 dives.・The boat is a shared boat for 2 dives, so you cannot return to the port after 1 dive. ・If you wish to do 1 dive, you will have to wait on board. (Please note that the price will be the same as 2 dives) ②We will check the availability of seats on the boat after making a reservation. ③ This plan is a trial diving for those who do not have a diving license (C card) ・If you have a diving license (C card), please select the "Fun Diving" plan [Onna Manzamo Fun Boat Diving] https://activityjapan.com/publish/plan/41876 Many other plans are available ♪ [List of BeNatural Diving & Yoga plans] https://activityjapan.com/publish/ plan_list/5209

description:
Enjoy fun diving in the coral reefs of the Sunabe area of ​​Miyagi Coast in Chatan Town, Okinawa Prefecture! ✨ You can enjoy many points of interest, including an underwater postbox and artificial structures sometimes referred to as UFOs! Since entry is from the beach, it's safe for beginners and those who don't like boats! The Sunabe area is covered in soft corals, which are as soft as sea anemones. It's rare to find a spot where you can see so many soft corals so easily from the beach! You can see many tropical fish up close, and they'll come right up to you during feeding time! ✨ You can also see lots of popular clownfish! ♡ In addition, you can encounter many other creatures besides fish, such as small and cute sea slugs and sea snakes that swim gracefully, though they can be a little scary! For those who have just obtained their license or divers who have been away from diving for a while, we will provide thorough explanations and practice sessions, including a review of knowledge and diving techniques, to ensure a safe and enjoyable dive, and we will create a plan tailored to your individual pace. We will do our best to guide you and make your Okinawa trip the best it can be! ~Schedule for the day~ (Approximately 3.5 hours from meeting to disbanding) ① Registration and application form completion ② Changing clothes and briefing (explanation of diving) ③ First dive (approximately 40 minutes) ④ Break ⑤ Second dive (approximately 40 minutes) ⑥ Changing clothes and photo handover *Additional dives are available as an option. For details, please see the price list. [Important Notes] Please be sure to check the following. ① This plan is for holders of a diving license (C-card). If you do not have a license, please choose the "Introductory Diving" plan. It is a diving tour by boat around Yomitan Village and Zanpamisaki. It's a boat with a limited number of people, so it's recommended for those who want to enjoy it slowly and leisurely. It's located in the middle of Yomitan Village, so it's also recommended for people staying in Yomitan Village. Speaking of diving spots near Yomitan Village, Cape Maeda The Blue Grotto is famous, but boat diving from the port of Yomitan Village is still a little-known little-known spot! It is not famous in general, but it is a famous area among divers. An area that even professional divers rate as “interesting!” There are few people, so it is attractive that you can enjoy it leisurely ✨ Since it will be a boat tour, you can also enjoy a small cruise from the port to the point ♪ Also, since it is a plan for 2 dives, the first dive is slowly getting used to the sea 2 It is also recommended for those who are a little worried about diving for the first time, as you can enjoy it with your own eyes. We will make a plan that matches your pace so that even those who are not good at the sea or swimming can enjoy the experience with peace of mind. I will do my best to guide you so that your trip to Okinawa will be the best trip! ! ~ Flow of the day ~ (about 4 hours from gathering to dissolution) ① Reception / application form ② Change of clothes / briefing (explanation of diving) ③ Boarding / departure ④ First dive about 40 minutes ⑤ Break on board about 20 minutes ⑥ 2nd dive about 40 minutes ⑦Return to port, change clothes, hand over photos *Additional dives are available as an option only for morning flights. For details, please refer to "See the price list" for details. description:
You can see the whale sharks, which are very popular at the Churaumi Aquarium, so close that you can almost reach out and touch them! You will definitely be excited and impressed by the scale of the world's largest fish! ? The world's first marine farm! You can swim with the whale sharks around the underwater cage where they swim! You can also observe the staff feeding them in front of you and the way they swim underwater. In addition to the whale sharks, you can see schools of swallowfish, gurukun, rainbow trout, and silver-eyed jacks. Even if you can't see the whale sharks, you can enjoy the other fishes, so it's no exaggeration to say that it's worth diving. The whale shark is very large, over 4m in size, but please rest assured that it has a gentle personality and will not attack people. Since it is a boat tour, you can also enjoy a short cruise from the port to the point. For those who have just obtained their license or those who have a gap in their diver training, we will provide detailed explanations and practice so that you can dive safely and enjoyably, including reviewing your knowledge and diving techniques, and we will create a plan that suits your pace. We will guide you to the best of our ability so that your trip to Okinawa will be the best trip ever! ! ~ Flow of the day ~ (From meeting to dismissal, it will take about 2.5 hours) ① Reception, filling out the application form ② Changing clothes, briefing (explaining diving) ③ Boarding and departure ④ Diving about 30 minutes ⑤ Return to port, changing clothes, handing over photos and videos [Notes] Please be sure to check. ① This plan is for those who have a diving license (C card). ・If you do not have a license, please select the "trial diving" plan. From the second dive, you can enjoy observing the charm and creatures of each different point because it is a different point from the first dive! ☆Safe support even for customers who are experiencing the underwater world for the first time From reception to seeing off, you will be handled by people with more than 5 years of experience, so you don't have to worry about being in the same group as people you don't know. ☆Guests will be guided by a dedicated diving boat, recommended for those who don't like crowds. We also offer a free Sanpincha and Chinsuko service on board between dives while basking in the Okinawan breeze and sun.☆ Free gifts of precious memories From the explanation of diving to the excitement of leaving the port at the diving point, underwater footage, as well as expressions of tension and excitement during the experience, and moving photos and videos, we will give you free gifts. We will take unlimited high-quality photos and videos of your precious memories for free. There is no additional charge on site. After the tour, the shooting data will be transferred to your smartphone immediately. ☆ Since it is an empty-handed gathering, you don't have to worry about luggage! All you need to bring is a swimsuit! Towels, sandals, and all the necessary equipment for diving are prepared. After the tour, you will take a hot shower, change clothes, and disband. description:
It is a plan for boat diving to points around Minnajima and Sesokojima off the coast of Motobu Town ♪ Because it is far from Naha, there are not many divers diving in Motobu, but in fact the transparency is comparable to or better than the Kerama Islands. It is a high water area. There are plenty of points. If there are places with roots in the sand where you can see schools of red-spotted sea bream, points with crepas and caves, points with outstanding coral beauty, and points where you can see blacktip reef sharks with a high probability. It is an area that you can enjoy no matter how many times you visit. The boat operates with plenty of time to spare, so even those who have just acquired a license and blank divers can relax and enjoy themselves. Since it will be a boat tour, you can also enjoy cruising from the port to the point ♪ For those who have just acquired a license and those who have a blank diver, you can dive safely and enjoyably, including reviewing knowledge and how to dive. We will carefully explain and practice so that you can do it, and we will make a plan according to each pace. I will do my best to guide you so that your trip to Okinawa will be the best trip! ! ~ Flow of the day ~ (about 4 hours from gathering to dissolution) ① Reception / application form ② Change of clothes / briefing (explanation of diving) ③ Boarding / departure ④ First dive about 40 minutes ⑤ Break on board about 20 minutes ⑥ 2nd dive about 40 minutes ⑦Return to port, change clothes, hand over photos *Additional dives are available as an option only for morning flights. description:
Field Earth's tours are waiting for you with an exciting tour prepared by experienced professional guides. Of course, safety is the first priority, and we will provide detailed explanations and support so that even first-timers can enjoy the tour with peace of mind. [The appeal of rafting] Rafting is a natural roller coaster that rides down rapids on a rubber boat called a "raft"! It is an activity where you plunge into rapids on a raft boat and go down the river while getting splashed with water! It is a team sport where everyone on board joins forces to face the rapids in unison, rowing forward and backward, in response to the guide's calls! *** Flow of half-day rafting tour *** Meet at Field Earth Minakami Base (please arrive 15 minutes before the reserved time) ↓ Check in at the front desk (all participants will fill out the reception form. The reception form will also serve as an insurance application form) ↓ Guide explains the tour contents, checks the participants' physical condition, explains the tour contents, explains the river conditions on the day, and explains the rental equipment to be used. (All rental fees for equipment used on the day are included in the tour price) ↓ Change into wetsuits (we recommend wearing a swimsuit underneath your wetsuit) ↓ Move to the starting point (your guide or staff will pick you up and drop you off) ↓ Safety briefing by your guide, who will teach you river signs to ensure safety on the river and explain how to use equipment (paddles, etc.) ↓ Start rafting Even in the same river, there are various currents and swells depending on the position of the riverbed and rocks. Our guides, who are very familiar with the river, will show you the best line, including points where you can slowly enjoy the scenery and exciting points where water splashes up. ↓ Arrive at the end point and transfer to Minakami Base by shuttle car ↓ Arrive at Minakami Base and change clothes (shower room and changing room available) ↓ Break time while viewing a slideshow of photos from the day ↓ Receive your tour photos and disband

Glass beads are a type of glass craft in which glass is melted with a burner and wrapped around a stick like syrup to create a shape. The lectures are easy to understand even for beginners, so anyone can make wonderful works. First, experience the charm of glass through the experience! We also have red and blue colored glasses in stock! ◇◆What is sandblasting◆◇ A method of blowing special sand onto glass, grinding the surface, and carving it into the glass. You can arrange the pattern on the glass to your work. You will have a one-of-a-kind original work. At Chiyono, we use special sand for the experience! Basic sticker pasting is easy to do and can be finished in a short time. Even beginners can easily make sandblasting. Just blow sand with an air gun and you're done!
